ARC01.blurb:
This non-ticketed session opens the archery program with qualifying rounds that set the bracket for individual events. Men shoot recurve while women compete in compound, the discipline making its Games debut in 2028.

Qualification determines seeding for elimination rounds ahead. Archers fire 72 arrows at 70 meters, with scores ranking competitors for knockout matchups that follow.

Though not held at Carson Stadium, these rounds launch storylines that culminate in medal matches later in the week.
ARC01.potentialContendersIntro: Paris 2024 Men's Recurve medalists Kim Woo-Jin, Brady Ellison, and Lee U-Seok could appear if they qualify for the 2028 Games.

potentialContenders.name: Kim Woo-Jin
potentialContenders.note: Paris 2024 Men's Individual Recurve gold medalist

potentialContenders.name: Brady Ellison
potentialContenders.note: Paris 2024 Men's Individual Recurve silver medalist

potentialContenders.name: Lee U-Seok
potentialContenders.note: Paris 2024 Men's Individual Recurve bronze medalist

ARC02.blurb:
The second qualification session flips the disciplines—men shoot compound while women fire recurve. Women's recurve features Paris 2024's top finishers aiming to secure favorable bracket positions.

Compound archers use mechanical sights and release aids, shooting for tighter groupings than their recurve counterparts. Seventy-two arrows determine who enters elimination rounds as top seeds.

This non-ticketed session sets up the week's individual competitions, with qualification scores dictating matchups through the knockout stages.
ARC02.potentialContendersIntro: Paris 2024 Women's Recurve medalists Im Si-Hyeon, Nam Su-Hyeon, and Lisa Barbelin are projected contenders if they qualify for 2028.

potentialContenders.note: Paris 2024 Women's Individual Recurve silver medalist

ARC03.blurb:
Compound archery makes its Games debut with mixed team competition, crowning the first-ever champions in a single night. Sixteen pairs advance from qualification to shoot head-to-head elimination rounds through to gold.

Mixed teams pair one man and one woman firing alternating arrows in a timed format. Compound bows with mechanical aids produce tighter scoring windows, making each arrow critical in close matches.

Carson Stadium hosts this historic evening session under lights. The modular soccer venue transforms into an archery arena with steep stands bringing fans close to the targets.
ARC03.potentialContendersIntro: No qualification status is announced for compound mixed team as of April 2026, making the field an open question for this debut event.

ARC04.blurb:
Knockout archery begins with first-round elimination for recurve individuals. Sixty-four men and women enter single-elimination brackets where one bad end can end a campaign.

Republic of Korea swept individual recurve golds in Paris 2024, with Kim Woo-Jin and Im Si-Hyeon leading dominant performances. If they qualify for 2028, they'll be marked targets from the opening round.

Carson Stadium's soccer bowl seats archers at field level with fans rising steeply overhead. Morning light illuminates targets 70 meters downrange as the bracket narrows toward medal matches ahead.
ARC04.potentialContendersIntro: Paris 2024 individual recurve champions Kim Woo-Jin and Im Si-Hyeon could appear if they qualify, alongside silver medalist Brady Ellison.

potentialContenders.note: Paris 2024 Men's Individual Recurve gold medalist

potentialContenders.note: Paris 2024 Women's Individual Recurve gold medalist

ARC05.blurb:
Evening eliminations bring recurve archers back to Carson Stadium as the bracket tightens. Survivors from morning matches face second-round opponents under lights, with the field cut from 64 to 32 in men's and women's events.

Head-to-head set matches reward consistency—archers shoot three arrows per end, five ends per match, with set points awarded to the higher scorer. Two sets wins the match, three sets if tied.

The open-air stadium takes on different character at night. Floodlights create dramatic shadows on targets as hometown crowds support USA archers against global competition.

ARC06.blurb:
Sunday morning brings more bracket carnage in recurve individual events. The round of 32 narrows to 16 as archers chase advancement toward medal rounds later in the week.

France's Lisa Barbelin took bronze in Paris 2024, establishing herself among elite women recurve archers. If she qualifies for 2028, expect Carson crowds to appreciate her challenge against Republic of Korea's depth.

Carson Stadium fills for weekend sessions with fans close enough to hear arrows thunk into targets. The soccer-specific design creates an intimate bowl that amplifies crowd reactions on clutch shots.

ARC07.blurb:
Sunday night continues the recurve individual grind with elimination rounds whittling the field. Archers return to Carson Stadium under lights for matches that determine who advances deeper into the bracket.

Republic of Korea's dominance in Paris 2024 included all five recurve golds. Their program's depth suggests multiple archers could survive to later rounds if they qualify for 2028.

The evening atmosphere at Carson brings different energy than morning sessions. Cooler temperatures and California night air create ideal shooting conditions as the competition intensifies.

ARC08.blurb:
Monday morning isolates the women's recurve bracket for dedicated elimination rounds. The field narrows from 32 to 16 as archers battle for advancement toward medal matches.

Republic of Korea claimed gold and silver in women's individual recurve at Paris 2024 with Im Si-Hyeon and Nam Su-Hyeon. If both qualify for 2028, they could face off again in LA.

Carson Stadium hosts midday archery with Southern California sun overhead. The venue's steep seating keeps fans engaged in tactical matchups where millimeter target differences decide outcomes.

ARC09.blurb:
Men's team recurve crowns champions in a single evening session running quarterfinals through gold. Republic of Korea won Paris 2024 over France, extending their dynasty in team events.

Team matches pit three-archer squads firing six arrows per end across four ends. Elimination format means one off day ends medal hopes, raising stakes on every release.

Carson Stadium under lights creates electric atmosphere for team finals. The soccer venue's proximity brings fans close enough to feel tension as archers alternate shots in rapid succession.

ARC10.blurb:
Men's recurve individual elimination resumes Tuesday morning with the round of 32 and 16. Survivors face mounting pressure as the bracket narrows toward quarterfinals and medal rounds.

Kim Woo-Jin captured Paris 2024 gold over USA's Brady Ellison in a final between Games veterans. If both qualify for 2028, their bracket paths could cross again in Carson.

Midday sessions at Carson Stadium bring steady California sun and growing crowds. The venue's modular design allows temporary seating expansion for archery, maximizing sightlines to distant targets.

ARC11.blurb:
Women's team recurve settles medals in one decisive evening. Republic of Korea won Paris 2024 gold over People's Republic of China, with Mexico claiming bronze in a strong performance.

An San, Jang Min-hee, and Jeon Hae-won formed Korea's Paris 2024 championship squad. If they remain active and qualify for 2028, they'll anchor another title push in Los Angeles.

Carson Stadium transforms for team finals with three-archer rotations creating rapid-fire tension. Steep stands bring fans close to the action as teams alternate ends in pursuit of gold.

ARC12.blurb:
Mixed team recurve begins elimination Wednesday morning with round-of-16 matches. One man and one woman per nation alternate arrows in a format that rewards both individual skill and tactical pairing.

Republic of Korea won Paris 2024 mixed team gold with Kim Woo-Jin and Im Si-Hyeon, a dominant pairing that could return if both qualify. USA's Casey Kaufhold and Brady Ellison took bronze.

Carson Stadium hosts midweek archery with the bracket tightening toward medal rounds. Mixed team's alternating-shot format creates unique tactical dynamics as partners coordinate strategy between ends.

ARC13.blurb:
Mixed team recurve crowns champions in a single night running quarterfinals through gold. Republic of Korea dominated Paris 2024, defeating Germany for gold with USA taking bronze.

Florian Unruh and Michelle Kroppen formed Germany's silver-medal pairing in Paris. If they qualify for 2028, they'll challenge Korea's depth in one of archery's most tactically complex events.

Carson Stadium under Wednesday night lights creates dramatic atmosphere for mixed team finals. Alternating shots between partners add tactical wrinkles as teams chase medals in rapid succession.

ARC14.blurb:
Men's individual recurve reaches the round of 16 Thursday morning. Eight matches determine quarterfinalists in the most prestigious individual event in archery.

Kim Woo-Jin leads world rankings following his Paris 2024 gold. If he qualifies for 2028, he'll face maximum pressure as the favorite in front of a packed Carson Stadium.

Morning sessions bring clear Southern California conditions ideal for precision shooting. The venue's soccer bowl design keeps fans engaged in tactical battles where wind reads and release timing decide tight matches.

ARC15.blurb:
Men's recurve individual settles medals in archery's marquee event. Quarterfinals through gold unfold in one decisive session, with Paris 2024 champion Kim Woo-Jin the archer to beat if he qualifies.

Brady Ellison earned Paris 2024 silver and remains active in 2026, potentially bringing Games experience to Carson Stadium. Lee U-Seok's bronze in Paris showed Korea's depth beyond their gold medalist.

Thursday night at Carson Stadium caps the archery program. The open-air venue under lights creates electric atmosphere as the final individual champion is crowned before Southern California fans.

ARC16.blurb:
The recurve women's field narrows from sixteen to eight in this morning elimination round at Carson Stadium. Archers compete in head-to-head matches, shooting five arrows per set across up to five sets, with the first to six set points advancing.

Republic of Korea has dominated women's recurve, sweeping gold and silver in Paris. If top-ranked competitors qualify, expect precision shot-making under pressure as medal contenders begin their campaigns.

Carson Stadium's steep stands bring spectators close to the targets. The open-air bowl fills with tension as each arrow lands, scores flash, and the field cuts in half before lunch.
ARC16.potentialContendersIntro: Paris 2024 saw Republic of Korea claim gold and silver, with France taking bronze. If the world's top-ranked archers qualify, this round could feature those medalists alongside other elite contenders.

potentialContenders.note: Paris 2024 gold medalist, holds top world ranking entering 2026

ARC17.blurb:
Four matches become two, then two become one as the recurve women's individual champion is crowned under the lights. Each elimination match plays as a best-of-five sets duel, quarterfinals through bronze and gold medal rounds all in one evening.

Republic of Korea swept the Paris podium with Im Si-Hyeon taking gold over compatriot Nam Su-Hyeon, while France's Lisa Barbelin claimed bronze. If those archers qualify for 2028, expect another showcase of precision and nerve.

The steep seating at Carson Stadium puts thousands of fans within shouting distance of the shooting line. Watch arrows fly seventy meters under floodlights, scores update in real time, and medals decided by millimeters.
ARC17.potentialContendersIntro: The Paris 2024 podium featured Im Si-Hyeon with gold, Nam Su-Hyeon with silver, and Lisa Barbelin with bronze. Those medalists could return if they qualify, making this a potential rematch of elite recurve shooting.

potentialContenders.name: Im Si-Hyeon
potentialContenders.note: Paris 2024 gold medalist, top world ranking entering 2026

ATH01.blurb:
The 2028 Games track and field program opens with morning qualifying across six events at the Coliseum. Sprinters take their first steps toward glory in the 100m prelims and round 1 for both men and women, while throwers and distance runners begin their campaigns in shot put, hammer throw, and the 5000m.

The session features an accessible entry point to witness world-class speed and power across multiple disciplines simultaneously. Field events unfold in the infield while track rounds rotate on the oval—pole vaulters chasing height, sprinters chasing tenths of a second.

Morning light fills the open-air bowl as the peristyle arch frames the eastern end. Early rounds mean approachable ticket prices and a chance to see breakout performances before the stands pack for finals.
ATH01.potentialContendersIntro: Paris 2024 gold medalists could return across multiple events, though rosters won't be confirmed until qualifying concludes in 2028.

potentialContenders.name: Camryn Rogers
potentialContenders.note: Paris 2024 women's hammer throw gold medalist

ATH02.blurb:
Saturday evening brings the meet's first finals sprint showdown as the women's 100m crowns its champion. The session also awards medals in women's shot put and men's 10,000m, mixing explosive power with endurance.

Julien Alfred of Saint Lucia won gold in Paris 2024, defeating a deep American field led by Sha'Carri Richardson and Melissa Jefferson. If those athletes return to form in 2028, expect a rivalry renewed under the lights. The shot put final and 10,000m provide counterpoint—throwers launching steel across the infield while distance runners chase laps in the heat.

Evening shadows stretch across the Coliseum bowl as the peristyle glows. Finals mean elevated stakes and crowd energy, with medal ceremonies punctuating the program.
ATH02.potentialContendersIntro: Paris 2024 medalists could contend if they qualify, with the women's 100m likely featuring the Saint Lucia-USA rivalry that defined the 2024 final.

potentialContenders.name: Yemisi Ogunleye
potentialContenders.note: Paris 2024 women's shot put gold medalist

potentialContenders.name: Thea LaFond
potentialContenders.note: Paris 2024 women's triple jump gold medalist

ATH03.blurb:
Day one of the men's decathlon begins with three disciplines: the 100m dash, long jump, and shot put. Decathletes balance speed, technique, and raw strength across ten events over two days, making this the ultimate test of all-around athleticism.

The session also includes qualifying rounds for discus, long jump, and the mixed 4x400m relay—a newer addition featuring two men and two women per team. Ryan Crouser of the USA, who won shot put gold in Paris 2024, could appear in the shot put qualifying round if he competes in 2028.

Sunday morning at the Coliseum offers a relaxed pace for qualifying events. Decathletes grind through their opening trio while field events unfold across multiple sectors of the infield.
ATH03.potentialContendersIntro: Paris 2024 medalists in individual events may compete in their qualifying rounds, while decathlon contenders begin their two-day campaign.

potentialContenders.name: Ryan Crouser
potentialContenders.note: Paris 2024 men's shot put gold medalist, three-time champion

potentialContenders.name: Miltiadis Tentoglou
potentialContenders.note: Paris 2024 men's long jump gold medalist

potentialContenders.name: Joe Kovacs
potentialContenders.note: Paris 2024 men's shot put silver medalist

ATH04.blurb:
Sunday afternoon delivers three gold medal races: men's shot put, men's 100m, and the mixed 4x400m relay final. The shot put crowns the world's most powerful thrower; the 100m determines the fastest human alive.

Noah Lyles won the Paris 2024 men's 100m by five-thousandths of a second, the closest finish in Games history. If he returns in 2028, expect another duel with Jamaica's Kishane Thompson and the USA's Fred Kerley. Ryan Crouser has dominated shot put for a decade—three consecutive golds through Paris 2024—and could be chasing a historic fourth title.

The Coliseum roars for sprint finals. Decathletes continue their campaign with high jump and 400m, adding a grinding undercurrent to the evening's fireworks.
ATH04.potentialContendersIntro: Paris 2024 medalists are projected to headline if they qualify, with the men's 100m and shot put featuring reigning champions.

potentialContenders.name: Noah Lyles
potentialContenders.note: Paris 2024 men's 100m gold medalist, won by 0.005 seconds

potentialContenders.name: Ryan Crouser
potentialContenders.note: Paris 2024 men's shot put gold medalist, seeking fourth consecutive title

potentialContenders.name: Camryn Rogers
potentialContenders.note: Paris 2024 women's hammer throw gold medalist

ATH05.blurb:
Decathletes return Monday morning to tackle three grueling disciplines: the 110m hurdles, discus throw, and pole vault. Day two separates contenders from pretenders as fatigue accumulates and technical precision becomes critical.

The session also awards the women's triple jump gold medal. Thea LaFond of Dominica won Paris 2024, becoming her nation's first-ever gold medalist in any sport—a historic breakthrough that could inspire a title defense in 2028 if she qualifies.

Monday mornings at the Coliseum mean thinner crowds and intimate views of decathletes grinding through their middle events. Triple jump finals bring explosive takeoffs and dramatic landings in the long jump pit.
ATH05.potentialContendersIntro: Paris 2024 triple jump medalists could return, with Dominica's Thea LaFond defending her nation's first-ever gold medal.

potentialContenders.name: Jasmine Moore
potentialContenders.note: Paris 2024 women's triple jump bronze medalist

ATH06.blurb:
The decathlon crowns its champion Monday evening after ten events over two days. Javelin and the 1500m finale test final reserves—throwers launching spears, then middle-distance running on exhausted legs.

The session also features the debut of the new mixed 4x100m sprint relay, with round 1 and final both contested. Two men and two women per team navigate baton exchanges at full speed. Men's pole vault awards its gold medal, while the women's 800m semifinals set the field for later rounds.

Evening light and medal ceremonies elevate the atmosphere. Decathletes collapse at the finish line; relay teams celebrate or agonize over exchange zones.
ATH06.potentialContendersIntro: The new mixed 4x100m relay features national teams yet to be confirmed, while decathlon and pole vault contenders emerge from Paris 2024 and world championships.

ATH07.blurb:
Tuesday morning opens qualifying for women's pole vault, men's triple jump, and women's 400m, while first-round heats continue in the men's 3000m steeplechase and 110m hurdles. Early rounds showcase depth—athletes from smaller nations alongside favorites.

Jordan Díaz of Spain won triple jump gold in Paris 2024 after switching federations from Cuba. If he qualifies in 2028, expect technical precision over the board. Marileidy Paulino of the Dominican Republic dominated the women's 400m in Paris and could return if in form.

Morning sessions at the Coliseum offer budget-friendly access. Pole vaulters warm up with practice runs; triple jumpers measure strides in the pit.
ATH07.potentialContendersIntro: Paris 2024 medalists in multiple events could appear in qualifying rounds, setting the stage for later finals.

potentialContenders.name: Nina Kennedy
potentialContenders.note: Paris 2024 women's pole vault gold medalist

potentialContenders.name: Jordan Díaz
potentialContenders.note: Paris 2024 men's triple jump gold medalist

ATH08.blurb:
Tuesday evening packs seven medal events into three hours: men's high jump, men's discus, women's 5000m, both 400m hurdles semifinals, men's 1500m semis, and the women's 800m final. Keely Hodgkinson of Great Britain won Paris 2024's 800m, breaking a 60-year British drought.

The session also opens men's 200m round 1. Letsile Tebogo of Botswana stunned Paris by winning the 200m, becoming the first African to claim gold in that event. If he and American silver medalist Kenny Bednarek return in 2028, expect another showdown.

The Coliseum's open bowl amplifies evening roars as medals hang around necks. Finals overlap—high jumpers clearing bars while runners chase laps.
ATH08.potentialContendersIntro: Paris 2024 gold medalists could contend across multiple finals, headlined by Hodgkinson in the 800m and Tebogo in the 200m rounds.

potentialContenders.name: Letsile Tebogo
potentialContenders.note: Paris 2024 men's 200m gold medalist, first African to win event

potentialContenders.name: Hamish Kerr
potentialContenders.note: Paris 2024 men's high jump gold medalist

ATH09.blurb:
Wednesday morning features the women's 3000m steeplechase final alongside qualifying rounds for men's hammer throw and women's long jump. The steeplechase demands endurance and hurdling precision over water barriers and fixed obstacles.

Tara Davis-Woodhall of the USA won long jump gold in Paris 2024 with a leap of 7.10 meters, then celebrated with an exuberant backflip. If she qualifies for 2028, expect similar energy in the qualifying round. Men's 400m and women's 400m repechage rounds give runners a second-chance path to semifinals.

Morning sun warms the Coliseum's concrete tiers. Steeplechasers splash through water jumps while long jumpers sprint down the runway in the infield pit.
ATH09.potentialContendersIntro: Paris 2024 medalists could appear in the 3000m steeplechase final and long jump qualifying, with Davis-Woodhall defending her title if she competes.

ATH10.blurb:
Wednesday evening delivers four gold medal races: women's discus, men's long jump, men's 1500m, and men's 400m hurdles. Miltiadis Tentoglou of Greece won Paris 2024 long jump with his final attempt, snatching gold from Jamaica's Wayne Pinnock in dramatic fashion.

The session also opens women's 200m round 1. Gabby Thomas of the USA won Paris 2024, leading an American podium sweep with Julien Alfred of Saint Lucia taking silver. If both return in 2028, expect renewed rivalry on American soil.

The Coliseum's evening atmosphere peaks during finals week. Medal ceremonies interrupt the flow as throwers, jumpers, and runners chase personal bests under the lights.
ATH10.potentialContendersIntro: Paris 2024 medalists could headline across multiple finals, with Tentoglou defending his long jump title and Thomas opening her 200m campaign.

ATH11.blurb:
Thursday morning features the men's 3000m steeplechase final alongside qualifying for women's javelin and high jump. The steeplechase awards gold after 7.5 laps of barriers and water jumps—a test of tactical pacing and hurdling technique.

Yaroslava Mahuchikh of Ukraine won Paris 2024 high jump, continuing her nation's tradition of excellence in the event despite the ongoing war. If she qualifies for 2028, her story carries weight beyond sport. Men's 800m round 1 seeds the field for semifinals.

Morning sessions offer intimate views of field events. High jumpers arch over the bar in the infield while steeplechasers lap the oval, splashing through the water pit on each circuit.
ATH11.potentialContendersIntro: Paris 2024 medalists could contend in the steeplechase final and high jump qualifying, headlined by Ukraine's Mahuchikh.

ATH12.blurb:
Thursday evening crowns champions in women's pole vault, men's hammer throw, and women's 400m hurdles. Nina Kennedy of Australia won Paris 2024 pole vault after sharing gold with Katie Moon in the 2023 World Championships—the two chose to split rather than continue jumping.

The session also features semifinals in men's 110m hurdles, men's 200m, and both 400m flat races. Letsile Tebogo could appear in the 200m semis if he qualifies, defending the title he won in Paris. Men's 1500m repechage offers a last-chance path to the final.

Evening energy builds as medal ceremonies interrupt the program. Pole vaulters chase heights under the lights while sprinters and hurdlers advance through rounds.
ATH12.potentialContendersIntro: Paris 2024 gold medalists could headline multiple finals, with Kennedy defending her pole vault title and Tebogo advancing in the 200m.

ATH13.blurb:
Friday morning launches the women's heptathlon with the 100m hurdles and high jump—two of seven events spread across two days. Heptathletes balance speed, power, and endurance across disciplines, making this the ultimate test of versatility.

The session also awards men's triple jump gold. Jordan Díaz of Spain won Paris 2024 after switching from Cuba, leaping 18.18 meters to claim gold. If he returns in 2028, expect technical mastery over the board. Men's 5000m round 1 seeds the distance field.

Friday morning brings a mix of multi-event grind and explosive finals. Heptathletes rotate through stations while triple jumpers sprint down the runway for their medal-deciding jumps.
ATH13.potentialContendersIntro: Paris 2024 triple jump medalists could contend in the final, while heptathlon competitors begin their two-day campaign.

potentialContenders.note: Paris 2024 men's triple jump silver medalist

ATH14.blurb:
Friday evening delivers three gold medal races: women's 10,000m, men's 110m hurdles, and men's 200m. The 10,000m tests endurance over 25 laps; the hurdles demand precision at full speed; the 200m crowns the curve's fastest runner.

Letsile Tebogo of Botswana won Paris 2024's 200m, stunning favorites to become Africa's first gold medalist in the event. If he advances through semifinals and reaches the final in 2028, the Coliseum crowd will witness history's defense. Heptathletes continue their campaign with shot put and 200m.

Evening energy peaks as finals week nears its climax. Medal ceremonies punctuate the program while heptathletes grind through their second day's opening events.
ATH14.potentialContendersIntro: Paris 2024 medalists could headline the 200m and 110m hurdles finals, with Tebogo defending his historic title if he qualifies.

potentialContenders.name: Gabby Thomas
potentialContenders.note: Paris 2024 women's 200m gold medalist, projected for semifinals

potentialContenders.name: Beatrice Chebet
potentialContenders.note: Paris 2024 women's 5000m gold medalist, projected for 10,000m

ATH15.blurb:
Saturday afternoon delivers five gold medal sprints: women's long jump, women's 400m, men's 400m, women's 200m, and the men's 4x100m relay. The heptathlon also crowns its champion after seven events across two days.

Gabby Thomas won Paris 2024's 200m, leading an American medal sweep. Marileidy Paulino dominated the women's 400m in Paris, setting a national record. Quincy Hall stunned the men's 400m field with a late surge. If these athletes return in 2028, expect fireworks on home soil.

The Coliseum roars for sprint finals and relay exchanges. Heptathletes finish their 800m finale, collapsing in exhaustion, while 4x100m teams navigate baton zones at maximum speed.
ATH15.potentialContendersIntro: Paris 2024 medalists could dominate across multiple sprint finals, headlined by Thomas, Paulino, and Hall defending their titles if they qualify.

potentialContenders.name: Tara Davis-Woodhall
potentialContenders.note: Paris 2024 women's long jump gold medalist

ATH16.blurb:
The penultimate evening at the Coliseum brings eight finals and semifinals across a marathon 2.5-hour sprint through field, track, and relay events. Gold medals will be awarded in women's high jump, javelin, 1500m, and men's 800m and 4x100m relay—five separate victory ceremonies packed into one session.

The men's 800m final is a tactical chess match at blazing speed, two laps of positioning and surge. The women's high jump and javelin finals unfold simultaneously on the infield while sprinters and middle-distance runners cycle through heats and medal races on the track.

Sunset light fills the open bowl as the session builds toward the men's 4x100m relay final, where four-person teams execute baton exchanges at full speed and hundredths of a second separate podium spots. The peristyle arch frames the homestretch under the San Gabriel Mountains.
ATH16.potentialContendersIntro: Paris 2024 medalists Yaroslava Mahuchikh, Emmanuel Wanyonyi, and Marco Arop are expected to defend their high jump gold and 800m medals, while relay lineups remain to be announced.

ATH17.blurb:
The closing night of track and field at the Coliseum delivers six straight finals with no preliminaries—pure medal races from first gun to last. Women's 100m hurdles and 4x100m relay, men's javelin and 5000m, and both 4x400m relays crown champions in rapid succession.

The men's 5000m final is 12.5 laps of pack tactics and late-race kicks, often decided in a final-lap sprint after 24 minutes of measured racing. Relay finals bookend the session with explosive baton passes and anchor-leg duels, while the javelin final adds field drama between track events.

This is the farewell to nine days of athletics at a venue steeped in Games history. The 1984 Carl Lewis sweep and the 1932 original athletics program both happened here; the same peristyle arch witnesses the final podium celebrations of 2028.
ATH17.potentialContendersIntro: Relay team lineups will be finalized closer to the Games, while individual event contenders will emerge from the week's earlier rounds and global championship results through 2027.

ATH18.blurb:
Two half-marathon race walk finals unfold in a single morning session, marking the debut of this distance at the Games. The shift from the traditional 20km format to 21.1km adds fresh variables — endurance, pacing strategy, and how world-class walkers adapt to the longer haul.

The men's field could feature Brian Pintado, who won Paris 2024's 20km in commanding fashion, alongside Spanish veteran Álvaro Martín and Brazil's Caio Bonfim. The women's race may see Yang Jiayu of China defending her title from the shorter distance, with Spain's María Pérez and Australia's Jemima Montag projected as contenders.

Race walking's technical demands — legal form, heel-toe contact, no visible loss of ground contact — create drama as judges patrol the course. Early morning starts keep competitors cool and deliver a rhythmic, relentless spectacle for spectators along the route.
ATH18.potentialContendersIntro: Paris 2024 medalists from the 20km events are expected to transition to the half-marathon distance. Recent world rankings and World Athletics Race Walking Team Championships results will shape the 2028 field.

ATH19.blurb:
The women's marathon final kicks off at first light along the Venice Beach Boardwalk, sending the world's best distance runners on a 42.195 km coastal journey through Los Angeles. Early morning start times are tradition in marathon racing, offering cooler conditions and spectacular sunrise views over the Pacific.

Sifan Hassan claimed gold in Paris 2024 after a stunning final kick, while Tigst Assefa and Hellen Obiri rounded out the podium in a race dominated by East African powerhouses. The 2028 field will likely feature returning medalists alongside emerging talents who've risen through World Marathon Majors and continental championships in the intervening years.

Spectators line the open-air promenade as runners pass multiple times on an out-and-back course, with ocean breezes and the iconic Venice skatepark and Muscle Beach forming a uniquely California backdrop. The three-hour window transforms the beachfront into a festival atmosphere, with roaring crowds greeting runners at every turn.
ATH19.potentialContendersIntro: Paris 2024 medalists Sifan Hassan, Tigst Assefa, and Hellen Obiri could return to defend their positions, though confirmation awaits national team selections through early 2028.

ATH20.blurb:
Sunrise over Venice Beach sets the stage for the men's marathon final, a 42.195 km test of endurance along the Pacific coastline. Tamirat Tola surged to gold in Paris 2024 with a commanding performance, while Bashir Abdi and Benson Kipruto battled for silver and bronze in scorching conditions.

The 2028 race promises another East African showdown, though the field won't be finalized until spring trials across competing nations determine who earns starting positions. Expect tactical racing early before the pace heats up in the final 10 km, where medals are won and lost.

The Venice Beach Boardwalk course offers spectators close-up views as runners loop through the iconic beachfront district, passing street performers and skate culture landmarks between surges of effort. Three hours of world-class racing unfold against a backdrop of California surf and sand, accessible to all as a free public event.
ATH20.potentialContendersIntro: Paris 2024 podium finishers Tamirat Tola, Bashir Abdi, and Benson Kipruto are likely contenders if they maintain form and secure national team spots through 2028 qualification windows.

BDM01.blurb:
The 2028 Games badminton competition opens with group play across all five medal events—men's and women's singles, men's and women's doubles, and mixed doubles. Nearly every athlete in the draw will see action on opening morning, making this the most accessible entry point to watch the global field.

Pairs that claimed Paris 2024 gold—Chinese Taipei's duo in men's doubles, China's tandems in women's doubles and mixed doubles—could return to defend their titles if they qualify. The group stage sorts 172 athletes into knockout brackets over the first week.

The climate-controlled Galen Center in Exposition Park seats over 10,000 and offers clear sightlines to multiple courts running simultaneously. Arrive early to catch shuttlecock speeds exceeding 200 mph and the rhythmic thwack of rallies echoing through the arena.
BDM01.potentialContendersIntro: Paris 2024 medalists from China, Chinese Taipei, Malaysia, Japan, and South Korea are projected to qualify for the 2028 Games if form holds through the ranking period ending April 2028.

BDM02.blurb:
Saturday afternoon group play continues with men's and women's doubles alongside women's singles. China's depth in women's doubles—they swept gold and silver in Paris—should be on display again if their top pairs qualify for 2028.

Each doubles category fields 16 pairs total, so every group match carries elimination stakes. The format rewards consistency: lose your group and the knockout rounds start without you.

The afternoon session offers a quieter atmosphere than the evening primetime slots, with smaller but knowledgeable crowds who appreciate the technical nuance of doubles positioning and deceptive net play.
BDM02.potentialContendersIntro: Paris 2024 women's doubles podium finishers from China and Japan are likely to send strong pairs if current rankings hold through the April 2028 cutoff.

BDM03.blurb:
The evening session wraps opening day with men's and women's doubles and singles group matches. Malaysia's bronze-medal men's doubles pair from Paris could return, while Japan's women's doubles duo will aim to improve on their third-place finish.

Group play runs until Tuesday, so patience is required—these are the opening volleys of a two-week campaign. But the best rallies emerge when players are fresh and the pressure is still building.

Evening sessions draw the largest and loudest crowds at the Galen Center, with hometown fans filling the 10,000-seat arena. The venue's acoustics amplify every smash and every roar when a rally stretches past 30 shots.
BDM03.potentialContendersIntro: Malaysia and Japan medaled in doubles events at Paris 2024 and are projected to field competitive pairs again if they secure qualification.

BDM04.blurb:
Sunday morning continues group play across all five events. China's Zheng Siwei and Huang Yaqiong, who claimed mixed doubles gold in Paris, could return to chase a repeat if they qualify through the world rankings.

With 172 athletes competing and only 86 quota spots per gender, the field represents the global elite. Even group matches feature world-class shotmaking and tactical chess matches between coaches.

The Sunday morning slot draws families and local badminton clubs who appreciate the relaxed schedule and affordable tickets. It's a rare chance to watch future medalists before the knockout rounds raise the stakes.
BDM04.potentialContendersIntro: China's mixed doubles Paris 2024 champions are projected to seek qualification, while singles and doubles fields remain wide open pending the April 2028 ranking deadline.

BDM05.blurb:
Sunday afternoon features another round of group play across all events. Chinese Taipei's men's doubles pair—Lee Yang and Wang Chi-lin, defending their Tokyo title with Paris gold—are favorites to qualify again if they stay healthy.

The doubles ranking system rewards consistency over the year-long qualification window, so current form matters as much as past results. Expect established pairs to face rising challengers hungry to break through.

Afternoon sessions at the Galen Center offer comfortable indoor viewing with climate control—a welcome respite from Los Angeles summer heat. Multiple courts run simultaneously, so position yourself to watch your favorite discipline.
BDM05.potentialContendersIntro: Chinese Taipei's back-to-back gold medalists in men's doubles are projected to compete again if they qualify, while China and Malaysia remain strong in multiple categories.

BDM06.blurb:
Sunday evening closes the second day of group play with all five events in action. Japan's mixed doubles bronze medalists from Paris could return, while the women's doubles draw may again feature multiple Chinese pairs.

Group standings begin to crystallize by the third day—some athletes are already playing for knockout seeding while others face elimination. The pace accelerates as players adapt to venue conditions and scout opponents.

Evening badminton at the Galen Center draws passionate fans who understand the sport's intricacies. The crowd erupts for diving saves and cross-court smashes that barely clip the line.
BDM06.potentialContendersIntro: Japan's Paris 2024 bronze medalists in mixed doubles and women's doubles are projected to field strong pairs if they secure qualification through world rankings.

BDM07.blurb:
Monday morning's group play begins the final push before knockout rounds start later in the week. China's second women's doubles pair, silver medalists in Paris, could return alongside their gold-winning teammates.

With a maximum of 16 athletes per country across all events, national federations make strategic choices about which specialists to send. Depth matters—some countries field multiple medal contenders while others concentrate talent in one or two categories.

The Monday morning crowd skews toward badminton insiders and international fans who've traveled specifically for the sport. Expect small but engaged audiences who appreciate subtle tactical adjustments.
BDM07.potentialContendersIntro: China's depth in women's doubles—gold and silver in Paris—positions them to potentially field two competitive pairs again if both qualify for the 2028 Games.

BDM08.blurb:
Monday afternoon features more group matches across all categories. South Korea's mixed doubles pair, silver medalists in Paris behind China's dominant duo, are projected to qualify again and seek an upgrade.

Each match uses a best-of-three games format with rally scoring to 21 points, so comebacks are always possible. Watch for momentum swings when a trailing pair adjusts their court positioning or switches up serving patterns.

The Galen Center's compact footprint puts even upper-deck seats close to the action compared to larger arenas. Shuttlecocks move so fast that close viewing makes a difference in appreciating the athletes' reaction time.
BDM08.potentialContendersIntro: South Korea's Paris 2024 mixed doubles silver medalists are likely to return if they maintain their world ranking through the April 2028 qualification cutoff.

BDM09.blurb:
Monday evening's session marks the final group matches before knockout play begins. Paris 2024 medalists from China, Chinese Taipei, Malaysia, Japan, and South Korea are all projected to field athletes if they secure quota spots.

By now, group winners are determined and seeding is finalized. But elimination-round intensity still emerges in matches between teams fighting for the last knockout berths or jockeying for favorable bracket positions.

Evening crowds at the Galen Center bring energy—drums, cheers for spectacular shots, and collective gasps when a rally reaches 50 strokes. The venue's acoustics make it feel louder than its 10,000 capacity suggests.
BDM09.potentialContendersIntro: Multiple Paris 2024 medal-winning nations are projected to qualify athletes across the five events, though exact rosters depend on world rankings through April 2028.

BDM10.blurb:
Tuesday morning concludes group play in men's and women's doubles plus singles. No mixed doubles matches are scheduled—that category advances to quarterfinals next.

The session determines final knockout brackets for the other four events. Expect tight matches between teams needing wins to advance, while some already-qualified pairs may rest key players.

The Tuesday morning slot offers one last chance to watch group-stage badminton at affordable prices before quarterfinals and beyond carry premium ticket costs. The atmosphere is workmanlike as athletes focus on securing their knockout spots.
BDM10.potentialContendersIntro: Paris 2024 medalists in men's doubles, women's doubles, and singles events are projected to appear if they qualify, with the Tuesday session finalizing brackets for those categories.

BDM11.blurb:
Tuesday afternoon features the final group matches in men's and women's doubles plus women's singles. An Se-Yeong of South Korea, Paris 2024 women's singles champion, is projected to compete again if she maintains top form through qualification.

The women's singles field could also include China's He Bingjiao, Paris silver medalist, and Indonesia's Gregoria Mariska Tunjung, who claimed bronze. Rankings through April 2028 will determine the full draw.

Afternoon sessions offer a relaxed atmosphere as group play winds down. Fans can watch emerging singles stars who may dominate the sport for the next Games cycle.
BDM11.potentialContendersIntro: Women's singles Paris 2024 medalists An Se-Yeong (South Korea), He Bingjiao (China), and Gregoria Mariska Tunjung (Indonesia) are projected to qualify if current form holds.

BDM12.blurb:
Tuesday evening closes group play in men's and women's doubles and singles. With Viktor Axelsen retired due to chronic back problems, the men's singles field is wide open—Paris silver medalist Kunlavut Vitidsarn of Thailand and bronze winner Lee Zii Jia of Malaysia could contend if they qualify.

The men's singles draw will feature a new champion for the first time since Tokyo 2020. Expect tactical adjustments as players adapt to the vacuum left by Axelsen's dominance.

Evening crowds bring energy for the final group matches before knockout intensity takes over. The Galen Center's indoor setting amplifies the shuttlecock's distinctive sound as it rockets off rackets at speeds faster than some tennis serves.
BDM12.potentialContendersIntro: Men's singles Paris 2024 silver medalist Kunlavut Vitidsarn (Thailand) and bronze winner Lee Zii Jia (Malaysia) are projected to qualify, with the gold-medal vacancy creating an open field.

BDM13.blurb:
Mixed doubles quarterfinals arrive on Wednesday morning, determining which four pairs advance to medal rounds. China's Zheng Siwei and Huang Yaqiong, Paris gold medalists, are favorites to qualify and defend their title.

South Korea's Paris silver medalists could return for another run, while Japan's bronze-winning pair may also compete if they secure a quota spot. The knockout format means every point matters—lose and you're eliminated.

Morning quarterfinals offer an intimate atmosphere as the field narrows to elite contenders. The Galen Center's climate-controlled setting keeps conditions consistent, eliminating wind as a variable and rewarding pure skill.
BDM13.potentialContendersIntro: Mixed doubles quarterfinals will likely feature Paris 2024 medalists from China (gold), South Korea (silver), and Japan (bronze) if they qualify through world rankings.

BDM14.blurb:
Wednesday afternoon features the last men's and women's singles group matches. With Axelsen retired, the men's field is unexpectedly open—Paris runner-up Kunlavut Vitidsarn and bronze medalist Lee Zii Jia are projected to qualify and contend for gold.

Women's singles should again showcase An Se-Yeong's precision and court coverage that dominated Paris, while China's He Bingjiao could return to challenge for gold after settling for silver in 2024.

The afternoon session offers a final chance to watch singles at group-stage prices before knockout rounds begin. Position yourself near center court to catch the headliners' matches.
BDM14.potentialContendersIntro: Singles group play will likely feature Paris 2024 medalists including An Se-Yeong (KOR women's gold), Kunlavut Vitidsarn (THA men's silver), and He Bingjiao (CHN women's silver) if they qualify.

BDM15.blurb:
Wednesday evening wraps men's and women's singles group play. Indonesia's Gregoria Mariska Tunjung, Paris bronze medalist, could return to the women's field if she qualifies through the year-long ranking period.

The session determines final singles knockout brackets. Seeding matters—a favorable draw can mean avoiding the top contenders until later rounds, while a tough bracket forces early showdowns.

Evening crowds fill the Galen Center for the final group matches, bringing drums and national flags. The venue's 22 private suites offer premium sightlines for fans willing to splurge, while general seating puts you close to courtside intensity.
BDM15.potentialContendersIntro: Singles group play concludes with projected appearances by Paris 2024 medalists including Gregoria Mariska Tunjung (INA women's bronze), Kunlavut Vitidsarn (THA men's silver), and An Se-Yeong (KOR women's gold).

BDM16.blurb:
Three major events converge in this morning session at Galen Center, with mixed doubles semifinal action determining the gold medal finalists. The Paris 2024 champions from China and silver medalists from South Korea could return to defend their positions if they qualify, alongside emerging pairs from Japan and Indonesia.

Women's doubles quarterfinals narrow the field to four pairs, with China's dominant program likely fielding multiple teams after sweeping gold and silver in Paris. Women's singles round of 16 features the sport's fastest and most athletic rallies, with An Se-Yeong of South Korea potentially defending her Paris gold.

The climate-controlled arena seats over 10,000 and offers close sightlines to see the shuttlecock's 200+ mph smashes. Morning sessions provide an accessible entry point to experience elite badminton before the evening crowds arrive.
BDM16.potentialContendersIntro: Paris 2024 medalists could dominate if they qualify, with China's mixed doubles champions and Korea's women's singles gold medalist An Se-Yeong likely to compete.

BDM17.blurb:
The round of 16 showcases badminton's explosive athleticism across both singles draws. With Viktor Axelsen retired from competition, the men's field opens up for a new generation of champions including Paris silver medalist Kunlavut Vitidsarn of Thailand and bronze medalist Lee Zii Jia of Malaysia.

Women's singles continues with An Se-Yeong of South Korea projected to defend her Paris title against challengers from China, Indonesia, and Japan. Each best-of-three match features rapid-fire exchanges and diving saves across the full court.

Afternoon sessions at Galen Center offer cooler pricing and multiple matches back-to-back. The compact arena configuration brings you close enough to hear the shuttlecock's distinctive snap off the racquet strings.
BDM17.potentialContendersIntro: Paris 2024 medalists Kunlavut Vitidsarn and Lee Zii Jia could lead the men's field, while An Se-Yeong likely defends her women's title if she qualifies.

BDM18.blurb:
Prime-time badminton brings men's doubles quarterfinals to center stage, where defending champions from Chinese Taipei could face renewed challenges from China and Malaysia's Paris bronze medalists. The doubles format emphasizes teamwork and court coverage, with pairs executing rapid exchanges at the net.

Men's singles round of 16 continues sorting the draw after Viktor Axelsen's retirement opened new pathways to gold. Thailand's Kunlavut Vitidsarn and Malaysia's Lee Zii Jia are projected contenders if they qualify, alongside rising stars from Indonesia, Japan, and China.

Evening sessions pack the arena with energy as Los Angeles crowds discover badminton's blend of finesse and power. The 10,000-seat configuration creates an intimate atmosphere where every shuttle trajectory is visible from anywhere in the venue.
BDM18.potentialContendersIntro: Chinese Taipei's Paris 2024 gold medalists Lee Yang and Wang Chi-lin could return to defend, while Paris medalists in men's singles advance through the round of 16.

Mixed doubles crowns its champion in this extended morning session featuring both medal matches. China's Zheng Siwei and Huang Yaqiong won Paris gold and could defend if they qualify, facing potential rematches against South Korea's silver medalists or Japan's bronze pair.

Women's doubles semifinals run between the medal matches, with China's deep program likely placing multiple pairs in contention after their Paris gold-silver sweep. Japan and South Korea bring strong challengers in a discipline that rewards precision and tactical variety.

This five-hour session delivers three high-stakes matches including the first badminton gold awarded at the 2028 Games. Galen Center's climate control ensures athlete performance peaks regardless of LA's summer heat outside.
BDM19.potentialContendersIntro: Paris 2024 mixed doubles medalists Zheng Siwei/Huang Yaqiong (gold) and Korea's Kim Won-ho/Jeong Na-eun (silver) could return, while China likely fields multiple women's doubles pairs.

An evening marathon session spans seven hours with men's singles quarterfinals determining semifinalists and men's doubles semis setting up the gold medal match. Thailand's Kunlavut Vitidsarn and Malaysia's Lee Zii Jia are projected to compete if they qualify, with Viktor Axelsen's retirement reshaping title contention.

Men's doubles features Chinese Taipei defending their back-to-back titles against China's program and Malaysia's Aaron Chia/Soh Wooi Yik if they qualify. The doubles format showcases lightning reflexes at the net and strategic shot placement across the full 44-foot court.

Friday night badminton at Galen Center offers exceptional value with multiple medal-contending matches. The arena's 22 private suites provide premium viewing, while general seating keeps you close to shuttlecocks traveling faster than highway speeds.
BDM20.potentialContendersIntro: Paris 2024 medalists across both events could dominate, with Kunlavut Vitidsarn and Lee Zii Jia projected in men's singles, and Chinese Taipei's doubles champions defending their title.

BDM21.blurb:
Women's doubles awards its medals in a focused Saturday morning session. China swept gold and silver in Paris with two dominant pairs and could repeat the feat if they qualify, while Japan's bronze medalists and emerging South Korean teams challenge for the podium.

Both medal matches play in rapid succession, with the bronze match warming up the crowd before the gold final. The women's doubles game emphasizes tactical shot variety and court positioning, with pairs mixing aggressive smashes and delicate net play.

A compact three-hour session makes this accessible for families and casual fans. Galen Center's downtown-adjacent location in Exposition Park allows easy pre-match exploration of LA's museums and gardens before the morning competition begins.
BDM21.potentialContendersIntro: China's Chen Qingchen/Jia Yifan (Paris gold) and Liu Shengshu/Tan Ning (Paris silver) could return if they qualify, facing Japan's bronze medalists and other contenders.

BDM22.blurb:
Women's singles quarterfinals determine the final four in a nearly five-hour evening showcase. An Se-Yeong of South Korea dominated Paris 2024 and could defend her title if she qualifies, while China's He Bingjiao (silver) and Indonesia's Gregoria Mariska Tunjung (bronze) likely return as challengers.

Each best-of-three quarterfinal tests endurance and tactical adaptability over potential 60-minute battles. The women's game combines explosive power with exceptional court coverage, requiring athletes to defend the full 17-by-44-foot singles court.

Saturday night badminton draws energized LA crowds to Galen Center's intimate indoor setting. The venue's flexible seating configuration brings spectators close enough to hear players' shoes squeak and racquets whistle through air.
BDM22.potentialContendersIntro: Paris 2024 medalists An Se-Yeong (gold), He Bingjiao (silver), and Gregoria Mariska Tunjung (bronze) are projected to compete if they qualify through rankings.

BDM23.blurb:
Men's doubles awards its gold medal while men's singles semifinals set up the championship match. Chinese Taipei's Lee Yang and Wang Chi-lin chase a historic third consecutive title if they qualify, facing China's powerful program and Malaysia's Paris bronze pair.

Men's singles features emerging stars competing for spots in Monday's final, with Viktor Axelsen's retirement opening the door for a new champion. Thailand's Kunlavut Vitidsarn and Malaysia's Lee Zii Jia could meet in a potential semifinal rematch of their Paris 2024 final if both qualify.

Sunday morning at Galen Center delivers badminton's fastest action with 200+ mph smashes echoing through the arena. The session combines medal ceremony pageantry with high-stakes semifinal drama across a compact three-and-a-half-hour window.
BDM23.potentialContendersIntro: Chinese Taipei's Lee Yang/Wang Chi-lin seek a third straight gold if they qualify, while Paris 2024 singles medalists Kunlavut Vitidsarn and Lee Zii Jia could reach the semifinals.

BDM24.blurb:
Women's singles semifinals determine the gold medal finalists in this Sunday evening doubleheader. An Se-Yeong of South Korea could defend her Paris title if she qualifies, facing challenges from China's He Bingjiao and Indonesia's Gregoria Mariska Tunjung among others projected to compete.

The men's doubles bronze medal match opens the session, with Malaysia's Paris bronze pair potentially returning alongside Chinese and Japanese teams. Doubles bronze matches carry their own intensity as teams battle for podium recognition after falling short of the final.

Evening sessions at Galen Center showcase badminton under arena lighting that highlights shuttlecock trajectories. The nearly four-hour window includes medal ceremony traditions for men's doubles alongside the high-stakes women's singles semifinals.
BDM24.potentialContendersIntro: Paris 2024 women's singles medalists An Se-Yeong, He Bingjiao, and Gregoria Mariska Tunjung could reach the semifinals if they qualify, while Malaysia's doubles pair seeks bronze again.

BDM25.blurb:
Men's singles crowns its champion in back-to-back medal matches to conclude Monday morning. With Viktor Axelsen retired due to chronic back problems, the field opens for a new generation including Paris silver medalist Kunlavut Vitidsarn of Thailand and bronze medalist Lee Zii Jia of Malaysia if they qualify.

The bronze match tests resilience after semifinal heartbreak, followed by the gold final determining badminton's premier individual title. Best-of-three format means matches can swing on single points, with players executing deceptive drops, thunderous smashes, and court-spanning retrieves.

Galen Center's morning atmosphere crackles with tension as shuttlecocks fly at highway speeds. The venue's acoustic design amplifies the crowd's energy, creating an intimate championship setting for fewer than 11,000 witnesses to history.

BDM26.blurb:
Women's singles concludes badminton competition with its final medal matches on Monday evening. An Se-Yeong of South Korea seeks to defend her Paris gold if she qualifies, potentially facing China's He Bingjiao or Indonesia's Gregoria Mariska Tunjung in a rematch of recent championships.

The bronze match delivers immediate drama, followed by the gold final determining the women's singles champion. These athletes cover the full court with explosive footwork and tactical shot placement, mixing power and finesse across matches that can exceed an hour.

BMF01.blurb:
Witness the sport's newest generation carve air in qualification rounds where riders launch towering tricks on massive ramps and bowls. Both men's and women's fields hit the course in a double-run format, every athlete chasing clean landings and technical combos to punch their ticket to finals.

Paris 2024 medalists José Torres Gil, Kieran Reilly, Deng Yawen, and Perris Benegas could return to defend their standings, though official 2028 rosters won't be locked until mid-2028. Qualification opened in late 2026, with the world's top park riders now in the hunt for one of 12 spots per gender.

The open-air venue sits in Sepulveda Basin, where San Fernando Valley's dry summer heat meets purpose-built ramps designed for maximum amplitude. Catch runs early in the session before the crowd swells and riders push the envelope on second attempts.
BMF01.potentialContendersIntro: Paris 2024 medalists are projected contenders if they qualify, alongside rising riders from the global BMX Freestyle circuit.

BMX01.blurb:
The BMX Racing competition kicks off with high-speed quarterfinals and last chance runs for both men and women. Riders navigate an eight-meter starting ramp and a 400-meter track of jumps, berms, and rhythm sections at speeds approaching 40 mph, with only the top finishers advancing to Sunday's medal rounds.

Paris 2024 saw France dominate the men's podium in a historic sweep, while Australia's Saya Sakakibara claimed women's gold. Expect those medalists—if they qualify and compete—to shape their quarterfinal heats, though upsets are common in a format where one mistake ends a campaign.

Valley Complex sits in the San Fernando Valley's Sepulveda Basin, a park built on natural terrain that puts spectators close to the track's jumps and turns. You'll feel the snap of gate starts and hear wheels clatter over dirt rollers as riders battle for semifinal spots.
BMX01.potentialContendersIntro: Paris 2024 medalists could headline their respective heats, though qualification through 2025 World Cup events will determine the final field.

BMX02.blurb:
Two gold medals will be decided in a single session as the men's and women's BMX Racing finals crown champions. After semifinals thin the field to eight per gender, the finals are winner-take-all sprints—roughly 35 seconds of full-throttle racing where a fractional misstep at a jump or berm costs a podium spot.

France's Paris 2024 podium sweep positions Joris Daudet, Sylvain André, and Romain Mahieu as likely medal threats if they compete. Saya Sakakibara and silver medalist Manon Veenstra could renew their rivalry from Paris, though the 2025 World Cup qualifying circuit will shape the actual field.

The outdoor Valley Complex track is built into park terrain, offering close sightlines as riders explode from the eight-meter gate and attack technical sections. Expect roaring starts, mid-air collisions, and tight finishes—BMX finals deliver drama in under a minute per race.
BMX02.potentialContendersIntro: Paris 2024 medalists are projected contenders, though four years of World Cup racing could shuffle the hierarchy by July 2028.

BOX01.blurb:
Opening day of boxing at the 2028 Games brings six weight classes to downtown LA's Peacock Theater in a rapid-fire afternoon session. Women's 54kg, 60kg, and 65kg fighters take their first swings in round of 32 action alongside men's 55kg and 65kg, while the men's super heavyweight division (+90kg) narrows the field in round of 16 bouts.

The single-elimination format means every punch counts from the start — lose and you're out. Early rounds showcase raw talent from across the globe, with boxers fighting to advance and keep medal hopes alive.

Peacock Theater seats 7,100 with no seat farther than 220 feet from the ring. The compact sight lines and theater acoustics amplify every crowd roar, creating an electric atmosphere as fighters trade combinations under the lights.
BOX01.potentialContendersIntro: No athletes have officially qualified for the 2028 Games as of mid-2026, but the field is expected to draw from reigning world champions and Paris 2024 medalists across these weight classes.

BOX02.blurb:
The evening session mirrors the afternoon's lineup with a fresh slate of fighters across the same six weight classes. Round of 32 battles continue for women's 54kg, 60kg, and 65kg and men's 55kg and 65kg, while super heavyweight hopefuls push deeper into the bracket at round of 16.

This is where unknown contenders make their names — early rounds have a history of upsets as hungry newcomers face established stars. Expect fast-paced action as boxers work to impose their styles and survive the opening gauntlet.

Under Peacock Theater's intimate ceiling, the crowd sits close enough to hear the snap of leather on skin. Four levels of lobbies offer VIP lounges and concessions between bouts, making this a full evening of boxing in the heart of L.A. Live.

BOX03.blurb:

Five weight classes across both genders means constant rotation — no lulls, just a steady stream of three-round bouts. The 70kg women's bracket could feature Paris medalists looking to defend their status.

CER01A.blurb:
The 2028 Summer Games launch with an unprecedented dual-venue Opening Ceremony, splitting the spectacle between the ultramodern 2028 Stadium in Inglewood and the historic LA Memorial Coliseum. The ceremony begins at 5:00 PM at the 2028 Stadium, a gleaming indoor arena making its Games debut just eight years after opening.

Expect a blend of cutting-edge technology and Los Angeles showmanship as the host city welcomes athletes from over 200 nations. The innovative format allows for intimate moments under the translucent roof of the 2028 Stadium while honoring the legacy of previous Games.

Seating inside the compact 38,000-capacity bowl offers excellent sightlines from every angle. Natural light filters through the roof as the ceremony unfolds, creating an atmosphere unlike any previous Games opening.

CER02.blurb:
The Games conclude beneath the stars at LA Memorial Coliseum, where the grand peristyle arch frames a celebration marking the Coliseum's historic third Games appearance. This open-air amphitheater hosted ceremonies in 1932 and 1984; now it closes the circle for Los Angeles.

The evening program blends tradition with contemporary Los Angeles culture, honoring 16 days of competition and handing the torch to the next host city. Athletes gather on the Coliseum floor as flags are lowered and the flame is extinguished.

The renovated terraced seating rises steeply from the arena floor, offering 77,500 spectators clear views across the elliptical bowl. The peristyle's Court of Honor glows as twilight deepens into night, creating an unforgettable backdrop for the Games' final moments.

CKT01.blurb:

Six teams divided into two groups of three begin their first-round group stage matches, with each nation fielding a 15-player squad in the fast-paced T20 format.

The qualification process prioritizes geographic diversity, aiming for one team from each of the five continental regions plus host United States. Results from this opening round carry forward through the second phase to determine the final standings for medal placements.

The 6,000-seat stadium sits within the sprawling 543-acre Fairplex, where scenic parks and plazas surround the cricket grounds, offering a relaxed morning atmosphere for this historic opening session.

CKT24.blurb:
Evening second-round action as teams jockey for final position. By now, the pecking order is taking shape—some squads are locking down gold-medal spots, others are fighting to stay in bronze range.

Cricket's beauty is its unpredictability: underdogs can upset favorites with one inspired bowling spell or a fearless batting display. The format rewards both preparation and improvisation.

Fairgrounds Cricket Stadium under lights captures the energy of cricket's global reach, transplanted to a Southern California summer night. It's a unique fusion of tradition and newness.
CKT24.potentialContendersIntro: West Indies and other qualified nations could headline as the tournament enters its final group-stage push.

potentialContenders.name: West Indies
potentialContenders.note: Two-time T20 World Cup winners, expected to contend based on 2025 form.

potentialContenders.name: United States
potentialContenders.note: Host nation with direct entry, likely competing for medal positions.

potentialContenders.name: India
potentialContenders.note: 2024 T20 World Cup champions, favorites to finish atop the table.

CKT25.blurb:
The penultimate second-round session brings clarity: who's in the medal matches and who's watching from the stands. Teams play for seeding now, trying to avoid the toughest opponent in the finals.

T20 cricket's pace means there's no coasting—every ball matters, every decision under scrutiny. A dropped catch or a missed run-out can haunt a team when the final table is tallied.

Morning matches at the Fairplex offer a quieter, more intimate vibe before the chaos of medal day. It's the calm before the storm.
CKT25.potentialContendersIntro: With host USA expected to qualify directly, they could face traditional powers like England or India in this late group-stage session.

potentialContenders.name: United States
potentialContenders.note: Host nation with direct qualification, competing in second-round standings.

potentialContenders.name: England
potentialContenders.note: 2022 T20 World Cup champions, projected to be in medal contention.

CKT26.blurb:
The final second-round match completes the standings, setting up bronze and gold medal contests. By the time this session ends, four teams know their fate—two will play for third, two for first.

Cricket at this level is about composure under pressure. Teams that held their nerve through two rounds of high-stakes matches now face the ultimate test: delivering when a medal is on the line.

Evening light fades over Pomona as the group stage closes. Tomorrow, the knockout drama begins.
CKT26.potentialContendersIntro: Top-ranked teams like India and South Africa could be securing their spots for medal matches in this final group-stage session.

potentialContenders.name: India

potentialContenders.note: 2024 T20 World Cup finalists, projected to contend for medals.

potentialContenders.name: Pakistan
potentialContenders.note: Strong T20 pedigree, could be fighting for medal-round placement.

CKT27.blurb:
Bronze is on the line as third and fourth from the final standings meet in a winner-takes-all T20 clash. It's not gold, but a podium finish in cricket's Games return carries historical weight.

Third-place matches reveal character—teams must reset emotionally after falling short of the gold-medal game and deliver one last performance. Expect urgency, pride, and no shortage of competitive fire.

Morning cricket at Fairgrounds Cricket Stadium sets the stage for medal day. By lunch, one nation will celebrate bronze; the other heads home empty-handed.
CKT27.potentialContendersIntro: With qualification geography-based and no finalized rosters, expect strong T20 nations like England, New Zealand, or Pakistan if they finish third or fourth in standings.

CKT28.blurb:
Cricket's gold medal match: the culmination of 128 years away from the Games and nine days of T20 drama in Southern California. The top two teams from the combined standings meet for the ultimate prize.

Expect the world's best cricketers at full intensity—power hitters, crafty bowlers, athletic fielders, tactical captains. Twenty overs per side, roughly four hours, one chance at history.

Fairgrounds Cricket Stadium hosts a moment bigger than sport: cricket's return to the global stage, with gold hanging in the balance. The crowd, the stakes, the pressure—everything amplified for a final that will be replayed for generations.
CKT28.potentialContendersIntro: While rosters aren't final, recent T20 World Cup champions India and traditional powers like England or South Africa are projected to contest the gold medal if they qualify and advance.

potentialContenders.name: India
potentialContenders.note: 2024 T20 World Cup champions, clear favorites to reach the gold medal match.

potentialContenders.name: England
potentialContenders.note: 2022 T20 World Cup winners, projected to be in gold medal contention.

potentialContenders.name: South Africa
potentialContenders.note: 2024 T20 World Cup finalists, expected to challenge for gold if qualified.

potentialContenders.name: United States
potentialContenders.note: Host nation with direct entry—an underdog gold medal run would be historic.

CLB01.blurb:
The Men's Boulder semifinal opens climbing's six-day run at Long Beach, narrowing a field of at least 12 competitors to the finalists who will chase medals the next morning. Boulder tests raw problem-solving power—climbers get four minutes per route, no ropes, no preview, just chalk and instinct on dynamic holds that demand equal parts strength and creativity.

Unlike the combined format of Paris, Boulder now stands alone as a medal discipline, spotlighting specialists who thrive on explosive sequences and beta-reading under pressure. Qualification paths through the 2027 World Championships and Continental events shape the field, with each country capped at two athletes per discipline.

The temporary arena sits steps from the Pacific, ocean breezes drifting through the open-air seating as climbers chalk up beneath walls framed by marine light.
CLB01.potentialContendersIntro: The field will emerge from 2027 qualification events including the World Championships in Brno and regional qualifiers; top boulderers from the World Cup circuit are projected to contend.

potentialContenders.name: Tomoa Narasaki
potentialContenders.note: Tokyo 2020 combined silver medalist, dominant boulderer with multiple World Cup titles

potentialContenders.name: Colin Duffy
potentialContenders.note: Youth world champion and emerging power on the World Cup circuit

potentialContenders.name: Mejdi Schalck
potentialContenders.note: Consistent World Cup podium finisher in Boulder

potentialContenders.name: Yoshiyuki Ogata
potentialContenders.note: Tokyo combined finalist with strong boulder credentials

CLB02.blurb:
Speed qualification opens with dual-gender heats on the standardized 15-meter wall, clocking the 14 fastest men and women as they race against identical routes and the ruthless timer. Paris medalists Veddriq Leonardo, Wu Peng, and Sam Watson headline the men's side, while Poland's Aleksandra Mirosław and Aleksandra Kałucka return alongside China's Deng Lijuan for the women.

Qualification times seed tomorrow's knockout brackets—fractions of a second separate seeding positions that dictate matchups through quarterfinals and beyond. Speed is climbing's most explosive spectacle: five seconds of adrenaline, no margin for error, pure reaction drilled into muscle memory.

Afternoon sun warms the Convention Center lot as the Pacific gleams beyond the walls, spectators tracking split times and false starts from open-air grandstands.
CLB02.potentialContendersIntro: Paris 2024 medalists anchor both fields, with the men's and women's top qualifiers seeding knockout rounds.

potentialContenders.name: Veddriq Leonardo
potentialContenders.note: Paris 2024 gold medalist, world record holder

potentialContenders.name: Aleksandra Mirosław
potentialContenders.note: Paris 2024 gold medalist, dominant on the women's circuit

CLB03.blurb:
Women's Boulder semifinal unfolds on walls set with cryptic sequences that reward climbers who can read holds and commit to high-risk dynos. At least 12 athletes vie for finalist spots, each tackling four boulder problems in isolation—no beta from teammates, just four minutes per route to unlock the crux and top out.

Janja Garnbret, if she qualifies in Boulder after her combined dominance at Tokyo and Paris, could anchor a field shaped by 2027 World Championships and Continental results. The standalone Boulder format elevates specialists who thrive on compact power problems over endurance walls.

Morning light filters through the open-air structure as chalk clouds rise against the Long Beach skyline, the Pacific a blue ribbon beyond the competition zone.
CLB03.potentialContendersIntro: Janja Garnbret is a potential dual qualifier in Boulder and Lead; the field will include top World Cup boulderers from 2027 qualification events.

potentialContenders.name: Janja Garnbret
potentialContenders.note: Tokyo combined gold, Paris combined gold—if she qualifies for Boulder

potentialContenders.name: Oriane Bertone
potentialContenders.note: Rising star with strong showings on the World Cup Boulder circuit

potentialContenders.name: Miho Nonaka
potentialContenders.note: Tokyo combined silver medalist, experienced boulderer

CLB04.blurb:
Men's Lead semifinal moves to the evening slot, filtering at least 12 climbers down to the finalists who will contest medals two nights later. Lead demands endurance and tactical route-reading over 15-meter+ walls, athletes clipping quickdraws as they climb into thinner air and shrinking holds.

Dual qualification between Boulder and Lead is permitted under the 2028 format, potentially bringing versatile competitors into both medal hunts without inflating country quotas. The 2027 World Championships and regional qualifiers determine who earns the right to rope up in Long Beach.

Twilight settles over the Convention Center lot, ocean breezes cooling the grandstands as climbers chalk up beneath floodlit walls and the crowd leans in for every clipped draw.
CLB04.potentialContendersIntro: The field will be shaped by 2027 qualification events; dual Boulder-Lead specialists could appear if they navigate the quota system.

potentialContenders.name: Jakob Schubert
potentialContenders.note: Tokyo combined bronze, multiple-time Lead world champion

potentialContenders.name: Adam Ondra
potentialContenders.note: Legendary lead climber, Tokyo finalist, if he qualifies and competes

potentialContenders.name: Ao Yurikusa

CLB05.blurb:
Speed crowns its champions in a single morning blitz—quarterfinals, semis, and medal finals for both men and women, all head-to-head knockout races on the identical 15-meter route. Paris gold medalists Veddriq Leonardo and Aleksandra Mirosław return to defend their crowns, while American Sam Watson seeks redemption after his Paris bronze.

One slip, one hesitation, and the climber in the next lane punches the buzzer first. Finals come down to hundredths of a second, world records within reach if conditions align.

The temporary venue pulses with crowd energy, ocean horizon framing the walls as dual timers flash red and green, announcing winners before climbers even hit the mats.
CLB05.potentialContendersIntro: Paris 2024 medalists headline the knockout brackets, with 14 qualifiers per gender racing for gold.

potentialContenders.name: Veddriq Leonardo
potentialContenders.note: Paris 2024 gold medalist, reigning world record holder

CLB06.blurb:
Women's Lead semifinal cuts the field to finalists under the glow of Long Beach's evening lights, athletes roped in and climbing walls that stretch toward the open sky. Lead rewards endurance and nerve—holds thin as climbers ascend, every clipped quickdraw a checkpoint, every rest a gamble against lactic burn.

Janja Garnbret could appear if she qualifies for both Lead and Boulder, leveraging the dual-discipline allowance. The 2027 qualification circuit shapes the roster, with each nation limited to two climbers per discipline.

Twilight bleeds into dusk, ocean breeze carrying chalk dust as the crowd watches climbers rope-check and stretch, preparing for 15 meters of vertical chess against gravity and fatigue.
CLB06.potentialContendersIntro: Janja Garnbret is a potential dual qualifier; the field will draw from 2027 World Championships and Continental qualifiers.

potentialContenders.name: Janja Garnbret
potentialContenders.note: Tokyo combined gold, Paris combined gold—if she qualifies for Lead

potentialContenders.name: Chaehyun Seo
potentialContenders.note: Paris combined finalist, strong lead climber

CLB07.blurb:
Men's Boulder final decides the first standalone Boulder medals in Games history, finalists facing fresh problems never climbed before—four routes, four minutes each, no rope, no preview, just holds and instinct. Tokyo and Paris combined formats are gone; this is pure boulderers' territory now.

The crowd watches attempts and falls in real time, erupts when a climber sticks the crux dyno or reads the beta mid-sequence. Dual Boulder-Lead qualifiers may compete here before shifting focus to the endurance walls two days later.

Morning sun warms the Convention Center lot, Pacific light glinting off chalk-dusted holds as finalists prepare to solve problems that could define their careers.
CLB07.potentialContendersIntro: Finalists will emerge from the semifinal two days prior; top World Cup boulderers are projected to contest medals.

CLB08.blurb:
Women's Boulder final awards the discipline's first standalone medals, finalists tackling four unseen problems in isolation—compact, explosive sequences that punish hesitation and reward commitment. Janja Garnbret looms as the name to beat if she qualifies, having dominated combined formats at Tokyo and Paris.

Each problem is a puzzle solved in real time, crowds reacting to every attempt, every top, every heartbreaking slip off the final hold. The standalone format elevates specialists who thrive on power and beta-reading over endurance climbing.

Morning air carries Pacific salt into the temporary arena, chalk clouds rising against blue sky as finalists eye routes that could make or break medal dreams.
CLB08.potentialContendersIntro: Janja Garnbret is the projected favorite if she qualifies; World Cup boulderers from 2027 events will round out the finalist field.

potentialContenders.name: Janja Garnbret

CLB09.blurb:
Men's Lead final crowns champions on 15-meter+ walls that test stamina, technique, and mental composure as holds shrink and arms burn. Jakob Schubert and Adam Ondra—if Ondra qualifies and competes—could bring their combined decades of world-level lead experience to the endurance showdown.

Climbers rope up knowing one slip ends the ascent; the highest hold reached determines placement. Dual qualifiers may arrive here fresh off Boulder finals two mornings prior, or specialists who saved energy for this single vertical marathon.

Afternoon light slants through the open-air structure, ocean breeze a brief respite between attempts as the crowd tracks every clipped quickdraw and rested shake-out.
CLB09.potentialContendersIntro: Jakob Schubert and Adam Ondra are projected contenders if they qualify; finalists will emerge from the July 25 semifinal.

potentialContenders.note: Tokyo combined bronze, multiple Lead world champion

potentialContenders.name: Adam Ondra
