CLB10.blurb:
Women's Lead final closes climbing's six-day run with the sport's ultimate endurance test—15-meter walls, thinning holds, and the clock ticking as arms swell and fingers lose grip. Janja Garnbret, if she navigates dual qualification and contests both Boulder and Lead, could cap the week with her third Games gold.

The highest hold reached wins; ties are broken by time to that hold. It's climbing's purest vertical marathon, crowds silent during ascents, erupting when climbers clip past the crux or slip into the void.

Morning sun warms the temporary arena one last time, Pacific horizon framing the final climbers as they chalk up and rope in, chasing history on the sport's biggest stage.
CLB10.potentialContendersIntro: Janja Garnbret is the overwhelming favorite if she qualifies; 2027 World Championship and World Cup lead specialists will round out the field.

potentialContenders.name: Janja Garnbret

CRD01.blurb:
Two races against the clock, back to back: men's and women's individual time trials over 35 kilometers each. Pure speed, no drafting, no teammates — just rider versus course and the ticking seconds.

Remco Evenepoel won gold in Paris after a late puncture nearly derailed his historic road race and time trial double. Filippo Ganna and Wout van Aert joined him on the podium. Grace Brown powered to gold in the women's race ahead of Anna Henderson and Chloé Dygert, capping a storied career.

The exact course is still TBD, but expect Los Angeles to deliver a challenging route — perhaps coastal stretches with ocean views or urban climbs through the city's hills. The time trial rewards aerodynamic precision and raw watts; riders launch solo at intervals, chasing personal bests and podium finishes.

You'll watch two finals unfold in one session: rhythmic departures from the start ramp, live splits flashing on screens, and the relentless arithmetic of who's up and who's fading. Every pedal stroke counts.
CRD01.potentialContendersIntro: Grace Brown retired after Paris, opening the women's field. Evenepoel, Ganna, van Aert, Henderson, and Dygert are all likely returning if they qualify, though rosters won't be confirmed until closer to the Games.

potentialContenders.name: Remco Evenepoel

potentialContenders.note: Silver medalist in Paris 2024; multiple-time world time trial champion

potentialContenders.name: Wout van Aert
potentialContenders.note: Bronze medalist in Paris 2024; elite all-rounder on road and cyclocross

potentialContenders.name: Anna Henderson
potentialContenders.note: Silver medalist in Paris 2024; strong time trialist with rising form

potentialContenders.name: Chloé Dygert

CRD02.blurb:
The women's road race launches from the Venice Beach Boardwalk and winds 163 kilometers through Los Angeles with nearly 3,000 meters of climbing—a punishing test of endurance and tactical savvy. Up to 90 cyclists from around the world will chase one gold medal in a mass-start format that rewards breakaway courage and late-race positioning.

Paris 2024 gold medalist Kristen Faulkner stunned the field with a daring late attack; she could return to defend alongside silver medalist Marianne Vos, a Dutch legend still competing at the highest level, and bronze medalist Lotte Kopecky of Belgium. Each nation can field up to four riders, turning the race into a chess match of team tactics and individual firepower.

The start and finish at Venice Beach brings world-class cycling to one of Southern California's most iconic settings—oceanfront energy, street performers, and a crowd lining the two-mile boardwalk. You'll watch riders roll out under the morning sun and return hours later, legs scorched, sprinting for the podium.
CRD02.potentialContendersIntro: Paris 2024 medalists Kristen Faulkner, Marianne Vos, and Lotte Kopecky could all return, alongside top-ranked riders from the UCI World Rankings who secure qualification by October 2027 or June 2028.

CRD03.blurb:
The men's road race is a grueling, all-day battle starting at the Venice Beach Boardwalk and covering 250 kilometers of Los Angeles terrain. Expect tactical fireworks as up to 90 riders jostle for position, launch breakaways, and save energy for a punishing finale—only one will claim gold.

Remco Evenepoel of Belgium won Paris 2024 in commanding fashion, outpacing France's Valentin Madouas and Christophe Laporte. Whether Evenepoel returns in 2028 remains to be seen, but the race will likely feature a mix of Tour de France stars, classics specialists, and national team tacticians.

Catch the early-morning start along the boardwalk, where riders roll past beachgoers and skaters, then return seven hours later for a finish that could come down to a mass sprint or a solo hero. The oceanfront promenade transforms into a cycling arena, packed with fans soaking in the Southern California sun.
CRD03.potentialContendersIntro: Paris 2024 medalists Remco Evenepoel, Valentin Madouas, and Christophe Laporte are potential returnees, alongside top Grand Tour contenders and world-ranked riders who qualify by mid-2028.

CSL01.blurb:
The 2028 Summer Games open with whitewater chaos as men's and women's kayak single paddlers attack the pumped rapids of OKC Whitewater Center. Each competitor navigates the course twice across three-plus hours of heats, threading through hanging gates while the clock runs — fastest combined time advances to the semifinal.

This session marks the first look at the field, with reigning world champions and Paris medalists sorting themselves against rising challengers. Expect strategic runs in the first heat, then aggressive attacks in the second as paddlers chase qualification.

The open-air venue wraps around the artificial rapids with elevated decks and patios overlooking the action, downtown Oklahoma City rising behind the course. You'll hear the roar of pumps, the snap of gate touches, and the crowd groan when a bow clips a pole.
CSL01.potentialContendersIntro: Jessica Fox of Australia, who swept kayak gold in Paris, is projected to dominate the women's field. Paris silver medalist Elena Lantsova and bronze medalist Valeria Merkacheva could challenge if they qualify.

potentialContenders.name: Jessica Fox
potentialContenders.note: Paris 2024 women's K1 gold medalist, leads world rankings

potentialContenders.name: Elena Lantsova
potentialContenders.note: Paris 2024 silver medalist, 2nd at 2025 World Championships K1

potentialContenders.name: Sideris Tasiadis
potentialContenders.note: Paris 2024 men's K1 silver medalist, 2025 World Cup champion

CSL02.blurb:
The women's kayak single crowns its champion in a session built for peak drama. Twelve semifinalists take one run each, fastest ten advancing to the medal final minutes later — then those ten paddle again for gold, silver, and bronze.

Jessica Fox has publicly vowed to defend her Paris title and enters as the favorite, but kayak slalom rewards precision over reputation. One gate touch, one missed line, and the standings flip. The semifinal provides the first filter; the final delivers the glory.

OKC's whitewater course runs fast and technical, spectators tracking paddlers from the two-story center as they carve through eddies and punch upstream gates. The Oklahoma River flows beyond, the downtown skyline framing medal ceremonies.
CSL02.potentialContendersIntro: Jessica Fox of Australia won Paris 2024 gold and remains the dominant force. Paris silver medalist Elena Lantsova and others will challenge if they advance from the semifinal.

potentialContenders.name: Jessica Fox
potentialContenders.note: Paris 2024 gold medalist, stated intent to defend 2028 title

potentialContenders.name: Elena Lantsova
potentialContenders.note: Paris 2024 silver, 2nd at 2025 Worlds K1 final

CSL03.blurb:
Men's kayak single decides its medals with the same ruthless format: semifinal culls the field to ten, then one final run determines the podium. Paris champion Giovanni de Gennaro retired after his gold, opening the door for Sideris Tasiadis and a new generation.

Tasiadis took Paris silver and won the 2025 World Cup final, positioning himself as a likely favorite if he qualifies. The slalom course punishes hesitation — paddlers must balance speed with clean gate clearances, every pole touch adding two seconds to the clock.

The venue's elevated viewing decks offer clear sightlines down the rapids as kayakers knife through turbulent water, helmets and spray visible against the whitewater. Oklahoma City's skyline looms beyond, medals awarded streamside.
CSL03.potentialContendersIntro: Paris 2024 champion Giovanni de Gennaro retired, leaving Paris silver medalist Sideris Tasiadis and other contenders to claim the open throne.

potentialContenders.name: Sideris Tasiadis
potentialContenders.note: Paris 2024 silver medalist, won 2025 World Cup final

CSL04.blurb:
Canoe single paddlers kneel in their boats and attack the OKC course with single-blade paddles, a different rhythm than kayak. Men's and women's heats run back-to-back, each competitor taking two runs to post their combined qualification time.

Jessica Fox, fresh off her kayak medals, could appear in women's C1 heats — she won both kayak and canoe gold in Paris. The canoe disciplines demand different technique, more torso rotation and bracing, paddlers working one side of the boat through hanging gates.

Expect a workmanlike morning session as athletes dial in lines and manage risk across two runs. The whitewater center's covered patios provide shade while the rapids churn below, downtown OKC visible upriver.
CSL04.potentialContendersIntro: Jessica Fox of Australia, who won Paris 2024 women's C1 gold alongside her kayak title, is projected to lead the women's field if she competes in both disciplines again.

CSL05.blurb:
Women's canoe single awards its medals through semifinal and final, the same pressure-cooker format as kayak. Jessica Fox aims to repeat her Paris double — she won both women's K1 and C1 in 2024 and has targeted 2028 for another sweep.

Paris silver medalist Tereza Fiserova finished top three at the 2025 World Championships and could challenge if she advances. One clean run in the final wins gold; one mistake drops you off the podium. Canoe paddlers kneel and use single-blade paddles, a more technical discipline than kayak.

The OKC venue's two-story center overlooks the rapids from elevated decks, spectators watching paddlers brace through upstream gates and eddy turns. Medals follow minutes after the final run, champions crowned streamside.
CSL05.potentialContendersIntro: Jessica Fox of Australia, who swept kayak and canoe gold in Paris 2024, is the favorite. Paris silver medalist Tereza Fiserova could contend if she qualifies.

potentialContenders.name: Jessica Fox
potentialContenders.note: Paris 2024 women's C1 and K1 gold medalist, targeting repeat

potentialContenders.name: Tereza Fiserova
potentialContenders.note: Paris 2024 silver medalist, top 3 at 2025 Worlds

CSL06.blurb:
Men's canoe single closes the traditional slalom disciplines with semifinal and final runs. Nicolas Gestin of France won Paris gold and leads recent rankings, stating his intent to defend in 2028 — expect him in the final if form holds.

The canoe format tests core strength and blade control, paddlers kneeling and working a single paddle through tight gate sequences. Semifinal narrows the field to ten; final run decides medals. The course at OKC runs fast, favoring aggressive lines over cautious navigation.

Spectators watch from covered patios and elevated decks as paddlers punch through standing waves and eddy out below gates, the Oklahoma River flowing beyond the competition channel. Medals awarded streamside, skyline in the background.
CSL06.potentialContendersIntro: Nicolas Gestin of France, Paris 2024 men's C1 champion, leads recent rankings and has confirmed plans to defend his title.

potentialContenders.name: Nicolas Gestin
potentialContenders.note: Paris 2024 gold medalist, stated plans to defend 2028 title

CSL07.blurb:
Kayak cross introduces full-contact chaos: four paddlers launch simultaneously down a mass-start ramp, racing through gates and a mandatory Eskimo roll zone, elbows and bows colliding. Round 1 sorts the field; repechage gives eliminated paddlers a second chance.

This is slalom stripped of time-trial calm — bump a competitor into a gate, surf their wake, block their line. Paris champions Jessica Fox and Matej Benus could appear if they qualify, Fox chasing another double after her Paris sweep. Expect crashes, flips, and aggressive jockeying.

OKC's venue wraps spectators around the start ramp and downstream gates, close enough to hear paddles clack and bodies hit water. The format is loud, unpredictable, and built for drama.
CSL07.potentialContendersIntro: Jessica Fox of Australia, Paris 2024 women's kayak cross champion, is projected to contend. Paris men's gold medalist Matej Benus placed high in 2025 World Cup races.

potentialContenders.name: Jessica Fox
potentialContenders.note: Paris 2024 women's kayak cross gold medalist

potentialContenders.name: Matej Benus
potentialContenders.note: Paris 2024 men's kayak cross gold, strong 2025 World Cup form

potentialContenders.name: Finlay Williamson
potentialContenders.note: Paris 2024 men's kayak cross silver, strong 2025-2026 results

CSL08.blurb:
Kayak cross heats continue the head-to-head brawl, advancing paddlers toward quarterfinals. Four-boat races launch off the mass-start ramp, competitors jostling through gates and the mandatory roll zone — top finishers per heat move forward, others go home.

Paris medalists who survived round 1 and repechage likely appear here, though kayak cross rewards aggression over pedigree. Expect contact, tactical blocking, and quick exits for anyone who misses a gate or flips without rolling.

The OKC course funnels paddlers through tight sequences with spectators close to the action, whitewater pumps roaring as boats collide. This is the penultimate filter before medal day.
CSL08.potentialContendersIntro: Top kayak cross paddlers, including likely Paris 2024 medalists who advanced from round 1, will battle through heats based on 2025-2026 form.

potentialContenders.name: Jessica Fox
potentialContenders.note: Paris 2024 women's kayak cross gold medalist

potentialContenders.name: Matej Benus

CSL09.blurb:
Kayak cross delivers the final whitewater medals in a knockout gauntlet: quarterfinals, semifinals, finals, all in one session. Four paddlers per race crash off the start ramp, racing and colliding through gates until only the fastest remain standing — or rolling.

Jessica Fox chases another kayak cross title after her Paris gold, though the format's chaos makes favorites vulnerable. Finlay Williamson took Paris silver and has shown strong recent form. Expect contact, flips, and dramatic four-boat sprints to the finish.

OKC's venue packs spectators around the course for the closing session, downtown skyline rising behind the podium. The pumped whitewater roars, bodies and boats tangle, and canoe slalom ends with a bang.
CSL09.potentialContendersIntro: Jessica Fox of Australia, Paris 2024 women's kayak cross champion, pursues a repeat. Paris men's silver medalist Finlay Williamson shows strong 2025-2026 form and could challenge for gold.

CSP01.blurb:
The 2028 Canoe Sprint competition opens at Long Beach's historic Marine Stadium with morning heats and quarterfinals across four events. Paddlers in kayak doubles and solo canoes race the protected flatwater course, advancing through rounds in the men's and women's K2 500m, men's C1 1000m, and women's C1 200m.

Early rounds offer an accessible entry point to the sport's precision and power. Watch crews synchronize their strokes in the K2 doubles while solo canoeists kneel and drive single-blade paddles through the 200m and 1000m distances.

Marine Stadium's shoreline seating brings spectators close to the action on calm water. The 1932-built venue carries Games history into a relaxed coastal morning, with sea breeze and unobstructed sightlines down the lane-marked course.
CSP01.potentialContendersIntro: Paris 2024 medalists and world champions are projected to headline the qualifying rounds, though 2028 rosters remain unconfirmed.

CSP02.blurb:
Four gold medals are decided as Canoe Sprint's opening events reach their finals at Marine Stadium. The morning session crowns champions in the men's and women's kayak doubles 500m and the men's solo canoe 1000m after semifinal racing narrows the fields.

The K2 500m doubles demand flawless synchronization over a blistering quarter-mile sprint. The men's C1 1000m tests endurance and technique as kneeling paddlers navigate the full kilometer with single-blade strokes, showcasing canoe sprint's most traditional discipline.

Medal races on the Long Beach flatwater deliver high-stakes drama against a coastal backdrop. Fans along the shoreline track crews stroke-for-stroke down the straightaway, with podium ceremonies following each final on the venue plaza.
CSP02.potentialContendersIntro: Paris 2024 champions and recent world titlists are likely to contest these medals, though qualification is ongoing.

CSP03.blurb:
The largest session of the Canoe Sprint program packs six events into a Thursday morning of heats and quarterfinals at Marine Stadium. Four-person kayak crews join doubles canoe teams and solo kayakers in early-round racing across men's and women's divisions, including the debuts of the men's K1 1000m and women's K1 500m.

The K4 500m showcases team boat speed with four paddlers churning in unison, while the C2 500m pairs test canoe technique over the sprint distance. Solo kayakers in the K1 events navigate their opening rounds in the program's most individual tests.

Expect a full morning of back-to-back races on the protected Long Beach course. The shoreline venue offers continuous action as crews rotate through lanes, with the coastal setting and open sightlines making every heat visible from the stands.
CSP03.potentialContendersIntro: World championship medalists and Paris 2024 stars are projected in the opening rounds, with 2028 participation pending qualification.

CSP04.blurb:
Three more gold medals are awarded as Canoe Sprint reaches its penultimate day at Marine Stadium. The women's solo kayak 500m, women's canoe doubles 500m, and men's solo kayak 1000m all crown champions after morning semifinals.

The women's K1 500m is among the sport's most prestigious races, testing individual speed and endurance. The men's K1 1000m doubles that distance for a grueling solo test, while the women's C2 500m pairs paddlers in the technical canoe discipline over a sprint.

Friday morning racing delivers three podium ceremonies on the Long Beach waterfront. Marine Stadium's intimate scale puts fans close to the finish line, where margins are often measured in fractions of a second as crews cross in tight clusters.
CSP04.potentialContendersIntro: Paris 2024 champions are expected to defend if qualified, with depth across all three events promising competitive finals.

CSP05.blurb:
Canoe Sprint concludes with a championship Saturday morning as the final four gold medals are decided at Marine Stadium. The women's ultra-short C1 200m sprint and men's C2 500m join the men's and women's K4 500m team boat spectacles in back-to-back finals.

The K4 races are pure power and synchronization as four-person crews sprint the half-kilometer in thundering unison. The women's C1 200m is over in less than a minute of explosive paddling, while the men's C2 500m showcases canoe pairs at peak speed.

The Games' flatwater racing wraps with four podium ceremonies on the Long Beach shore. Marine Stadium's historic venue and coastal atmosphere provide a fitting close to a week of sprint competition, with the final medals awarded before midday.
CSP05.potentialContendersIntro: Paris 2024 medalists and world championship crews are projected in the finals, though team boat rosters may shift before 2028.

CTR01.blurb:
The 2028 track cycling program opens with qualifying rounds for both men's and women's team sprints at Carson Velodrome. Teams of three riders hit the steeply banked 250-meter Siberian pine track in qualifying runs, posting times that will seed the afternoon medal rounds.

These are pure speed events where teams accelerate to over 50 mph on the 45-degree banking. The top eight teams in each discipline advance to first-round matchups.

The velodrome's climate-controlled environment keeps conditions ideal for fast times. Morning sessions offer an accessible entry point to track cycling's most explosive discipline.
CTR01.potentialContendersIntro: Expect defending Paris 2024 champions Netherlands in the men's event and Great Britain in the women's to return as favorites, alongside strong challenges from Australia, New Zealand, and Germany.

CTR02.blurb:
Two sets of team sprint medals will be decided in rapid succession as the top qualifiers from the morning session return for head-to-head match racing. The Dutch men and British women enter as defending champions.

Team sprint finals deliver pure velocity—three riders launching in synchronized fury around 750 meters of banked track. After the sprint medals are settled, men's team pursuit qualifying begins, with four-rider squads posting times over 4000 meters.

The afternoon session packs medal ceremonies and qualifying drama into three hours. Expect a packed velodrome and deafening noise when sprinters hit top speed on the banking.
CTR02.potentialContendersIntro: The men's sprint field likely features Netherlands defending their Paris 2024 title against Great Britain and Australia, while Great Britain's women face New Zealand and Germany.

CTR03.blurb:
A marathon morning session blends qualifying, first rounds, and repechages across four disciplines. Women's team pursuit qualifying sets the stage for medal rounds, while men's team pursuit advances to first-round matchups between the top qualifiers.

The session also introduces individual sprint competition with men's sprint qualifying—200-meter flying laps where riders clock raw speed. Women's keirin first round and repechages add tactical racing to the mix, with riders jockeying behind a motorized pacer.

Over three hours, the velodrome showcases track cycling's range from endurance teamwork to individual explosive power. The United States women's pursuit squad won gold at Paris 2024 and could contend on home boards.
CTR03.potentialContendersIntro: The women's pursuit features defending Paris 2024 champions United States, while Harrie Lavreysen of Netherlands and Matthew Richardson of Australia could lead the men's sprint qualifying if they compete.

CTR04.blurb:
The men's team pursuit final headlines this afternoon session, with Australia defending the gold they won at Paris 2024 against Great Britain and other top qualifiers. Four riders per team cover 4000 meters in tight formation, rotating lead duties to maintain devastating pace.

The session also features men's sprint knockout rounds and women's keirin quarterfinals. Sprint racing brings tactical cat-and-mouse at walking pace before explosive surges, while keirin riders navigate bunch racing behind a motorized pacer.

Three hours of high-stakes racing across multiple disciplines. The velodrome's intimate sightlines put spectators close to the action as pursuit teams roar past at 40 mph.
CTR04.potentialContendersIntro: Australia's men's pursuit team defends Paris 2024 gold, while Ellesse Andrews of New Zealand could advance through the women's keirin rounds if she competes.

CTR05.blurb:
Five hours of finals and all-day competition across four disciplines make this the most packed session on the track cycling schedule. The women's team pursuit final crowns a champion, with the United States defending gold won at Paris 2024. The women's keirin final delivers another medal race.

Between finals, the men's omnium runs all four rounds—scratch race, tempo race, elimination race, and points race—to determine an all-around champion. Men's sprint knockout rounds continue throughout.

Expect a full house and sustained energy as medals pile up. The omnium's marathon format adds endurance drama to a day dominated by sprint explosiveness.
CTR05.potentialContendersIntro: The women's pursuit features defending champions United States, while Ellesse Andrews of New Zealand and Harrie Lavreysen of Netherlands could contend for keirin and sprint medals respectively if they compete.

CTR06.blurb:
The men's sprint semifinals narrow the field to the final four as medalists vie for championship matchups. Harrie Lavreysen of Netherlands won gold at Paris 2024 and could be the rider to beat if he competes, facing challenges from Australia and Great Britain.

Meanwhile, the women's sprint competition begins with qualifying and early knockout rounds. Sprint racing combines chess-match tactics with raw power, as riders track-stand nearly motionless before launching 50-mph attacks.

A compact two-hour session showcasing track cycling's most tactical discipline. The velodrome's 2,450 seats offer clear views of every feint and surge on the banking.
CTR06.potentialContendersIntro: Harrie Lavreysen of Netherlands likely leads the men's semifinal field based on his Paris 2024 gold, with Matthew Richardson of Australia and Jack Carlin of Great Britain as projected contenders.

CTR07.blurb:
The men's sprint final delivers one of track cycling's signature showdowns as the world's fastest riders face off in a best-of-three series. Tactical mind games and explosive power decide gold over three laps of high-speed chess.

The women's madison final runs concurrently—a chaotic 120-lap points race where two-rider teams execute hand-sling exchanges while sprinting for points every 10 laps. Italy won gold at Paris 2024 against Great Britain and Netherlands.

Two hours, two gold medals, two utterly different styles of racing. The madison's controlled mayhem contrasts sharply with the sprint's one-on-one intensity.
CTR07.potentialContendersIntro: Harrie Lavreysen of Netherlands could defend his Paris 2024 sprint title if he competes, while Italy's madison duo likely returns to defend their gold.

CTR08.blurb:
The men's madison final anchors this session—200 laps of tactical chaos where Portugal defends the gold they won at Paris 2024. Teams of two execute hand-sling exchanges while racing for sprint points, creating sustained drama over an hour of racing.

Women's sprint knockout rounds advance toward medal matches, while the men's keirin begins first-round heats. Keirin racing packs riders behind a motorized pacer that accelerates to 50 kph before pulling off, triggering a furious sprint to the line.

Three disciplines across three hours showcase the range of track racing from endurance strategy to pure velocity. Carson Velodrome's climate control keeps conditions consistent for a long afternoon of racing.

CTR09.blurb:
The 2028 track cycling program concludes with a championship morning: women's sprint and men's keirin finals decide gold, while the women's omnium runs all four rounds to crown an all-around champion.

Ellesse Andrews of New Zealand won both sprint and keirin at Paris 2024—if she competes, she could chase double gold again. Harrie Lavreysen of Netherlands swept sprint and keirin on the men's side and enters as the projected favorite.

Four hours of racing wrap the velodrome program with maximum stakes. The omnium's grueling four-race format adds endurance to a morning dominated by sprint power, giving spectators the full spectrum of track cycling at its highest level.
CTR09.potentialContendersIntro: Ellesse Andrews of New Zealand and Harrie Lavreysen of Netherlands could both chase sprint-keirin doubles if they compete, mirroring their Paris 2024 dominance.

DIV01.blurb:
The 2028 Games diving program opens with the first cut in Women's 3m Springboard, where nearly 30 athletes compete for 18 semifinal spots. Five dives separate contenders from also-rans in a format that rewards consistency and nerves—miss one execution and the door closes.

China swept all eight golds in Paris, but Chinese Taipei's silver medalist and Australia's bronze winner proved the podium isn't a lock. Expect nations across Asia, Europe, and the Americas fielding springboard specialists looking to upset the hierarchy.

Rose Bowl Aquatics Center in Pasadena offers open-air poolside viewing steps from the storied stadium. Morning sun lights the diving well as athletes launch from the 3m board, twisting and somersaulting above crystal water before splashdown.
DIV01.potentialContendersIntro: China is projected to field top talent following its Paris 2024 sweep, while Chinese Taipei's silver medalist and Australia's bronze winner could return if they qualify.

DIV02.blurb:
The top 18 from Sunday's preliminary return for six dives that will cut the field in half. One misstep—an under-rotated reverse or a splash on entry—can cost a finalist berth.

China dominated springboard in Paris, taking gold and silver, but Australia's Keeney proved bronze is within reach for nations outside the traditional powerhouse. Expect refinements in technique as athletes dial in degree of difficulty against execution scores.

Mid-morning at the open-air Rose Bowl Aquatics Center means cooler air and sharp shadows across the pool. Spectators sit poolside, close enough to hear the diver's breath before launch and the crowd's collective gasp on a near-perfect entry.
DIV02.potentialContendersIntro: Paris 2024 medalists Chen Simin of China and Maddison Keeney of Australia are likely to advance if they compete, alongside other top-12 finishers from the preliminary.

Afternoon light streams across the Pasadena facility as the crowd leans forward for each approach. Between dives, scoreboards update and nations wave flags, the open-air bowl amplifying every cheer and groan.

The men take the boards for the first time, launching five dives apiece to narrow a deep field to 18 semifinalists. Springboard diving rewards explosive power off the board and pinpoint body control through multiple rotations.

China's Wang Zongyuan defended his Paris title with training partner Xie Siyi taking silver—a 1-2 finish that set the bar. Nations from Europe to Latin America field contenders capable of clean dive lists, making the preliminary round a proving ground for consistency under pressure.

Morning in Pasadena means crisp air and a crowd still settling in as divers test the boards. The open-air Rose Bowl Aquatics Center offers unobstructed sightlines from every seat, the diving well framed by dry-land facilities and parkland beyond.

Wang Zongyuan won Paris 2024 and could return to defend if he qualifies. China's depth in springboard makes them favorites, but the format rewards nerve and execution over reputation. One splash too many and the podium slips away.

China's Quan Hongchan took Paris gold with training partner Chen Yuxi claiming silver, a 1-2 finish that underscored their platform dominance. Nations worldwide send athletes capable of executing armstands, reverse dives, and twisting somersaults from 33 feet above the water.

Morning at the open-air Rose Bowl Aquatics Center. Spectators crane their necks to track divers ascending the tower ladder, then hold their breath through the two-second freefall. Pasadena's parkland setting and poolside seating make every dive feel intimate despite the dizzying height.

Synchronized diving pairs return after individual events conclude. Two athletes, one board, five dives executed in perfect unison—judges score both synchronization and individual technique.

China's Chang Yani and Chen Yuxi won Paris gold, showcasing the precision training that underpins their synchro program. Pairs must match takeoff timing, body position through rotations, and water entry within fractions of a second. One partner's mistake drags down both scores.

Late morning at the Rose Bowl Aquatics Center. Spectators watch two divers approach the 3m board side by side, launch in tandem, then twist through matching somersaults before dual splashdown. The open-air venue's intimate scale makes synchronization errors—and flawless executions—immediately obvious.

DIV14.blurb:
The men's synchro springboard final asks pairs to execute five dives in perfect tandem from the 3m board. Judges score both individual technique and synchronization—takeoff, flight, rotation, and entry must match within milliseconds.

Wang Zongyuan and Xie Siyi won Paris gold for China, combining explosive power with pinpoint timing. Synchro diving demands trust and thousands of hours of training; partners must anticipate each other's movements through complex rotations at high speed.

Afternoon in Pasadena. Two divers stand side by side on the board, nod, then launch in unison—the crowd holding its breath through matching somersaults before dual splashdown. Rose Bowl Aquatics Center's open-air setting and poolside viewing put spectators close enough to see synchronized precision or tiny mismatches.
DIV14.potentialContendersIntro: China's Wang Zongyuan and Xie Siyi won gold at Paris 2024 and are projected to contend if they qualify as a pair for 2028.

potentialContenders.name: Wang Zongyuan / Xie Siyi
potentialContenders.note: Gold medalists in Men's Synchronized 3m Springboard at Paris 2024

DIV15.blurb:
The diving program closes with synchronized platform, where pairs execute five dives from 33 feet in perfect unison. Judges score individual technique and synchronization—one partner's mistimed rotation or splashy entry costs both athletes.

China's Chen Yuxi and Quan Hongchan won Paris gold, showcasing the training depth that makes their synchro program nearly unbeatable. Pairs must match every phase of flight—takeoff angle, body position through rotations, entry timing—while managing the physics of a 33-foot freefall.

Afternoon at Rose Bowl Aquatics Center. Two divers climb the tower, stand side by side at the edge, then launch in tandem—rotating through matching armstands or reverse dives before dual splashdown. The open-air venue and Pasadena's summer light frame the final act of the diving program, the crowd on its feet for the last medal ceremony.
DIV15.potentialContendersIntro: Chen Yuxi and Quan Hongchan of China won gold in Women's Synchronized 10m Platform at Paris 2024 and are expected to contend if they qualify as a pair.

potentialContenders.name: Chen Yuxi / Quan Hongchan
potentialContenders.note: Gold medalists in Women's Synchronized 10m Platform at Paris 2024

DIV16.blurb:
The diving competition closes with the men's synchronized 10m platform final, where pairs leap from 33 feet into coordinated perfection. Eight duos execute six dives in tandem—judged on technical precision, synchronization, and entry—with the cumulative score determining medals.

China dominated this event in Paris 2024 and enters as the projected favorite, though Great Britain's silver medalists return with momentum. The synchro format amplifies stakes: partners must match rotation speed, body position, and water entry within fractions of a second.

Rose Bowl Aquatics Center's open-air diving well offers unobstructed sightlines from deck level, where you'll hear the splash and feel the crowd tension as each pair climbs the ladder for their final attempt.
DIV16.potentialContendersIntro: China won gold in Paris 2024 and remains the benchmark, while Great Britain's Tom Daley and Noah Williams claimed silver and could return if both compete.

potentialContenders.name: China
potentialContenders.note: Paris 2024 gold medalists in men's synchronized 10m platform

potentialContenders.name: Tom Daley
potentialContenders.note: Paris 2024 silver medalist; five-time Games competitor

EQU01.blurb:
Eventing opens with its most elegant phase as riders and horses perform prescribed dressage tests at Santa Anita Park. Team and individual competition begins together — each combination's score carries through the grueling three-day event.

The dressage phase tests harmony and precision through a series of movements judged for accuracy and artistry. Clean rounds here build crucial cushions before cross-country's high-speed drama.

Morning sunlight filters across the historic Art Deco grandstand as horses warm up against the San Gabriel Mountains backdrop. Early-session crowds watch technical mastery unfold in the cool morning air.
EQU01.potentialContendersIntro: Germany and USA could field strong eventing teams based on their Paris 2024 podium finishes, though 2028 rosters remain unconfirmed.

potentialContenders.name: Michael Jung
potentialContenders.note: Won individual eventing gold and team silver at Paris 2024

EQU02.blurb:
Dressage continues into evening as the remaining eventing pairs complete their opening tests. Afternoon heat gives way to cooler conditions for horses and riders navigating the technical demands.

Late starters carry the weight of knowing morning scores — every point matters across eventing's three phases. Judges scrutinize each transition, halt, and extended trot for flaws that could prove costly on the final leaderboard.

The setting sun casts long shadows across the infield as spectators fill picnic tables under the trees. Santa Anita's grandstand lights up for the evening session, mountain silhouettes darkening behind the arena.
EQU02.potentialContendersIntro: Top eventing nations like Germany and USA are projected to compete based on recent results, though specific athlete entries await qualification.

potentialContenders.name: Michael Jung
potentialContenders.note: Paris 2024 individual gold medalist, likely contender if competing

potentialContenders.name: USA Eventing Team
potentialContenders.note: Paris 2024 bronze medalists could return strong roster

EQU03.blurb:
The final dressage session determines starting positions before Monday's cross-country gauntlet. Riders aim for clean tests knowing terrain challenges loom ahead.

Countries chasing team medals calculate scores as the last combinations finish — dressage leaders carry slim advantages into the speed phase. Individual contenders bank points while horse welfare checks ensure fitness for cross-country's demands.

Sunday morning brings families to the infield park where kids watch from picnic areas. The 1934 grandstand frames riders in classic silhouette against mountain peaks.
EQU03.potentialContendersIntro: Germany and USA remain likely team medal contenders given their Paris 2024 performances, though 2028 lineups are unconfirmed.

potentialContenders.name: Germany Eventing Team
potentialContenders.note: Team silver at Paris 2024, led by gold medalist Michael Jung

EQU04.blurb:
Cross-country explodes onto Santa Anita's specially built course as eventing shifts from artistry to adrenaline. Riders gallop through obstacles up to 1.20m high at speeds approaching 30 mph — one mistake can upend medal hopes.

This phase separates contenders through sheer courage and horse fitness across multiple kilometers of jumps, ditches, and water complexes. Dressage leaders defend positions while aggressive pursuers gamble on fast times and clean rounds.

Crowds line the course perimeter as horses thunder past, hooves spraying turf. The infield becomes a festival atmosphere with giant screens tracking riders navigating distant obstacles against mountain backdrops.
EQU04.potentialContendersIntro: Paris 2024 medalists from Germany and USA could lead the field, though cross-country's unpredictability makes any prediction speculative.

potentialContenders.name: Michael Jung
potentialContenders.note: Paris 2024 individual gold medalist, if competing

EQU05.blurb:
Eventing crowns its champions through a climactic jumping finale that rewards consistency across all three phases. After dressage precision and cross-country endurance, riders face a technical show jumping course where rails down cost precious penalties.

Team and individual medals hang on these final rounds — a single knocked fence can shuffle the podium. Horses that survived cross-country's demands must summon fresh legs for tight turns and combination fences.

Evening light bathes the arena as medal contenders enter. Grandstand crowds roar at clean rounds while scoreboards flash real-time standings, every rail rattling changing fortunes under the San Gabriels.
EQU05.potentialContendersIntro: Michael Jung and the German team are projected favorites based on Paris 2024 dominance, with USA defending team bronze.

EQU06.blurb:
Dressage's Grand Prix phase begins as the world's most refined equestrian athletes perform tests of supreme difficulty. These qualifiers determine both team finals and individual freestyle spots — every half-pass and pirouette under scrutiny.

Judges score movements for rhythm, suppleness, and invisible aids as horse and rider move in balletic unison. Germany enters as defending team champion from Paris while individual contenders lay groundwork for freestyle medal runs.

Santa Anita's enclosed dressage arena focuses attention on subtle artistry. Morning sessions draw purists who appreciate collected canters and extended trots against the grandstand's Art Deco lines.
EQU06.potentialContendersIntro: Germany's Jessica von Bredow-Werndl won individual gold at Paris 2024 and could return, though 2028 entries remain unconfirmed.

potentialContenders.name: Jessica von Bredow-Werndl
potentialContenders.note: Paris 2024 individual dressage gold medalist, if competing

EQU07.blurb:
Grand Prix qualifying continues as afternoon sessions bring remaining dressage pairs into Santa Anita's arena. Team medal calculations begin taking shape alongside individual leaderboards.

The technical demands never relent — piaffe, passage, and flying changes executed with metronomic precision. Countries stacking strong performances across three team riders build medal positions for Saturday's special.

Late afternoon crowds settle into shaded grandstand seats as temperatures moderate. The infield picnic area fills with fans debating scores, mountain breezes carrying across the historic racetrack grounds.
EQU07.potentialContendersIntro: Defending champions Germany and medalists Netherlands and USA could field competitive teams, though specific 2028 rosters await announcement.

potentialContenders.name: Jessica von Bredow-Werndl
potentialContenders.note: Individual gold medalist from Paris 2024, likely contender

EQU08.blurb:
The second day of Grand Prix qualifiers separates medal contenders from also-rans as technical perfection becomes mandatory. Individual hopefuls chase top-15 finishes for Monday's freestyle while teams cement final positions.

Each test unfolds under five judges' scrutiny — scores averaging above 70% signal elite performance. Horses demonstrate years of training in seamless transitions between collected and extended gaits.

Morning sessions catch Santa Anita in crisp light, San Gabriel peaks sharp against blue sky. The grandstand's vintage architecture provides elegant backdrop for dressage's timeless ritual.
EQU08.potentialContendersIntro: Germany's dominance at Paris 2024 suggests strong medal chances, with Jessica von Bredow-Werndl a projected individual favorite if she competes.

EQU09.blurb:
Grand Prix qualifying concludes as final combinations complete tests that determine Saturday's team final field. The afternoon session caps two days of technical brilliance — leaderboards now set for medal rounds.

Countries outside traditional powers hunt upsets while favorites bank solid scores. Individual qualifiers lock in freestyle spots, knowing Monday's musical tests offer final medal opportunities.

The session carries anticipatory energy as standings solidify. Spectators fill the infield park's picnic areas, scoreboards updating in real-time against the grandstand's sweeping facade.
EQU09.potentialContendersIntro: Germany, Netherlands, and USA all earned Paris 2024 team medals and could contend again, though rosters remain unannounced for 2028.

potentialContenders.name: Jessica von Bredow-Werndl
potentialContenders.note: Individual gold at Paris 2024, if competing

EQU10.blurb:
Team dressage medals arrive through the Grand Prix Special as qualified nations compete in the shortened, more difficult test. Germany defends Paris 2024 gold against hungry challengers — fractions of percentage points separate podium spots.

The special demands extreme collection and precision across demanding movements. Three riders per team, best scores counting — one mistake can cost a nation years of preparation.

Saturday morning brings serious crowds to Santa Anita's grandstand, fans filling 26,000 seats for medal-deciding performances. The arena hushes during tests, erupts after final salutes.
EQU10.potentialContendersIntro: Germany enters as defending champion with Netherlands and USA chasing medals based on Paris 2024 results.

EQU11.blurb:
The final session of team dressage competition completes medal distribution as remaining nations perform their Grand Prix Specials. Afternoon light illuminates closing rounds that could shuffle standings.

Teams calculate scores in real-time, knowing late clean rides can vault positions. The shortened test format magnifies every error — extended trots must reach, piaffes must hover perfectly in place.

Evening atmosphere builds at Santa Anita as medals loom. The grandstand fills for anticipated podium shake-ups, mountain shadows lengthening across the infield where families gather around screens.
EQU11.potentialContendersIntro: Germany, Netherlands, and USA competed for team medals at Paris 2024 and could return strong lineups, though 2028 entries are unconfirmed.

EQU12.blurb:
Dressage's individual showpiece combines technical mastery with artistic flair as top-15 qualifiers perform freestyle tests to music. Riders choreograph movements to soundtracks — Mozart, Beyoncé, film scores — turning precision into performance art.

The freestyle allows creative interpretation within rules, rewarding both difficulty and harmony. Jessica von Bredow-Werndl could defend Paris 2024 gold if competing — extended trots synced to crescendos, pirouettes on downbeats.

Monday morning brings dressage devotees who appreciate the discipline's pinnacle. Santa Anita's arena becomes concert hall, judges scoring artistry against the San Gabriels' natural grandeur.
EQU12.potentialContendersIntro: Jessica von Bredow-Werndl won Paris 2024 individual gold and could contend again, though her 2028 participation is unconfirmed.

potentialContenders.name: Jessica von Bredow-Werndl
potentialContenders.note: Paris 2024 individual dressage gold medalist, if competing

EQU13.blurb:
Jumping's team qualifier sorts contenders through fast-paced courses testing precision over obstacles up to 1.6m. Clean rounds and quick times determine which nations advance to Wednesday's medal final.

The qualifier's speed-and-accuracy format penalizes both knocked rails and slow times. Teams stack three strong riders hunting zero-fault performances — one bad round can eliminate medal chances before the final.

Tuesday morning sees jumping's explosive energy arrive at Santa Anita. Horses clear spreads and verticals in quick succession, crowds gasping at tight turns near the grandstand's edge.
EQU13.potentialContendersIntro: USA won team jumping gold at Paris 2024 with Ireland taking silver, making both projected favorites if they qualify for 2028.

EQU14.blurb:
Team jumping medals ride on two rounds of high-stakes obstacle courses as qualified nations battle for podium spots. USA defends Paris 2024 gold against Ireland and other challengers — each rail down adds four penalties to team totals.

The final demands nerves of steel as horses navigate technical combinations and water jumps. Three riders per team, cumulative scores deciding medals — anchors carry enormous pressure on closing rounds.

Wednesday morning's medal session packs the grandstand with flag-waving supporters. The San Gabriel backdrop frames jumps as horses soar, every clean round met with roars echoing across the historic venue.
EQU14.potentialContendersIntro: USA enters as defending champion with Ireland likely challenging based on Paris 2024's gold-silver finish.

EQU15.blurb:
Individual jumping's qualifier determines Monday's medal final field through two grueling rounds over technical courses. Riders chase top-30 spots with rail-free rounds and aggressive times — hesitation costs advancement.

The morning marathon tests horse stamina across hours of competition. Christian Ahlmann won Paris 2024 individual gold and could return, though qualification remains uncertain — young challengers hunt upset chances.

Friday crowds fill Santa Anita knowing these rounds separate contenders from spectators. The infield becomes a gallery as horses tackle spreads and oxers, San Gabriel peaks presiding over equestrian drama's penultimate chapter.
EQU15.potentialContendersIntro: Christian Ahlmann of Germany won individual jumping gold at Paris 2024 and could compete again, though 2028 qualifiers remain unannounced.

potentialContenders.name: Christian Ahlmann
potentialContenders.note: Paris 2024 individual jumping gold medalist, if competing

EQU16.blurb:
The final equestrian medal event of the 2028 Games crowns the world's best show jumper against the San Gabriel Mountains. Thirty rider-horse partnerships navigate a demanding course of obstacles, with every rail down adding four faults to their score.

This is pure precision under pressure: clear rounds separate medalists from the rest, and a jump-off decides ties. The format rewards both technical mastery and the invisible communication between rider and horse.

Santa Anita's grandstand and infield offer sightlines across the entire course. Morning start means crisp air and sharp shadows as competitors chase gold in the Games' closing days.
EQU16.potentialContendersIntro: The field will include the top 30 qualifiers from earlier rounds. Scott Brash of Great Britain, Paris 2024 silver medalist, could return if he qualifies.

FBL01.blurb:
The men's tournament kicks off in New York as 12 nations begin their pursuit of gold. Group stage matches use a round-robin format, setting the stage for knockout drama to come.

Spain enters as defending champion after their Paris 2024 triumph, while France and Morocco—silver and bronze medalists respectively—could return to challenge. The 12-team field represents a more compact format than past editions.

Etihad Park sits 40 feet below street level in Queens, trapping crowd noise under its canopy while keeping every seat within close range of the pitch. The atmosphere builds from the first whistle in soccer's newest purpose-built home.
FBL01.potentialContendersIntro: The field could feature Paris 2024 medalists Spain, France, and Morocco, though final rosters won't be confirmed until qualification concludes.

FBL02.blurb:
More opening-day action unfolds at Etihad Park as another pair of group stage opponents take the field. These early matches determine position heading into the knockout rounds that follow.

The 12-team bracket creates high stakes even in preliminary play—every result shapes the path to medal contention. Nations that qualified through continental championships arrive battle-tested.

The stadium's 3,400 safe-standing positions and rooftop supporters bar give fans layered vantage points. Below-grade seating creates an enclosed bowl effect, amplifying every chance and near-miss.

FBL03.blurb:
Columbus hosts group stage soccer as the men's tournament spreads across six U.S. cities. The round-robin format gives every team multiple chances to prove themselves before the knockouts.

Spain's Paris 2024 gold-medal run set a new standard, but the compressed 12-team field means any slip-up carries consequences. France and Morocco both return as recent medalists if they qualify again.

Scotts Miracle-Gro Field's full canopy shelters all 20,371 seats while leaving the pitch open to the Ohio sky. The heated hybrid grass surface and downtown setting make this an intimate venue for world-class competition.
FBL03.potentialContendersIntro: Paris 2024 podium finishers Spain, France, and Morocco are projected contenders if they secure qualification.

FBL04.blurb:
Another matchup in Columbus continues the distributed opening round across the United States. Six cities host group stage matches, bringing the tournament to regional audiences before the finals converge on Los Angeles.

The men's field features nations that advanced through FIFA-approved continental qualification. Early-round positioning matters—the knockout bracket rewards group winners with favorable draws.

Inside Scotts Miracle-Gro Field, steep-raked seating puts every spectator close to the action. The 40,000-square-foot outdoor plaza becomes a pregame gathering point, while the canopy overhead keeps the weather at bay without closing off the sky.

FBL05.blurb:
Nashville's Geodis Park—venue of the 2023 League's Cup final featuring Lionel Messi—now welcomes the world stage. Group stage play determines which nations advance to the knockout rounds and which go home early.

Defending champion Spain, along with Paris 2024 runners-up France and bronze medalists Morocco, are among the expected qualifiers. The 12-team tournament creates a tighter field than previous editions, raising the stakes for every result.

The 360-degree canopy amplifies crowd noise across 30,109 seats, while the natural grass pitch and exposed steel-and-timber design create a modern yet grounded atmosphere. Multiple entry points ease access, including a dedicated supporters gate on the north side.
FBL05.potentialContendersIntro: Paris 2024 medalists Spain, France, and Morocco could all appear if they navigate qualification.

FBL06.blurb:
St. Louis enters the tournament rotation as Energizer Park hosts opening-round matches. The group stage unfolds across the country, with the men's gold medal match set for July 28 at the Rose Bowl.

Spain claimed gold at Paris 2024, while France and Morocco earned silver and bronze. Those three nations are projected to qualify again, though final rosters remain to be determined through continental play.

The stadium sits 40 feet below street level, creating a natural amphitheater with seating on two tiers. A clear plastic awning protects from weather while trapping sound, and every seat sits within 120 feet of the pitch for close-range views.
FBL06.potentialContendersIntro: Spain, France, and Morocco—the Paris 2024 podium finishers—are likely to return if they secure their spots.

FBL07.blurb:
The women's tournament begins in Nashville with 16 teams vying for gold—a larger field than the men's 12. Group stage play spreads across six cities before knockout rounds narrow the field.

The United States won gold at Paris 2024, defeating Brazil in the final while Germany took bronze. All three nations are expected to return, with the U.S. potentially playing on home soil for the first time in Games history.

Geodis Park's 360-degree canopy and steep seating create an intimate, high-energy environment. The natural grass surface and 65-foot shared concourse give fans room to roam, while the exposed timber and steel design reflects Nashville's blend of tradition and modernity.
FBL07.potentialContendersIntro: The U.S., Brazil, and Germany—Paris 2024's gold, silver, and bronze medalists—are projected to qualify, with the Americans competing at home.

FBL08.blurb:
St. Louis opens its doors to the women's tournament as group stage matches begin. The 16-team field uses round-robin play to sort contenders before the knockouts arrive.

The U.S. enters as defending champion after defeating Brazil in the Paris 2024 final, with Germany claiming bronze. If all three qualify, this could mark a rematch on American soil with the U.S. as hosts.

Energizer Park's below-street-level design creates a sunken bowl that keeps every seat within 120 feet of the action. The clear awning overhead traps crowd noise while protecting from the elements, and two tiers of seating wrap the pitch in close proximity.
FBL08.potentialContendersIntro: The U.S., Brazil, and Germany are expected contenders, with the Americans competing at home for the first time in a Summer Games women's soccer tournament.

FBL09.blurb:
Columbus joins the women's tournament rotation as 16 nations begin group stage play. The tournament prioritizes gender parity, fielding more women's teams than men's for the first time in Games history.

Defending champion United States, along with Paris 2024 finalists Brazil and bronze medalists Germany, are projected to qualify. The Americans could face a partisan home crowd if drawn into this venue.

Scotts Miracle-Gro Field's full canopy covers all seats while the pitch remains open to the sky. The heated hybrid grass and downtown Arena District location create an ideal setting for high-level soccer, with 30 suites and 1,900 club seats ringing the action.
FBL09.potentialContendersIntro: The U.S., Brazil, and Germany—the Paris 2024 medalists—are among the expected qualifiers for the 16-team field.

FBL10.blurb:
New York City's first soccer-specific stadium hosts the women's group stage as 16 teams begin their campaigns. The tournament schedule has the women starting before the men, with the gold medal match set for July 29 at the Rose Bowl.

The United States won gold at Paris 2024, while Brazil took silver and Germany bronze. All three are likely to return, with the U.S. potentially drawing massive home support in Queens.

Etihad Park features 3,400 safe-standing positions, a rooftop supporters bar, and a 40,000-square-foot food hall showcasing vendors from across the city's five boroughs. The modern design and intense atmosphere make this a showcase venue for the women's game.
FBL10.potentialContendersIntro: Paris 2024 medalists United States, Brazil, and Germany are expected to qualify, with the U.S. competing on home soil.

FBL11.blurb:
San Diego enters the tournament as Snapdragon Stadium welcomes the women's group stage. Sixteen nations compete in round-robin play, with knockout rounds to follow.

The United States claimed gold at Paris 2024, defeating Brazil in the final while Germany took bronze. If all three qualify, the Americans would compete on home soil for the first time in a Summer Games women's soccer tournament.

Snapdragon sits on the former Qualcomm Stadium site, now transformed into a modern open-air venue with landscaped concourses and a 5,000-seat student section. The lower deck encloses the field while upper decks on both sides create sightlines across Mission Valley.
FBL11.potentialContendersIntro: The U.S., Brazil, and Germany—the Paris 2024 podium finishers—are projected to return, with the U.S. as hosts.

FBL12.blurb:
More group stage action unfolds in San Diego as the women's tournament continues across the United States. The 16-team field represents the largest women's bracket in Games history, reflecting growing global investment in the sport.

Defending champion United States, along with Brazil and Germany, could all appear in this preliminary round. Early positioning in group play determines knockout matchups and potential medal paths.

Snapdragon Stadium's 35,000 capacity makes it one of the larger venues in the rotation. Safe-standing sections, loge boxes, and social decks with field views offer varied spectator experiences, while cashless concessions and extensive catering keep the crowds moving.

San José brings the women's tournament to Northern California as PayPal Park hosts group stage matches. The 16-team field competes in round-robin play before advancing to knockout rounds.

PayPal Park features the steepest-raked seating in MLS for close-up views, plus North America's largest outdoor bar behind one goal. A 2-acre fan zone and field-level suites create varied atmospheres, while the open-air design and canopy roof balance weather protection with outdoor energy.
FBL13.potentialContendersIntro: The U.S., Brazil, and Germany—Paris 2024's medalists—are likely to appear, with the U.S. competing at home.

FBL14.blurb:
Another women's group stage matchup takes the field in San José as preliminary play continues. These early rounds set the knockout bracket and determine which nations advance to medal contention.

The 16-team format creates more opportunities than the men's 12-team field, reflecting the tournament's commitment to gender parity. Nations qualified through continental championships arrive with momentum and regional pride.

PayPal Park's 18,000-seat intimacy puts spectators close to the action. The steep seating rake, field-level suites, and massive outdoor bar create varied vantage points, while the home of both the Earthquakes and Bay FC brings established soccer culture to the Games.

FBL15.blurb:
Group stage play continues in Queens as the men's tournament progresses toward knockout rounds. Nations compete in round-robin matches with advancement on the line.

Spain won gold at Paris 2024, while France and Morocco claimed silver and bronze. If all three qualify for the 12-team field, they could meet again on U.S. soil in pursuit of another podium finish.

Etihad Park's below-street-level pitch and surrounding seating create an enclosed atmosphere despite the open sky. FBL15.potentialContendersIntro: Spain, France, and Morocco—the Paris 2024 medalists—are expected to return if they navigate qualification.

FBL16.blurb:
The men's tournament kicks off in Queens as group stage action begins across the United States. Twelve nations—likely including reigning champions Spain, runners-up France, and bronze medalists Morocco from Paris 2024—compete in round-robin play, with the top finishers advancing to knockout rounds.

Etihad Park offers New York City's first purpose-built soccer venue, just unveiled in 2027. The compact 25,000-seat stadium puts every fan close to the action, with 3,400 safe standing positions and a rooftop supporters bar creating an intense, European-style atmosphere.

This is your accessible entry point to the tournament—early group matches before knockout drama, in a brand-new stadium that captures the city's soccer-mad energy.

FBL17.blurb:
Group stage play opens in Columbus as twelve nations begin their quest for medals. The compact 12-team format means every match matters—three group games determine who advances to the knockout rounds and who goes home.

Scotts Miracle-Gro Field's full canopy shields all 20,371 seats from the elements while leaving the pitch open to sky. The heated hybrid grass surface and steep sightlines put you on top of the action, with downtown Columbus just steps away.

Early tournament soccer offers a chance to see future stars before the stakes reach fever pitch. The atmosphere builds match by match as fans pack this intimate downtown venue.

Columbus hosts a second group stage match as the men's tournament unfolds across six American cities. With only twelve spots in the field, every team here has earned its place through regional qualification—no easy opponents, no throwaway games.

The stadium's architecture creates a cauldron: 20,000-plus fans under a protective canopy, weather out, noise in. The 40,000-square-foot plaza outside buzzes pregame with food and fans from competing nations.

Group stage soccer rewards the tactical chess match as much as individual brilliance. Watch teams probe for weaknesses, coaches adjust on the fly, and underdogs hunt upsets that reshape the knockout bracket.
FBL18.potentialContendersIntro: Rosters remain speculative until qualification closes, but Paris 2024 form suggests Spain, France, and Morocco as favorites. Traditional powers like Argentina and Brazil could also feature.

St. Louis welcomes the 2028 Games with group stage soccer in a venue built 40 feet below street level. The sunken-bowl design and transparent awning trap crowd noise while keeping sightlines intimate—no seat farther than 120 feet from the pitch.

Energizer Park debuted in 2022 as the home of St. Louis City SC and has already hosted U.S. national team friendlies. For the Games, expect full-throated support as Midwest fans pack the 22,000-seat stadium.

Group stage openers set the tone for each nation's tournament. A strong start builds momentum; a stumble means climbing out of a hole with only two matches left.
FBL19.potentialContendersIntro: Spain enters as defending champion from Paris 2024. France and Morocco, the Paris silver and bronze medalists, are projected qualifiers if they navigate European and African competitions.

FBL20.blurb:
Nashville's Geodis Park—which set the MLS attendance record at its 2022 debut—hosts opening-day group stage soccer. The 360-degree canopy amplifies every chant, every groan, every roar as twelve nations begin tournament play.

The natural grass surface and 30,000-seat capacity make this the largest soccer-specific venue in the group stage rotation. Exposed steel and mass timber design elements give the stadium a distinct Music City feel, with multiple concourse entry points spreading crowds efficiently.

Early group matches offer a chance to see world-class talent before tickets become scarce in knockout rounds. The math is simple: win or draw keeps dreams alive, lose and you're chasing from behind.

The women's tournament opens across the United States with group stage play in Nashville. Sixteen nations compete in round-robin format, with defending champions United States likely headlining a field that could include Paris 2024 runners-up Brazil and bronze medalists Germany.

Gedois Park's 30,000-seat capacity and covered stands create an amphitheater for soccer. The venue hosted the 2023 League's Cup final featuring Lionel Messi and will bring that same intensity to the women's game.

Women's soccer has grown exponentially since the U.S. last hosted in 1996. Expect technical brilliance, tactical sophistication, and a fanbase that fills stadiums from coast to coast.
FBL21.potentialContendersIntro: The United States enters as defending champion from Paris 2024. Brazil and Germany, the Paris silver and bronze medalists, are projected to qualify alongside European powers Spain and England.

FBL22.blurb:
St. Louis opens its doors to women's group stage soccer as sixteen nations begin the tournament. The U.S. hosted the women's game in 1996 and returns 32 years later with a sport transformed—bigger crowds, bigger stakes, bigger stars.

Energizer Park's below-grade design creates an intimate bowl where fans are on top of the action. The transparent canopy keeps weather out and noise in, with every seat within 120 feet of the pitch.

Group stage matches determine knockout seeding and set up potential blockbuster matchups. The opening matches carry added weight—win and you control your destiny, stumble and you're chasing points.
FBL22.potentialContendersIntro: Defending champions United States lead a projected field featuring Paris 2024 medalists Brazil and Germany, plus likely qualifiers Spain, England, and France.

potentialContenders.name: Sophia Smith
potentialContenders.note: Paris 2024 gold medalist, explosive U.S. forward

potentialContenders.name: Lauren James
potentialContenders.note: England's projected star if they qualify

FBL23.blurb:
Columbus welcomes women's soccer as group stage play spreads across six American cities. Sixteen teams means four groups of four—three matches each to determine who advances to the knockout rounds.

Scotts Miracle-Gro Field's full-coverage canopy protects all 20,371 fans from the elements while leaving the pitch open to the sky. The heated hybrid grass ensures consistent playing conditions, and the steep seating bowl puts you on top of every touch.

Women's group stage soccer offers tactical variety—European possession, South American flair, North American athleticism. Early matches showcase styles before knockout pressure tightens the game.
FBL23.potentialContendersIntro: The United States won Paris 2024 and enters as favorites on home soil. Brazil and Germany, the Paris runners-up and bronze medalists, are expected qualifiers alongside Spain and other European powers.

FBL24.blurb:
Women's soccer debuts at New York's new Etihad Park, a 25,000-seat venue unveiled in 2027 as the city's first purpose-built soccer stadium. Sixteen nations compete in group stage action, with defending champions United States likely among the field.

The compact design features 3,400 safe standing positions and a rooftop supporters bar overlooking the pitch. A 40,000-square-foot food hall showcases vendors from across New York's five boroughs—soccer meets the city's culinary diversity.

Group play is where tournaments are won or lost. Three matches, six available points, and the margin for error is razor-thin in a format that sends half the teams home after the round-robin.
FBL24.potentialContendersIntro: The United States defends its Paris 2024 title on home soil. Brazil and Germany, the Paris silver and bronze medalists, are projected to qualify alongside Spain, England, and France.

FBL25.blurb:
San Jose's PayPal Park hosts opening-day women's group stage soccer in the tournament's most intimate venue. The 18,000-seat capacity and steepest-raked seating in MLS create close-up views from every angle—you're practically on the pitch.

The stadium features North America's largest outdoor bar behind one goal and a 2-acre fan zone for pregame atmosphere. Bay Area fans have supported both the Earthquakes and Bay FC; expect a soccer-savvy crowd that knows the game.

Group stage matches offer early tournament value—world-class soccer before knockout tickets spike. Watch teams navigate the tactical puzzle: when to attack, when to sit deep, when to settle for a draw.
FBL25.potentialContendersIntro: Defending champions United States headline a projected field with Paris 2024 silver medalists Brazil and bronze medalists Germany. Spain and England could also feature if they qualify.

FBL26.blurb:
PayPal Park doubles up with a second women's group stage match as the tournament spreads across six U.S. cities. The women's game returns to American soil for the first time since 1996, when the U.S. won gold in front of record crowds.

San Jose's soccer-specific design means no bad seats—the steep rake and intimate 18,000 capacity put you on top of every pass, every tackle, every celebration. Field-level suites offer premium views while the outdoor bar behind the goal brings festival atmosphere.

Group stage soccer is a three-match sprint. Early points matter: win and you're in control, lose and you're chasing, draw and you're still alive but sweating the final matchday math.
FBL26.potentialContendersIntro: The sixteen-team field will feature defending champions United States and Paris 2024 medalists Brazil and Germany, with additional spots determined through continental qualification.

FBL27.blurb:
San Diego's Snapdragon Stadium—built in 2022 on the site of the old Qualcomm Stadium—hosts women's group stage action. The 35,000-seat venue is the largest soccer-specific stadium in the tournament rotation, with open-air sightlines and landscaped concourses.

The lower deck wraps the field for intimacy despite the size, while a 5,000-seat student section includes 2,900 safe-standing spots for intense atmosphere. Premium loge boxes and social decks offer upscale viewing with field views.

Group stage matches set knockout seeding. Three points for a win, one for a draw, zero for a loss—the table doesn't lie, and by matchday three, the math determines who advances and who packs bags.
FBL27.potentialContendersIntro: Defending champions United States lead the projected field. Paris 2024 runners-up Brazil and bronze medalists Germany are expected qualifiers, alongside European contenders Spain and England.

FBL28.blurb:

The 35,000-capacity design includes extensive catering, cashless concessions, and social viewing decks. The lower bowl encloses the pitch while upper decks on both sides add volume—expect full-throated support for sixteen nations chasing knockout berths.

Women's soccer has evolved into a global powerhouse. Group stage play showcases that depth—every match competitive, every team dangerous, every point precious in the race to advance.
FBL28.potentialContendersIntro: The United States defends its Paris 2024 title with home-field advantage. Brazil and Germany, the Paris medalists, are projected qualifiers. Spain, England, and France could round out a stacked field.

New York's Etihad Park continues group stage action as twelve men's teams navigate the tournament's opening round. With only a dozen spots in the field, the competition is elite—every nation earned its place through grueling regional qualification.

The stadium's compact 25,000-seat design creates European-style intensity, with safe standing sections and a rooftop bar overlooking the pitch. Queens hosts the Games with a venue purpose-built for soccer, unveiled just a year before the tournament.

Group stage math is unforgiving: three matches to collect enough points to advance. Win twice and you're likely through; stumble twice and you're watching knockouts from home.
FBL29.potentialContendersIntro: Spain defends its Paris 2024 gold medal, with France and Morocco—the Paris silver and bronze medalists—projected as qualifiers. Argentina and Brazil could also feature if they navigate South American competition.

Columbus wraps the opening weekend with men's group stage soccer at Scotts Miracle-Gro Field. The 20,000-seat venue's full canopy and steep sightlines create an intimate cauldron—weather stays out, crowd noise stays in, and every fan feels the match up close.

The heated hybrid grass surface ensures consistent play, while downtown Columbus pulses with tournament energy just outside the gates. The stadium hosted the 2024 MLS All-Star Game and multiple U.S. national team matches—big-game atmosphere is built in.

By matchday three of the group stage, the knockout picture clarifies. Watch teams fight for points that mean advancement or elimination, with every tackle and save carrying extra weight.
FBL30.potentialContendersIntro: Defending champions Spain are expected to qualify, along with Paris 2024 silver medalists France and bronze medalists Morocco. The compact 12-team format ensures only the elite reach the Games.

Spain enters as defending champions after their Paris 2024 gold, while France and Morocco—the silver and bronze medalists—could return if they qualify. Group play determines who advances to the knockout rounds.

Scotts Miracle-Gro Field sits in downtown Columbus with all seats covered and the pitch open to the sky. The atmosphere is intimate, the sightlines are tight, and you're watching world-class talent in a soccer-first venue.

Spain won gold at Paris 2024, France took silver, and Morocco claimed bronze—all three could appear in the 2028 field if they qualify. The format is straightforward: win or draw to build group-stage points.

Geodis Park traps sound like few venues in North America. The pitch is natural grass, the concourse wraps the stadium, and the supporters section on the north side creates a wall of noise.
