Nations field six-athlete rosters spanning combined weight categories in the mixed team event's elimination rounds, repechage, and semifinals. Teams need competitors in women's -57kg/-70kg/+70kg ranges and men's -73kg/-90kg/+90kg ranges, rewarding NOCs with depth across individual weight classes.

Best-of-seven match format mixes genders in strategic sequences—nations choose which athletes compete in which combined categories, creating tactical puzzles. Winners advance to afternoon medal finals while losers enter repechage for bronze opportunities.

All-day Saturday session at the Convention Center transforms individual competition into team dynamics. You'll see athletes who medaled earlier in the week now fighting for national pride, with crowd energy building as teams advance toward finals.
JUD15.potentialContendersIntro: Teams need qualified athletes across all six combined weight categories, with strong individual performers from earlier sessions likely anchoring their national rosters if eligible.

JUD16.blurb:
The mixed team event closes the judo program with bronze and gold medal matches staged back-to-back in downtown Los Angeles. Teams of six judoka—three men, three women—battle in best-of-seven showdowns where strategy matters as much as strength.

Each bout unfolds across six weight classes, from lightweight to heavyweight, with coaches choosing their lineup to exploit matchups. A single ippon can swing momentum; golden score overtime can decide championships in seconds.

LA Convention Center Hall 2 transforms into an arena for close-quarters combat, with spectators ringed around the tatami watching throws, chokes, and armbars that end in an instant. The crowd feeds directly into the tension—every score change echoes through the hall.
JUD16.potentialContendersIntro: France won gold at Paris 2024, defeating Japan in the final. Japan, France, Germany, and South Korea are projected to field strong squads based on their depth across weight classes.

potentialContenders.name: France
potentialContenders.note: Reigning mixed team gold medalists from Paris 2024

potentialContenders.name: Japan
potentialContenders.note: Silver medalists at Paris 2024, historically dominant in judo

potentialContenders.name: Germany
potentialContenders.note: Bronze medalists at Paris 2024 with strong European depth

potentialContenders.name: South Korea
potentialContenders.note: Consistent podium contender across multiple weight classes

potentialContenders.name: Brazil
potentialContenders.note: Rising program with medalists across both men's and women's divisions

LAC01.blurb:
Lacrosse makes its first Games appearance in 120 years with a brand-new sixes format—six players per side, compact 70x36 meter fields, and a 30-second shot clock that keeps the action relentless. Three men's qualifying round matches tip off the tournament, setting the stage for medal games later in the week.

The host United States men's team is expected to compete if they participate in the 2026 continental championships and 2027 world event. The full six-team field will be determined through a multi-year qualification pathway spanning four continents, with the final roster locked in early 2028.

Exposition Park Stadium's open-air grandstands and downtown LA skyline create an energetic backdrop for this historic debut. The smaller field and all-action format mean you're always close to the play, with goalies restarting immediately after scores and all twelve roster players handling both offense and defense.
LAC01.potentialContendersIntro: The six-team men's field will be determined through continental championships in 2026, the 2027 World Lacrosse Sixes Championships, and a final qualifying tournament in early 2028, with the United States automatically qualifying as host nation.

potentialContenders.name: United States
potentialContenders.note: Automatic host nation qualifier if participating in continental and world championships

LAC02.blurb:
The evening session brings three women's qualifying round games under the lights at Exposition Park Stadium. This is lacrosse's Games debut, reimagined in the sixes format—faster, higher-scoring, and built for global competition.

Each roster features exactly twelve players who must handle both ends of the field, with no long crosses allowed and teams needing six on the pitch to start. Four 8-minute quarters and sudden-death overtime if tied mean games move quickly.

The night atmosphere at Exposition Park amplifies the hometown energy, with LA's skyline glowing beyond the stadium rim. This accessible format makes it easy to follow even if you've never seen lacrosse before—just watch the shot clock and the relentless pace.
LAC02.potentialContendersIntro: The six-team women's field will be determined through continental championships in 2026, the 2027 World Lacrosse Sixes Championships, and a final qualifying tournament in early 2028, with the United States automatically qualifying as host nation.

potentialContenders.name: United States
potentialContenders.note: Automatic host nation qualifier if participating in continental and world championships

LAC03.blurb:
Day two of men's qualifying rounds delivers three more games as the six-team field battles for advantageous seeding heading into placement matches. The compact tournament format means every prelim matters—results here shape the path to bronze and gold.

Teams qualified through a rigorous pathway: continental championships across Pan-America, Asia-Pacific, Europe, and Africa in late 2026, then four spots at the 2027 World Sixes Championships, and a final qualifier in early 2028. The United States is projected to compete as host nation.

Exposition Park Stadium seats roughly 22,000 in an intimate soccer venue configuration, meaning sightlines stay tight even for a sport making its Games return after more than a century away.
LAC03.potentialContendersIntro: The men's field includes teams qualified through a multi-stage process beginning with 2026 continental championships and culminating in a 2027 world event and early 2028 final qualifier.

potentialContenders.name: United States
potentialContenders.note: Projected to compete as host nation pending continental and world championship participation

LAC04.blurb:
Three more women's qualifying games unfold in the evening session, with teams jockeying for position in the medal round bracket. The sixes format emphasizes speed—no long crosses, goalies restart play immediately after goals, and the 30-second shot clock never stops ticking.

The women's qualification pathway mirrors the men's: continental events in late 2026, four berths at the 2027 World Lacrosse Sixes Championships, and a final tournament in early 2028 for the last spot. Host United States is expected to field a competitive squad.

Under the lights at Exposition Park, the compact field and rapid transitions make for a viewing experience closer to basketball than traditional field lacrosse—constant movement, quick substitutions, and goals coming in waves.
LAC04.potentialContendersIntro: The women's field is determined through the same rigorous qualification process as the men, with the United States projected to compete as host nation.

potentialContenders.name: United States
potentialContenders.note: Projected to compete as host nation pending continental and world championship participation

LAC05.blurb:
Midweek qualifying continues with three men's games as the tournament builds toward Saturday's medal matches. Teams earned their spots through a grueling two-year qualification cycle that began with continental championships and narrowed a field of roughly 100 nations down to six.

The European Sixes Championships in Salou, Spain (November 2-18, 2026) will send qualifiers to the 2027 world event, where a maximum of three teams per continental federation can advance. The final Olympic spot goes to the top finisher at a March/April 2028 qualifier.

Exposition Park Stadium sits adjacent to the historic LA Memorial Coliseum, giving the venue a legacy sports feel even though it only opened in 2018. The open-air design and summer afternoon heat add to the intensity.
LAC05.potentialContendersIntro: Teams competing reached the 2028 Games through a qualification pathway spanning four continents, with the final berth determined in early 2028.

LAC06.blurb:
The women's prelims hit the halfway mark with three evening games on Wednesday. The six-team field represents the culmination of a global qualification process that saw continental allocations of five teams each from Asia-Pacific, Europe, and Pan-America, plus one from Africa.

After the 2027 World Lacrosse Sixes Championships award four berths, one final spot remains open for a March/April 2028 qualifier featuring the top six eligible teams based on world rankings. African Sixes Championships in December 2026 will send one team per gender forward.

Exposition Park Stadium's proximity to downtown LA makes it a natural gathering spot for the city's lacrosse community—expect a knowledgeable, passionate crowd even for prelim games.
LAC06.potentialContendersIntro: The women's field of six is determined through the same multi-stage qualification as the men, with continental representation from four federations.

LAC07.blurb:
Thursday afternoon brings three more men's qualifying games as teams fine-tune positioning ahead of the medal round. The 30-second shot clock and compact field dimensions mean scoring comes fast—expect double-digit totals and goalies constantly in motion.

All twelve players on each sixes roster handle both offense and defense, a departure from traditional field lacrosse's specialized roles. Teams needed a top-12 finish at the 2027 World Sixes Championships to remain eligible for the final Olympic qualifier.

Exposition Park Stadium's 22,000-seat capacity creates an intimate atmosphere for a sport returning to the Games stage. The venue's home clubs—Angel City FC and LAFC—have built a reputation for raucous crowds that should carry over to lacrosse.
LAC07.potentialContendersIntro: Teams reached the 2028 Games by finishing in the top tiers of their continental championships and the 2027 world event, with strict limits on teams per federation.

LAC08.blurb:
The women's qualifying rounds enter the home stretch with three Thursday evening games at Exposition Park Stadium. This is the last full slate of group play before Saturday's bronze and gold medal matches.

Lacrosse gained full IOC recognition based on the sixes format's accessibility and global reach—the streamlined rules and smaller rosters make the sport more feasible for emerging nations. Games require exactly six players including a goalkeeper to start; fall below that and it's a forfeit.

The venue hosts all lacrosse action from July 24-29, giving the sport its own dedicated footprint in the Games calendar. The open-air stadium and summer evening kickoffs create a festival atmosphere as fans settle in for triple-headers.
LAC08.potentialContendersIntro: The women's six-team field represents continental champions and top finishers from the 2027 World Lacrosse Sixes Championships.

LAC09.blurb:
The final day of men's prelims wraps up Friday with three games determining final seeding for the medal round. Approximately 100 teams entered the overall qualification pathway in 2026; now only six remain, each representing a continental powerhouse or breakthrough nation.

Host United States has not yet announced its 2028 roster as the Games approach. Sixes rosters are capped at 12 players, all of whom participate in offense and defense—no specialists, no substitutions based on possession.

Exposition Park Stadium's sightlines keep you close to the action on the compact field. With four 8-minute quarters and a 15-minute halftime, sessions move briskly—three games in under four hours including breaks.
LAC09.potentialContendersIntro: The men's field represents the top six teams from a multi-year global qualification process spanning continental championships, world rankings, and final qualifiers.

potentialContenders.name: United States
potentialContenders.note: Projected host nation qualifier; roster not yet announced

LAC10.blurb:
Friday night closes out the women's qualifying rounds with three games setting the bracket for Saturday's medal matches. Lacrosse's Games debut culminates the next evening, capping a tournament structure that prioritizes speed and scoring.

World Lacrosse earned full IOC recognition by building the sixes format as a more inclusive, globally accessible version of the sport. The six-player requirement is strict—teams forfeit if they can't field a goalkeeper plus five.

Exposition Park Stadium has hosted national and international soccer since opening in 2018, and the lacrosse setup uses similar field dimensions. The venue's open-air design and LA skyline backdrop give every session a distinctly Californian vibe.
LAC10.potentialContendersIntro: The women's field consists of six teams qualified through continental championships and the 2027 World Lacrosse Sixes Championships.

LAC11.blurb:
Lacrosse's historic return to the Games reaches its climax with the men's bronze and gold medal matches in a Saturday afternoon double-header. Six teams have battled through a week of prelims; now two pairs will settle the podium in the sixes format's fast-paced, high-scoring style.

The United States is projected to compete as host nation if they participated in the 2026 continental championships and 2027 World Lacrosse Sixes Championships. The full field represents continental champions from Pan-America, Asia-Pacific, Europe, and Africa, plus top finishers at the world event and a final 2028 qualifier.

Exposition Park Stadium will be packed for medal day—22,000 fans in an open-air venue with downtown LA rising beyond the rim. Four 8-minute quarters and sudden-death overtime if needed mean each game delivers nonstop action from opening faceoff to final whistle.
LAC11.potentialContendersIntro: The men's medal matches will feature teams qualified through 2026 continental championships, the 2027 World Lacrosse Sixes Championships, and an early 2028 final qualifier, with the United States projected to compete as host nation.

potentialContenders.name: United States
potentialContenders.note: Projected to compete as host nation if participating in continental and world championships

LAC12.blurb:
The women's bronze and gold medal games close out lacrosse's 2028 debut under the Saturday night lights at Exposition Park Stadium. This is the sport's first Games appearance since 1908, reimagined for a global stage with the compact, inclusive sixes format.

The United States is expected to field a competitive squad as host nation, having automatically qualified if they met participation requirements in 2026 and 2027 events. The remaining five teams earned their spots through a rigorous two-year pathway spanning four continents and a world championship.

Medal night at Exposition Park brings epic energy—the open-air stadium, LA skyline backdrop, and hometown crowd create a quintessential Summer Games atmosphere. The 30-second shot clock and all-action sixes rules guarantee a thrilling finale to lacrosse's long-awaited return.
LAC12.potentialContendersIntro: The women's medal matches will feature teams qualified through 2026 continental championships, the 2027 World Lacrosse Sixes Championships, and an early 2028 final qualifier, with the United States projected to compete as host nation.

potentialContenders.name: United States
potentialContenders.note: Projected to compete as host nation if participating in continental and world championships

MPN01.blurb:
Modern Pentathlon opens the 2028 Games with a thrilling women's semifinal, where athletes compete in five disciplines over one intense morning. This year marks a historic shift: the traditional equestrian event gives way to a high-octane Ninja-style obstacle course, adding explosive head-to-head sprints to the fencing, swimming, and laser run.

Eighteen competitors battle through the disciplines to earn their spots in the Monday final. Points earned in fencing bouts, obstacle runs, and 200m swims determine starting positions for the decisive laser run — a grueling run-shoot-run sequence where athletes must hit targets before continuing.

Valley Complex 2 brings fans right to the action in an intimate, purpose-built arena. The open-air setting in Sepulveda Basin puts you close enough to hear the clash of fencing blades and the rhythm of laser pistols firing.
MPN01.potentialContendersIntro: Paris 2024 medalists Michelle Gulyás, Élodie Clouvel, and Seong Seung-Min are expected to headline the semifinal field, though final rosters depend on 2028 qualification results.

MPN02.blurb:
The men's field tackles all five disciplines in a single four-and-a-half-hour showcase of versatility and endurance. Eighteen athletes vie for spots in Tuesday's final, navigating fencing rounds, the new obstacle course, a 200m swim, and the pressure-packed laser run.

The debut of the obstacle event reshapes strategy — a fast, technical sprint through Ninja-style barriers replaces the unpredictability of show jumping. Consistent performers across fencing, obstacle, and swimming earn crucial time advantages heading into the laser run, where the first to cross the line after completing four shoot-and-run laps advances.

Spectators at Valley Complex 2 experience the full spectrum of Modern Pentathlon in one location. The temporary venue configuration keeps all five events within sight, letting you track athletes from the fencing piste to the finish line.
MPN02.potentialContendersIntro: Ahmed El-Gendy, Taishu Sato, and Giorgio Malan — the Paris 2024 podium finishers — are projected to compete if they qualify through the 2028 system.

MPN03.blurb:
Gold, silver, and bronze are decided in a two-hour final where nine women who survived Saturday's semifinal compete across fencing, obstacle, swimming, and the decisive laser run. The new obstacle format — a head-to-head Ninja course — adds explosive drama to a sport already known for unpredictable finishes.

Points from the first three disciplines set staggered starting positions for the laser run, where athletes alternate between 800m running legs and shooting at five targets. The first woman to cross the finish line after four laps claims gold — no matter her starting deficit.

The compact Valley Complex 2 venue puts every twist and turn on display. Morning sun illuminates the San Fernando Valley as the competition builds toward the laser run, where fortunes can flip in seconds.
MPN03.potentialContendersIntro: If the Paris 2024 medalists qualify, Michelle Gulyás will defend her title against Élodie Clouvel and Seong Seung-Min in a rematch of the closest finish in recent years.

MPN04.blurb:
Nine finalists chase medals in a test of all-around athleticism that crowns Modern Pentathlon's most versatile competitor. Fencing bouts, the new obstacle course, and a 200m swim determine who enters the laser run with a time advantage — and who must chase from behind.

The laser run finale is pure drama: staggered starts mean the leader on the course wins the gold. Athletes sprint 800m, stop to shoot five targets, then repeat three more times. A single missed shot or flagging pace in the final lap can cost a medal.

Valley Complex 2's intimate layout ensures you won't miss a moment as the competition builds to its climax. The temporary venue in Sepulveda Basin offers close-range views of athletes pushing through fatigue in the sport's most demanding format.
MPN04.potentialContendersIntro: Ahmed El-Gendy could defend his Paris 2024 gold if he qualifies, with silver medalist Taishu Sato and bronze winner Giorgio Malan likely in the field based on recent form.

MTB01.blurb:
Watch the world's toughest mountain bikers attack a punishing cross-country course through the San Gabriel Valley foothills. Riders navigate a 4-6km loop of technical climbs, high-speed descents, and jarring rock gardens — racing multiple laps over two and a half hours with medals on the line.

Tom Pidcock of Great Britain defended his title in Paris, outlasting France's Victor Koretzky in a thrilling sprint finish. South Africa's Alan Hatherly claimed bronze. The 36-rider field in 2028 could feature rematch storylines if those medalists return, along with rising contenders from the World Cup circuit.

Industry Hills puts spectators right on top of the action — hillside vantage points let you track riders through successive laps as they grind uphill and send jumps. The open-air venue captures the raw physicality of cross-country racing better than any TV feed.
MTB01.potentialContendersIntro: Tom Pidcock won gold in Paris and remains the rider to beat if he competes in 2028. Victor Koretzky and Alan Hatherly stood on the Paris podium and could return as top contenders.

MTB02.blurb:
The women's cross-country final crowns the best all-around mountain biker over multiple laps of brutal terrain. Expect two-plus hours of racing as 36 riders tackle climbs that test stamina, descents that demand nerves, and technical sections where one mistake costs positions.

Pauline Ferrand-Prévot of France dominated in Paris to finally claim her first gold medal. American Haley Batten took silver on home soil's horizon, while Sweden's Jenny Rissveds returned to the podium with bronze. If Batten competes in Los Angeles, she'll race with a home crowd advantage and unfinished business.

Industry Hills offers close-up drama — watch riders suffer on the climbs, then send it through rock drops moments later. The hillside layout means you'll see the same athletes multiple times per lap, tracking their fade or surge as the race unfolds.
MTB02.potentialContendersIntro: Haley Batten of the United States won silver in Paris and would be a home-crowd favorite if she qualifies for 2028. Paris gold medalist Pauline Ferrand-Prévot and bronze medalist Jenny Rissveds could also return as contenders.

OWS01.blurb:
The women's 10km marathon swim puts endurance and strategy on full display in a single-loop Pacific Ocean course. Twenty-two of the world's toughest distance swimmers will battle currents, swells, and each other over two-plus hours of open water.

Paris 2024 gold medalist Sharon van Rouwendaal could return to defend her title, while silver medalist Moesha Johnson and bronze medalist Ginevra Taddeucci are projected contenders if they qualify through the 2027 World Championships or spring 2028 qualification event.

Watch from the sandy beach at Belmont Shore as swimmers navigate the coastal course, their bright caps visible against the blue water, with the Pacific stretching to the horizon.
OWS01.potentialContendersIntro: The field will be determined through the 2027 World Championships and a 2028 qualification event, with Paris 2024 medalists among the projected contenders.

OWS02.blurb:
Gold medals are on the line in the men's 10km marathon swim, a grueling test of pace judgment and mental fortitude. The coastal course off Long Beach offers ever-changing conditions as swimmers stroke through salt water for nearly two and a half hours.

Paris 2024 champion Kristóf Rasovszky could defend his title if he qualifies, with silver medalist Oliver Klemet and bronze medalist Dávid Betlehem likely in the mix. Hungary claimed two Paris podium spots and will be a nation to watch.

Belmont Shore provides prime beachside viewing as the 22-athlete field churns through the Pacific, with feeding platforms and course buoys marking the swimmers' progress against the open horizon.
OWS02.potentialContendersIntro: Paris 2024 podium finishers are projected to be among the field, pending qualification through the 2027 World Championships and 2028 qualification event.

RCB01.blurb:
Beach sprint rowing makes its Games debut with solo time trials and opening knockout rounds for women and men on the Pacific waters off Long Beach. Rowers navigate tides and currents in specialized coastal sculls designed for open water, racing short sprints that test explosive power and boat handling in conditions far removed from the protected flatwater of classic rowing.

The morning features two time trial heats to seed the last 16 knockout brackets, where direct head-to-head racing begins. Solo scullers must balance raw speed with the technical demands of waves and wind, skills honed in coastal rowing circuits but never before tested at this level.

Belmont Shore's sandy waterfront offers spectators close-up views as boats launch directly from the beach, with the break wall visible offshore providing some protection while keeping the coastal character of the racing intact.
RCB01.potentialContendersIntro: No world rankings or qualification rosters have been announced for the 2028 solo events as of April 2026, making the field wide open for the discipline's Games debut.

RCB02.blurb:
The first-ever beach sprint rowing medals will be decided in back-to-back women's and men's solo finals, crowning pioneers in this new discipline. Eight finalists in each event race head-to-head sprints on open water, their boats cutting through Pacific swells in a test of pure speed and adaptability that has no equivalent in flatwater rowing.

The final 8 format brings knockout racing to its climax, with each heat eliminating rowers until gold, silver, and bronze are decided in a closing sprint. Coastal specialists who have dominated world beach sprint championships face traditional flatwater stars testing their skills in unfamiliar conditions.

The afternoon session at Belmont Shore puts spectators right on the sand as boats finish mere meters from shore, offering an intimate view of a discipline making its mark on the Games program.
RCB02.potentialContendersIntro: With no Paris 2024 precedent and no announced 2028 rosters, the solo finals could draw from both coastal rowing specialists and flatwater stars adapting to beach sprints.

RCB03.blurb:
Mixed doubles make their debut as coastal rowing adds a team dimension to beach sprint competition. Pairs of one woman and one man navigate time trials and last 16 knockout rounds in specialized double sculls built to handle waves, currents, and the challenges of racing just offshore from Long Beach's beaches.

Two time trial heats seed the field for direct elimination racing, where crew coordination becomes as critical as individual power. The mixed format creates new tactical dynamics as pairs balance weight distribution and stroke rhythm in conditions that can shift with wind and tide during the session.

Spectators watch from the sandy shoreline as doubles launch through surf and sprint parallel to the beach, the temporary infrastructure bringing fans within shouting distance of crews navigating the Pacific's coastal waters.
RCB03.potentialContendersIntro: No 2028 qualification results or world rankings have been released for mixed doubles as of April 2026, leaving the inaugural field uncertain.

RCB04.blurb:
The mixed double sculls final 8 will award beach sprint rowing's third and final medal, capping the discipline's debut with a showdown between the eight fastest pairs from morning prelims. Crews navigate knockout heats in doubles designed for open water, racing short sprints where synchronization and boat handling separate medalists from the field.

The final 8 structure intensifies as pairs are eliminated heat by heat until three crews remain for the medal race. Break wall protection keeps conditions manageable while preserving the coastal character that defines this new branch of rowing, testing skills rarely seen in the protected lanes of traditional regattas.

Belmont Shore's beachfront setup puts spectators at the finish line as doubles cross mere meters from sand, concluding two days of competition that expand rowing's reach at the 2028 Games.
RCB04.potentialContendersIntro: With no announced teams or recent form available for 2028 mixed doubles, the final 8 field remains speculative until qualification concludes.

ROW01.blurb:
The Games begin at Marine Stadium with opening heats across three boat classes. Women's single scullers take their first strokes on the new 1500m course, while men's double sculls and pairs launch their campaigns in back-to-back heats.

The shortened distance compresses races into faster, tighter battles—expect sub-five-minute sprints with less room for tactical recoveries. Early heats sort the field for later knockout rounds, but top contenders often show their cards immediately.

Marine Stadium's calm, protected waters and shoreline bleachers put you close to the starting docks and finish line. The Long Beach venue—America's first manmade rowing course from 1932—returns to host another generation of scullers.
ROW01.potentialContendersIntro: Paris 2024 medalists Karolien Florijn (NED, gold), Emma Twigg (NZL, silver), and Viktorija Senkutė (LTU, bronze) could all return in women's single sculls heats, though 2028 qualification is still underway.

ROW02.blurb:
Men's single sculls heats join women's pairs and doubles heats on Day 2, with men's semis in double sculls and pairs delivering higher stakes. Quarterfinal-bound scullers face elimination pressure, while semifinalists race for lane assignments in upcoming A finals.

Semifinals typically feature the session's fastest times as medal contenders reveal peak form. The mixed schedule lets fans track multiple boat classes in one afternoon, from solo scullers to synchronized pairs.

Long Beach's open-air setup and waterfront access make for a casual yet engaged atmosphere—fans walk the shoreline between races, tracking crews warming up and recovering in the boat bay.
ROW02.potentialContendersIntro: Oliver Zeidler (GER) dominated in Paris with men's single sculls gold; if he qualifies for 2028, expect him in these heats. Yauheni Zalaty and Simon van Dorp could also return.

ROW03.blurb:
The first gold medals of the rowing program are awarded as men's double sculls and pairs crown champions in A finals. Women's single sculls quarterfinals and women's doubles and pairs semis fill out the slate, mixing elimination rounds with medal ceremonies.

Gold-medal finals on the 1500m course deliver decisive sprints with minimal recovery time—crew positioning in the first 500 meters often determines the podium. B and C finals resolve minor placings before the medal races.

Marine Stadium's bleachers and shoreline vantage points offer clear sightlines to the finish-line scramble. The afternoon session builds to its medal moments as national anthems echo across Long Beach's historic waters.
ROW03.potentialContendersIntro: Men's pairs and double sculls contenders will be determined by 2028 qualifying, but Paris 2024 form offers clues to likely finalists. Women's single sculls will see Paris medalists Florijn, Twigg, and Senkutė—if qualified—racing through quarterfinals.

ROW04.blurb:
Women's double sculls and pairs award their medals in A, B, and C finals while men's quad sculls and fours launch their campaigns with opening heats. Men's single sculls quarterfinals add knockout drama as scullers fight for semifinal berths.

The session blends closure—medal ceremonies for women's boats—with beginnings as larger crew boats enter the fray. Quad sculls and fours bring coordinated power, four or more rowers moving in perfect sync across 1500 meters.

Afternoon sunshine on Marine Stadium's calm water creates ideal racing conditions. Fans gather near the dock area to watch victorious women's crews celebrate and men's quads rig up for their first tests.
ROW04.potentialContendersIntro: Men's single sculls quarterfinals could feature Paris 2024 medalists Oliver Zeidler (GER, gold), Yauheni Zalaty, and Simon van Dorp (NED, bronze) if they qualify for 2028.

ROW05.blurb:
An early-morning session packs medals and advancement rounds into just over two hours. Men's quad sculls and fours reach their A and B finals, deciding gold while women's single sculls semis determine who races for medals later in the week.

Women's quad sculls and fours start their competition with heats, introducing the largest women's boats to the 1500m course. The compact schedule and sunrise start time give the session an intimate, focused energy.

Marine Stadium at 8 a.m. offers cool coastal air and soft morning light. Die-hard fans claim shoreline spots early; the relaxed crowd watches medal ceremonies and heats unfold against Long Beach's waking waterfront.
ROW05.potentialContendersIntro: Men's quad sculls and fours medals will go to crews still qualifying, but European powerhouses typically dominate these events. Women's single sculls semis likely include Paris 2024's top three if they advance.

ROW06.blurb:
Women's quad sculls and fours crown champions in A and B finals while men's eights begin their pursuit with opening heats. Men's single sculls semis send the fastest scullers toward medal finals, with E and F finals resolving the rest of the field.

Eights—the sport's largest and fastest boats—debut with dramatic power. Eight rowers plus a coxswain drive the 60-foot shell in a spectacle of synchronized force and speed.

Mid-morning at Marine Stadium brings reliable conditions and growing crowds. The bleachers fill as fans watch women's crews receive medals and men's eights power through their first 1500-meter tests on the historic course.
ROW06.potentialContendersIntro: Men's single sculls semis could feature Oliver Zeidler (GER), the Paris 2024 champion, if he qualifies and advances. Women's fours and quad sculls medals go to crews determined through ongoing qualification.

ROW07.blurb:
Women's single sculls reach their medal finals—A, B, C, and D—crowning the champion sculler in the session's marquee race. Men's eights award medals in A and B finals while women's eights open their campaign with heats.

The women's single sculls A final determines the sport's most prestigious individual title. One rower, one boat, 1500 meters—no teammates to share the load or the glory.

Marine Stadium's finish-line area becomes the emotional center as medal races conclude. Fans line the water's edge to watch exhausted scullers cross, national flags waving as the Games' rowing climax nears its conclusion.
ROW07.potentialContendersIntro: Paris 2024 medalists Karolien Florijn (NED, gold), Emma Twigg (NZL, silver), and Viktorija Senkutė (LTU, bronze) could all appear in the A final if they qualify and advance through earlier rounds.

ROW08.blurb:
The final rowing session at Marine Stadium awards the last two titles: men's single sculls and women's eights. Both A finals deliver gold-medal drama on the 1500m course as the sport's traditional program concludes before coastal rowing begins.

Men's single sculls mirrors the women's championship race two days prior—one athlete, total exposure, no margin for error. Women's eights brings the largest and fastest women's boat to the medal stand.

The Long Beach venue bids farewell to classic rowing with late-morning races and closing ceremonies. Fans soak in the historic setting one last time before coastal rowing debuts elsewhere on July 24.
ROW08.potentialContendersIntro: Oliver Zeidler (GER) won Paris 2024 men's single sculls gold and could return as the favorite if he qualifies for 2028. Yauheni Zalaty and Simon van Dorp (NED) are also potential finalists.

RU701.blurb:
The women's Rugby Sevens tournament kicks off with morning pool play at Carson Stadium, where 12 nations begin their medal chase across six rapid-fire matches. Each game lasts just 14 minutes, making this fast-paced format the perfect introduction to sevens — wide-open running, constant action, and scores that can flip in seconds.

Three pools of four teams each face off in round-robin play, with New Zealand, Canada, and Australia — the Paris 2024 podium finishers — expected to appear across the session. Pool results determine quarterfinal seeding, so every try and tackle carries knockout-stage implications.

Carson Stadium's steep stands bring fans close to the action, and the open-air design means you'll feel the collisions. Morning starts keep temperatures comfortable for three hours of nonstop rugby.
RU701.potentialContendersIntro: Paris 2024 medalists New Zealand, Canada, and Australia are projected to lead the field, though final rosters won't be confirmed until qualification concludes.

RU702.blurb:
Pool play continues under the late afternoon sun with the second session of women's group-stage matches. Teams that won in the morning return to solidify their standings, while opening-match losers face must-win pressure to stay in contention for the knockout rounds.

Six matches rotate through Carson Stadium's single pitch, maintaining the tournament's breakneck pace. The Paris 2024 medalists — New Zealand, Canada, and Australia — could all appear again depending on pool assignments, setting up potential preview matchups of later medal clashes.

Evening starts bring cooler air and rowdier crowds as fans settle in for another three-hour block. The stadium's modular design keeps sightlines intimate even with temporary seating added for the Games.
RU702.potentialContendersIntro: The same Paris 2024 podium nations — New Zealand (gold), Canada (silver), and Australia (bronze) — are likely to feature prominently if they compete in this session's pool matches.

RU703.blurb:
The final round of women's pool play decides everything — quarterfinal seeding, knockout matchups, and which four teams exit early. With group standings on the line, expect desperation tries and tactical kicking as teams protect leads or chase comebacks.

New Zealand, Canada, and Australia — the Paris 2024 medal trio — will likely have already secured quarterfinal spots by now, but final pool positions determine whether they face weaker opponents or immediate heavyweight clashes in the knockout rounds. One bad result here can derail a medal run.

Morning kickoff means fresh legs and crisp execution before the heat sets in. Carson Stadium's proximity to the pitch amplifies every referee decision and big tackle as the group stage reaches its climax.
RU703.potentialContendersIntro: Paris 2024's top three — New Zealand (gold), Canada (silver), and Australia (bronze) — are expected to navigate pool play, though their exact seeding depends on these final group results.

RU704.blurb:
The knockout stage begins as the top eight teams enter single-elimination quarterfinals, where one loss ends all medal hopes. The four winners advance to Saturday's semifinals; the losers drop into 5th-8th classification.

New Zealand, Canada, and Australia — the Paris 2024 podium — are projected to reach this stage, potentially facing each other in quarterfinal showdowns that would serve as early medal-match previews. Classification 9-12 matches open the session, giving teams that didn't escape pool play a chance to finish strong.

Afternoon crowds bring energy as stakes escalate. Carson Stadium's steep seating puts you right on top of the sideline, close enough to hear the calls and feel the collisions that decide who survives.
RU704.potentialContendersIntro: Paris 2024 medalists New Zealand (gold), Canada (silver), and Australia (bronze) are expected to contend in the quarterfinals, though quarterfinal pairings depend on pool results.

RU705.blurb:
Medal day begins with semifinals — the final hurdle before gold and bronze matches. Two quarterfinal winners face off in each semi, with victors advancing to the evening's championship and losers dropping to the bronze-medal game.

New Zealand, defending their Paris 2024 gold, could meet Canada or Australia here in a rematch of their previous podium finishes. Classification matches for 5th-8th and placing finals for 9th-12th run alongside the semis, completing the tournament bracket for all 12 teams.

Afternoon starts follow a rest day on July 14 that allows athletes to attend opening ceremonies. Carson Stadium fills with fans anticipating the evening medal matches, building energy as the tournament narrows to its final four contenders.
RU705.potentialContendersIntro: New Zealand (Paris 2024 gold), Canada (silver), and Australia (bronze) are projected to reach the semifinals based on recent form, though semifinal matchups depend on quarterfinal outcomes.

RU706.blurb:
The women's Rugby Sevens champions will be crowned in the evening's gold medal match, capping four days of pool play and knockouts. The bronze medal game precedes it, giving semifinal losers one final shot at the podium.

New Zealand seeks to defend their Paris 2024 title, while Canada aims to upgrade from silver and Australia from bronze — if all three reach the medal rounds, expect physical, tactical battles that showcase the sport's explosive speed and strategic depth. Placing finals for 5th-8th open the session, ensuring every team finishes with a defined rank.

Night matches under Carson Stadium's lights bring peak atmosphere. The compact venue amplifies roars from the crowd as medals are decided, and winners circle the pitch in celebration just meters from the stands.
RU706.potentialContendersIntro: New Zealand (Paris 2024 gold), Canada (silver), and Australia (bronze) are the likeliest medal contenders, though final matchups depend on semifinal results earlier in the day.

RU707.blurb:
The men's tournament launches the morning after women's finals, with 12 teams divided into three pools for the opening session of round-robin play. France, Argentina, and New Zealand — the Paris 2024 medal trio — are expected to begin their campaigns here in six fast, physical matches.

Pool results shape quarterfinal brackets, so dominant performances now can mean easier knockout draws later. Sevens' 14-minute format keeps the action relentless — breakaway tries, desperate tackles, and momentum swings that flip scoreboards in under a minute.

Carson Stadium's open-air design and steep stands bring fans close to the collisions. Morning kickoffs keep temperatures manageable for three hours of nonstop rugby as the men's medal race begins.
RU707.potentialContendersIntro: France (Paris 2024 gold), Argentina (silver), and New Zealand (bronze) are projected to lead the men's field, though final rosters depend on qualification.

RU708.blurb:
Pool play continues into the afternoon with the second round of men's group-stage matches. Teams that won in the morning look to lock down quarterfinal spots, while losers face elimination pressure with just one pool match remaining.

France, Argentina, and New Zealand — the Paris 2024 podium — could all appear depending on pool draws, potentially facing off in group-stage previews of later knockout battles. Six matches rotate through Carson Stadium's single pitch, maintaining the tournament's frenetic pace.

Late afternoon starts bring bigger crowds and cooler air as the session stretches into evening. The stadium's modular seating keeps sightlines intimate even with capacity expansions for the Games.
RU708.potentialContendersIntro: The Paris 2024 medalists — France (gold), Argentina (silver), and New Zealand (bronze) — are likely to feature in this session's pool matches if they compete.

RU709.blurb:
The final round of men's pool play determines quarterfinal seeding and which four teams exit without reaching the knockout stage. With group standings finalized after these six matches, expect tactical kicking, desperation tries, and all-out efforts to secure favorable quarterfinal matchups.

France, Argentina, and New Zealand — the Paris 2024 medal winners — will likely have quarterfinal spots secured, but their final pool positions decide whether they face weaker opponents or immediate heavyweight clashes in the next round. One bad result can derail a medal run.

Morning starts mean fresh legs and crisp execution before the heat builds. Carson Stadium's proximity to the pitch amplifies every big hit and referee call as pool play concludes and the knockout bracket locks in.
RU709.potentialContendersIntro: Paris 2024's top three — France (gold), Argentina (silver), and New Zealand (bronze) — are expected to navigate pool play, with exact seeding depending on these final group results.

RU710.blurb:
The men's knockout stage begins with single-elimination quarterfinals — four matches where the winners advance to semifinals and the losers drop into 5th-8th classification. Medal hopes end here for half the remaining field.

France, Argentina, and New Zealand — the Paris 2024 podium — are projected to reach this round, with potential quarterfinal clashes that could eliminate medal favorites before the semis. Classification 9-12 matches open the session, giving teams that didn't escape pool play a final chance to finish strong.

Afternoon crowds bring heightened energy as stakes escalate. Carson Stadium's steep seating puts fans right on the sideline, close enough to hear the calls and feel the bone-rattling tackles that decide who survives to medal day.
RU710.potentialContendersIntro: Paris 2024 medalists France (gold), Argentina (silver), and New Zealand (bronze) are expected to contend in the quarterfinals, though pairings depend on pool results.

RU711.blurb:
Medal day begins with the men's semifinals — the final step before gold and bronze matches. Two quarterfinal winners meet in each semi, with victors advancing to the evening's championship and losers dropping to the bronze-medal game.

France (Paris 2024 gold), Argentina (silver), and New Zealand (bronze) could face off here in rematches of their previous podium finishes, though Fiji — fifth in Paris — also competes in the SVNS Series and could appear if they qualify. Classification 5-8 semis and 9-12 placing finals complete the bracket for all 12 teams.

Afternoon starts build energy toward the evening medal matches. Carson Stadium fills with fans anticipating the night's championship as the tournament narrows to its final four medal contenders.
RU711.potentialContendersIntro: France (Paris 2024 gold), Argentina (silver), and New Zealand (bronze) are projected to reach the semifinals based on recent form, though matchups depend on quarterfinal outcomes.

RU712.blurb:
The men's Rugby Sevens champions will be crowned in the evening's gold medal match, concluding the tournament. The bronze medal game precedes it, giving semifinal losers one final shot at the podium and ending four days of pool play and knockouts.

France seeks to defend their Paris 2024 title, while Argentina and New Zealand aim to improve on their silver and bronze finishes — if all three reach the medal rounds, expect tactical, physical battles showcasing sevens' explosive speed and strategic depth. Placing finals for 5th-8th open the session, ensuring every team finishes with a defined rank.

Night matches under Carson Stadium's lights bring peak atmosphere. The compact venue amplifies crowd roars as medals are decided, and winners circle the pitch in celebration just meters from the stands.
RU712.potentialContendersIntro: France (Paris 2024 gold), Argentina (silver), and New Zealand (bronze) are the likeliest medal contenders, though final matchups depend on semifinal results earlier in the day.

SAL01.blurb:
Kiteboarding makes its opening statement at Belmont Shore as the men's and women's Formula Kite qualification series begins. Competitors race high-speed foiling boards across multi-day preliminaries, building points toward the final.

The format rewards consistency over three days of racing, with wind conditions off Long Beach determining strategy. Athletes navigate shifting Pacific breezes while balancing risk and placement.

Watch from the sandy shore with unobstructed ocean views as foils lift riders above the water at speeds exceeding 30 knots. The Long Beach waterfront provides accessible vantage points for this fast-paced opening session.
SAL01.potentialContendersIntro: Paris 2024 medalists anchor the projected field, with Austria's Valentin Bontus defending gold and France's Lauriane Nolot likely returning after her 2024 victory.

potentialContenders.name: Valentin Bontus
potentialContenders.note: Paris 2024 gold medalist in men's Formula Kite

potentialContenders.name: Toni Vodišek
potentialContenders.note: Paris 2024 silver medalist, leads 2025 world rankings

potentialContenders.name: Lauriane Nolot
potentialContenders.note: Paris 2024 gold medalist, defended title in 2025 events

potentialContenders.name: Julia Karla Moura Santos
potentialContenders.note: Paris 2024 silver medalist in women's Formula Kite

SAL02.blurb:
Day one of windsurfing qualifiers joins day two of kite racing in a double-header session on the Pacific. The iQFoil class debuts its preliminary round as Formula Kite athletes continue building their qualification standings.

Windsurfers launch into their own multi-day series while kite racers complete their second set of heats. Both disciplines feature foiling technology that lifts boards above the surface, creating dramatic racing across Belmont Shore's course.

The extended six-hour window accommodates shifting afternoon sea breezes. Spectators along the Long Beach waterfront can track both events simultaneously as boards crisscross the offshore racing area.
SAL02.potentialContendersIntro: Windsurfing could see defending champions Luuc van Opzeeland and Sharon Kantor if they qualify, while kite contenders continue from day one.

potentialContenders.name: Luuc van Opzeeland
potentialContenders.note: Paris 2024 iQFoil gold medalist, remains active in world rankings

SAL03.blurb:
Midweek qualifiers advance both disciplines as windsurfers enter day two and kiteboarders complete their third and final preliminary session. Points accumulated across these races determine who advances to medal contention.

The dual-event format creates continuous action on the water, with athletes rotating through heats based on wind conditions. Kite racers face their last opportunity to position themselves for the finals while windsurfers build momentum through their series.

Belmont Shore's beach setup offers close viewing as athletes launch and return between races. The Pacific backdrop frames the afternoon competition with Southern California's coastal landscape visible behind the course.
SAL03.potentialContendersIntro: Paris 2024 podium finishers from both events could appear if they qualify, with the kite field finalizing its top 10 for medal races.

potentialContenders.name: Toni Vodišek
potentialContenders.note: Paris 2024 kite silver medalist, 2025 world rankings leader

potentialContenders.name: Lauriane Nolot
potentialContenders.note: Paris 2024 women's kite gold medalist, 2025 title defender

potentialContenders.name: Luuc van Opzeeland
potentialContenders.note: Paris 2024 windsurfing gold medalist, active through 2025

SAL04.blurb:
The first medals of sailing competition are decided as Formula Kite finals crown champions while windsurfers complete their third day of qualifiers. Kite racers condensed to the top 10 face two single-point races where any mistake can cost a podium position.

The compressed-points format erases qualification advantages, making the final a tactical sprint. Athletes must balance aggressive positioning with risk management as the Pacific wind determines who claims gold.

Finals command the premium price point for good reason—medal races deliver concentrated drama compared to the longer qualification series. The Long Beach shore provides prime viewing as champions are crowned against the ocean backdrop.
SAL04.potentialContendersIntro: Paris 2024 gold medalists Valentin Bontus and Lauriane Nolot could vie for repeat titles if they qualify, with Slovenia's Toni Vodišek a strong challenger based on 2025 form.

potentialContenders.name: Valentin Bontus
potentialContenders.note: Paris 2024 men's Formula Kite gold medalist

potentialContenders.name: Toni Vodišek
potentialContenders.note: Paris 2024 silver medalist, leads 2025 world rankings

potentialContenders.name: Lauriane Nolot
potentialContenders.note: Paris 2024 women's gold medalist, defended title in 2025

SAL05.blurb:
Windsurfing crowns its champions as men's and women's iQFoil finals close the board events at Belmont Shore. The top 10 from qualifiers race twice under compressed points, where a single race can swing from bronze to gold.

Three days of preliminaries narrow the field, but the final format resets the competition. Athletes navigate tactical decisions about when to attack and when to protect position as wind shifts create opportunities.

The shorter three-hour window concentrates the action compared to earlier sessions. Spectators watch from the shore as the final boards cross the line to decide the first windsurfing medals of the 2028 Games.
SAL05.potentialContendersIntro: Paris 2024 champions Luuc van Opzeeland and Sharon Kantor are projected to compete if they qualify, with Kantor having publicly targeted the 2028 Games.

potentialContenders.name: Sharon Kantor
potentialContenders.note: Paris 2024 women's gold medalist, targeting 2028 Games

potentialContenders.name: Luuc van Opzeeland
potentialContenders.note: Paris 2024 men's gold medalist, no retirement announced as of 2026

potentialContenders.name: Yu Kuwahara
potentialContenders.note: Paris 2024 women's silver medalist, competing in post-2024 events

SAL06.blurb:
The 2028 sailing program opens with a double-header dinghy qualifier in the Port of Los Angeles, where men sail ILCA 7 boats and women race ILCA 6s through a marathon six-hour window. Dinghies are one-person boats requiring tactical positioning and wind-reading across a series of short races that set the field for medal finals later in the week.

Racers zigzag between buoys in the harbor's industrial backdrop, jockeying for inside lanes and wind advantage on every leg. The qualification series is a war of attrition — consistency across multiple races matters more than a single brilliant showing.

Waterfront stands at the Port of Los Angeles offer sightlines across the racecourse, with sail numbers visible on a giant screen and boats streaming past in clusters. The open Pacific breeze and working harbor setting make for an unconventional but distinctly Southern California venue.
SAL06.potentialContendersIntro: Men's and Women's Dinghy fields are still taking shape, but recent world champions and Paris medalists are projected to compete if they qualify through continental trials.

potentialContenders.name: Matt Wearn
potentialContenders.note: Gold medalist in Men's Dinghy at Paris 2024; two-time world champion in ILCA 7 class.

potentialContenders.name: Pavlos Kontides
potentialContenders.note: Silver medalist in Men's Dinghy at Paris 2024; veteran of four Games.

potentialContenders.name: Stefano Peschiera
potentialContenders.note: Bronze medalist in Men's Dinghy at Paris 2024.

potentialContenders.name: Sarah-Louise Stökken
potentialContenders.note: Gold medalist in Women's Dinghy at Paris 2024.

potentialContenders.name: Line Flem Høst
potentialContenders.note: Silver medalist in Women's Dinghy at Paris 2024.

SAL07.blurb:
Three classes share the harbor on a packed midweek slate — Men's and Women's Dinghy pushing into Day 2 of their qualification series, while Mixed Dinghy (470 class) and Mixed Multihull (Nacra 17) open their campaigns.

Dinghy sailors thread tactical puzzles through shifting winds and tidal currents in the outer harbor. The 470s demand synchronized teamwork between helmsman and crew, while the Nacra 17 catamarans fly across the water on foils at startling speeds.

Six hours of racing means constant action as fleets rotate through the course. Grab a harbor-side seat and watch sails pop against the San Pedro skyline — this is sailing's everyday grind, where consistency across multiple races separates contenders from the field.
SAL07.potentialContendersIntro: Paris 2024 medalists are likely anchors if they return, with reigning world champions and recent podium finishers projected to compete across all three classes.

potentialContenders.name: Mat Belcher
potentialContenders.note: Gold medalist in Men's 470 at Paris 2024, three-time world champion
