potentialContenders.name: Sarah Steyaert
potentialContenders.note: Gold medalist in Women's Dinghy at Paris 2024

potentialContenders.name: John Gimson / Anna Burnet
potentialContenders.note: Silver medalists in Mixed Multihull Nacra 17 at Paris 2024

potentialContenders.name: Ruggero Tita / Caterina Banti
potentialContenders.note: Gold medalists in Mixed Multihull Nacra 17 at Paris 2024, back-to-back world champions

SAL08.blurb:
The most crowded session of the week: four dinghy and multihull classes continue their series, and Men's Skiff (49er) and Women's Skiff (49erFX) join the fray. Skiffs are high-performance two-person dinghies with asymmetric spinnakers and trapeze systems — sailors hike out on wires to balance the boat at speed, creating acrobatic racing that's both technical and physical.

Spain's gold-medal crew from Paris 2024 is projected to return in the Men's 49er, along with New Zealand's silver medalists and the American bronze crew. Qualification series points accumulate across multiple days, so early consistency is critical.

Six racecourses fill the harbor with sail traffic, making for a sensory spectacle — hundreds of sails in white, red, and national colors zigzagging through the Port of Los Angeles basin. Announcers juggle updates across disciplines while scoreboards flash results from overlapping heats.
SAL08.potentialContendersIntro: Men's Skiff contenders are expected to include the Paris 2024 medalists, all of whom could return for 2028.

potentialContenders.name: Diego Botín / Florian Trittel
potentialContenders.note: Gold medalists in Men's Skiff at Paris 2024.

potentialContenders.name: Isaac McHardie / William McKenzie
potentialContenders.note: Silver medalists in Men's Skiff at Paris 2024.

potentialContenders.name: Ian Barrows / Hans Henken
potentialContenders.note: Bronze medalists in Men's Skiff at Paris 2024.

SAL09.blurb:
The first medals of the 2028 sailing program are decided as Men's and Women's Dinghy hold their finals — a single medal race where only the top ten qualifiers compete and points are doubled. One race determines the podium, turning the entire qualification series into a high-stakes prologue.

Meanwhile, skiff and mixed boat classes grind through their penultimate qualification rounds in the background, setting the stage for their own medal races later in the week. The harbor buzzes with overlapping drama as dinghy finalists race for gold while others fight for qualifying spots.

Final races are scheduled for prime afternoon wind, and the compressed field of ten boats makes for tight tactical battles. Waterfront crowds watch medal ceremonies unfold dockside immediately after racing concludes.
SAL09.potentialContendersIntro: Men's and Women's Dinghy finals are expected to feature Paris 2024 medalists if they qualify through the series.

potentialContenders.name: Matt Wearn
potentialContenders.note: Reigning gold medalist in Men's Dinghy from Paris 2024; projected favorite if he competes.

potentialContenders.name: Pavlos Kontides
potentialContenders.note: Silver medalist at Paris 2024; veteran campaigner in ILCA 7 class.

potentialContenders.name: Sarah-Louise Stökken
potentialContenders.note: Paris 2024 gold medalist in Women's Dinghy.

potentialContenders.name: Line Flem Høst
potentialContenders.note: Paris 2024 silver medalist in Women's Dinghy.

SAL10.blurb:
Two more medal races: Mixed Dinghy (470) and Mixed Multihull (Nacra 17) crown their champions in back-to-back finals. The 470 rewards crew synergy and tactical smarts; the Nacra 17 is raw speed and boat-handling under pressure. Both are double-points medal races where the top ten crews from qualifying compete for podium positions.

Men's and Women's Skiff sailors complete their third and final day of qualification, jockeying for spots in their own medal races on Friday. The harbor hums with the sound of skiff crews calling wind shifts and trimming sails at speed.

Afternoon sun and building breeze create ideal racing conditions. Spectators watch multihulls accelerate on foils and mixed dinghy crews execute synchronized maneuvers, then witness immediate podium celebrations as medalists step ashore.
SAL10.potentialContendersIntro: Mixed Dinghy and Mixed Multihull fields are expected to include Paris 2024 medalists and top-ranked crews from recent world championships.

SAL11.blurb:
The 2028 sailing program concludes with a doubleheader of skiff medal races — Men's 49er and Women's 49erFX finals back-to-back. Spain's Paris 2024 gold crew is projected to compete in the Men's race if they qualify, along with New Zealand's silver team and the American bronze duo. Ten crews per final, double points, one race for all the marbles.

Skiff racing is the most physically demanding discipline in the program: crews hike out on trapeze wires, hoist and drop spinnakers at speed, and execute rapid tacks and gybes in a high-stakes choreography. The boats plane and surf downwind, hitting 20 knots in strong breeze.

The Port of Los Angeles atmosphere is festive as the sailing community gathers for the final medals of the regatta. Wind, strategy, and crew execution collide in a three-hour window that caps nearly two weeks of racing.
SAL11.potentialContendersIntro: Men's Skiff is expected to feature the Paris 2024 medalists if they qualify, while the Women's field includes reigning world champions and recent podium finishers.

potentialContenders.name: Diego Botín / Florian Trittel
potentialContenders.note: Reigning gold medalists in Men's Skiff from Paris 2024.

potentialContenders.name: Isaac McHardie / William McKenzie
potentialContenders.note: Silver medalists in Men's Skiff at Paris 2024.

potentialContenders.name: Ian Barrows / Hans Henken
potentialContenders.note: Bronze medalists in Men's Skiff at Paris 2024; racing on home waters.

SHO01.blurb:
The Games open with rifle and pistol's first medals as women and men contest 10m Air Rifle from qualification through finals in one continuous session. Sixty shooters per gender begin qualification in the morning; the top eight advance to elimination finals where single-shot accuracy decides podium positions.

This discipline demands absolute stillness—competitors fire .177 caliber pellets at targets the size of a pinhead from 10 meters away, standing unsupported. The slightest tremor costs points; finals are decided by tenths.

The temporary range inside Long Beach's waterfront convention center holds over 13,000 spectators in tiered seating overlooking firing lines. The indoor arena muffles rifle reports to soft pops, creating an unusually quiet atmosphere where you can hear the metallic click of pellet loading and the collective inhale before each shot.
SHO01.potentialContendersIntro: China dominated both genders in Paris—Huang Yuting took women's gold and Sheng Lihao men's—and could field similarly strong shooters in 2028 if current form holds.

potentialContenders.name: Huang Yuting
potentialContenders.note: Paris 2024 gold medalist; 19 years old in Paris, would be 23 in 2028

SHO02.blurb:
Air pistol finals follow the same qualification-to-medal structure as air rifle, but with a distinct technical challenge: shooters hold a 1.5 kg pistol at arm's length, firing one-handed with no support. Qualification begins at 1:45 PM; finals cap the evening.

The 10m distance remains, but the scoring zone shrinks and the body mechanics shift entirely. Elite shooters achieve heart rates below 60 beats per minute between shots, using breath control and mental discipline honed over thousands of hours.

Long Beach's indoor setup brings the crowd unusually close to the action compared to outdoor ranges. Finals unfold under broadcast lighting with live scoring displays—watch competitors' point totals update instantly after each pellet strikes the electronic target downrange.
SHO02.potentialContendersIntro: South Korea swept women's medals in Paris with Oh Ye Jin claiming gold, while Serbia's Damir Mikec topped the men's podium. Both nations consistently produce world-class pistol shooters.

SHO03.blurb:
The opening day of skeet qualification brings the world's finest shotgun athletes to Whittier Narrows, where shooters engage clay targets at high speed from eight different stations. Over five hours, competitors fire at orange discs launched at 60 mph, with precision and consistency determining who advances to the finals.

Qualification unfolds across the rolling green landscape of the Whittier Narrows Recreation Area, with athletes rotating through stations in a rhythm of calls, launches, and shots. The morning session features women's qualifiers, followed by men's rounds in the afternoon.

Spectators watch from elevated viewing areas with unobstructed sightlines across the open-air range, close enough to hear the crack of each shot and see targets shatter mid-flight against the Southern California sky.
SHO03.potentialContendersIntro: Paris 2024 gold medalist Austen Smith returns as a likely contender in women's skeet, while reigning world champion Vincent Hancock could anchor the U.S. men's squad if he qualifies.

potentialContenders.name: Vincent Hancock
potentialContenders.note: Four-time Games gold medalist, projected to compete if he qualifies at age 39

SHO04.blurb:
Mixed team events pair one woman and one man per nation in both 10m Air Rifle and 10m Air Pistol, with both disciplines contested back-to-back in a single afternoon and evening session. Teams of two combine scores across qualification, then the top four pairs advance to medal matches.

The 2028 format change brings all four semifinalist teams into one unified final rather than split gold and bronze matches, intensifying the pressure. Teammates alternate shots in a rapid-fire sequence where communication and mutual trust prove as vital as marksmanship.

This session offers the broadest variety of the shooting program—you'll see rifle shooters firing from standing platforms, then pistol athletes in one-handed stance, all within hours. The crowd witnesses four medals awarded before the evening concludes.
SHO04.potentialContendersIntro: China claimed both mixed team golds in Paris with different pairings (Huang/Sheng in rifle, Li/Xie in pistol), establishing themselves as the nation to beat if they field similar depth in 2028.

potentialContenders.name: Huang Yuting / Sheng Lihao
potentialContenders.note: Paris 2024 Mixed Team 10m Air Rifle gold medalists

potentialContenders.name: Alexandra Le / Islam Satpayev
potentialContenders.note: Paris 2024 Mixed Team 10m Air Rifle silver medalists

potentialContenders.name: Li Xue / Xie Yu
potentialContenders.note: Paris 2024 Mixed Team 10m Air Pistol gold medalists

potentialContenders.name: Manu Bhaker / Sarabjot Singh
potentialContenders.note: Paris 2024 Mixed Team 10m Air Pistol bronze medalists

SHO05.blurb:
Finals day arrives after two days of qualification, with medal rounds deciding men's and women's skeet champions. The top six from each discipline face off in a newly expanded eight-competitor final format, shooting head-to-head elimination rounds where a single miss can end medal hopes.

The session opens with morning qualifiers wrapping up, then transitions to afternoon finals where pressure mounts with each station. The updated format accelerates pacing, creating a faster, more intense spectacle than previous Games cycles.

The finals unfold against the Whittier Narrows hills, with afternoon light casting long shadows across the range as shooters battle for podium positions in front of packed stands.
SHO05.potentialContendersIntro: Paris 2024 champions Austen Smith and Vincent Hancock could defend their titles if they advance, facing strong challenges from Italy's medalists and emerging shooters.

potentialContenders.name: Austen Smith
potentialContenders.note: Paris 2024 women's skeet gold medalist, projected for finals if qualified

potentialContenders.name: Vincent Hancock
potentialContenders.note: Paris 2024 men's skeet gold medalist, likely finalist if he qualifies

SHO06.blurb:
The men's 50m Rifle 3 Positions compresses the sport's full technical repertoire into one grueling qualification followed by a standing-only final. Qualification runs three hours as shooters fire 40 rounds each in kneeling, prone, and standing—120 shots total at a target half the size of a tennis ball from 50 meters.

The 2028 format change eliminates position rotation in finals: the top eight finalists compete exclusively in standing position, the most unstable and demanding stance. This shifts the final from endurance test to pure precision under pressure.

Morning sessions at Long Beach capture natural light filtering through the convention center's high windows. The 50-meter range stretches the full length of the arena floor, giving spectators in upper tiers an eagle's-eye view of the complete firing line and target array.
SHO06.potentialContendersIntro: China's Liu Yukun won gold in Paris, while Norway's Jon-Hermann Hegg took silver. The standing-only final format could favor specialists in that position over all-around competitors.

SHO07.blurb:
Women's 50m Rifle 3 Positions mirrors the men's schedule and format: morning qualification through all three positions, then a standing-only final to decide medals. The event tests every aspect of rifle shooting—muscular endurance in kneeling, stability in prone, balance in standing.

Qualification scores reset for finals, meaning the day's leader after 120 shots starts the medal round on equal footing with the eighth qualifier. Finals compress into 45 elimination shots where the last-place shooter is cut after every second shot until three remain for the medal series.

The Long Beach venue's downtown waterfront location puts you steps from the Pacific after the session concludes. Inside, the arena's air conditioning and controlled lighting create laboratory conditions for a sport where environmental variables—wind, temperature, humidity—typically factor heavily into outdoor results.
SHO07.potentialContendersIntro: Switzerland's Chiara Leone won Paris gold at age 26; India's Sift Kaur Samra took silver. The standing-only final format introduced in 2028 adds a new variable to legacy results.

SHO08.blurb:
Trap qualification begins with shooters facing one of shotgun sports' most demanding disciplines. Athletes fire at clay targets launched away from them at varying angles, requiring split-second reads and flawless tracking across 75 targets per round.

The day splits into women's and men's sessions, each building toward the six finalists who will compete for medals. Consistency matters more than flash—one missed target can derail an entire campaign at this level.

The Whittier Narrows setting offers spectators views across multiple trap fields simultaneously, with the distinctive sound of shotguns echoing across the recreation area's open terrain.
SHO08.potentialContendersIntro: Paris 2024 gold medalists Adriana Ruano Oliva and Nathan Hales could return if qualified, while Italy's Silvana Stanco seeks to build on her silver medal performance.

SHO09.blurb:
The precision stage opens Women's 25m Pistol qualification with slow-fire accuracy: five-second exposure windows where a target rotates to face the shooter, who must raise, aim, and fire before it turns away. Thirty shots across six five-shot series build the first half of each competitor's qualification total.

This discipline uses .22 caliber sport pistols at 25 meters, doubling the air pistol distance. The precision stage rewards absolute steadiness and trigger control—competitors seek near-perfect centering on a bullseye roughly the diameter of a quarter.

Morning preliminary sessions offer the most accessible entry to elite shooting. Qualification runs until noon with multiple firing relays, giving spectators a window into the technical preparation and mental routines world-class athletes employ before the rapid fire stage the following day determines final advancement.
SHO09.potentialContendersIntro: South Korea's Yang Jiin won Paris gold, continuing the nation's 25m pistol dominance. The precision stage is the first of two qualification components, with rapid fire to follow.

SHO10.blurb:
Medal day delivers trap finals across both men's and women's fields, with the expanded eight-shooter format raising stakes from the jump. Morning qualifiers complete the field before afternoon finals transform Whittier Narrows into a pressure cooker where decades of training meet single-elimination reality.

The finals feature progressive elimination rounds, each miss potentially fatal to medal chances. Shooters work through stations with crowds pressed close to the action, creating an atmosphere more intense than any qualification round.

Late afternoon shadows stretch across the range as gold medal rounds decide champions, with Southern California's dry heat and open skies framing the climactic showdown.
SHO10.potentialContendersIntro: Paris 2024 champions Nathan Hales and Adriana Ruano Oliva could defend their titles if they reach finals, facing challengers from Italy, China, and Spain's emerging trap programs.

potentialContenders.name: Nathan Hales
potentialContenders.note: Paris 2024 men's trap gold medalist, likely finalist if qualified

potentialContenders.name: Adriana Ruano Oliva
potentialContenders.note: Paris 2024 women's trap gold medalist, projected for finals

SHO11.blurb:
Qualification concludes with the rapid fire stage—three-second target exposures demanding instant reaction and precision—before the top eight advance to the afternoon final. Combined precision and rapid scores determine qualification rank; finals reset the scoreboard.

The final itself alternates rapid (three-second) and precision (five-second) series across multiple rounds, cutting the last-place shooter periodically until the medal positions are decided. It's the only pistol event mixing two distinct timing formats within a single competition.

Afternoon sessions at Long Beach capture the progression from tense qualification finishes through the methodical final where every shot appears on arena scoreboards instantly. The rapid-fire sequences produce a distinctive staccato rhythm as eight shooters fire in quick succession, their reports echoing through the indoor hall.
SHO11.potentialContendersIntro: Yang Jiin's Paris gold came from superior rapid fire execution after a competitive precision stage. Hungary and France also medaled, suggesting depth across European and Asian programs.

SHO12.blurb:
Mixed team trap wraps the shotgun program with nations pairing one male and one female shooter for a same-day qualification and final. The format compresses the entire competition into one marathon session, testing both individual skill and team coordination under pressure.

Qualification runs through the morning with teams firing in tandem, their combined scores determining the four finalists. The afternoon final uses an integrated format where all qualified teams compete together rather than separate medal matches, creating a single continuous showdown.

The session caps a week of shooting at Whittier Narrows, with teams representing their countries in the discipline's only mixed-gender event before the venue returns to its role as Southern California's premier clay shooting facility.
SHO12.potentialContendersIntro: Italy's trap program could field a strong mixed team if Silvana Stanco qualifies, while the U.S., China, and Great Britain are projected to compete based on their individual trap depth.

potentialContenders.name: Silvana Stanco / Giovanni Pellielo
potentialContenders.note: Italy's individual medalists could form a strong pairing if both qualify

potentialContenders.name: Adriana Ruano Oliva / Jean Pierre Brol Cardenas
potentialContenders.note: Paris 2024 individual medalists from Guatemala, likely team if qualified

potentialContenders.name: USA Mixed Team
potentialContenders.note: Projected contenders based on strong U.S. trap program depth

potentialContenders.name: CHN Mixed Team
potentialContenders.note: Expected to field competitive team given Qi Ying's Paris silver

SHO13.blurb:
Men's 25m Rapid Fire Pistol begins its two-stage qualification with Stage 1: shooters engage five targets in succession within tight time limits (eight seconds, six seconds, then four seconds per series). Each target appears once; misses are irretrievable.

The event demands explosive hand-eye coordination married to precision—transitioning between five separate aim points in four seconds leaves roughly 0.8 seconds per shot including target acquisition. Elite competitors develop muscle memory so refined they can execute the sequence nearly identically across dozens of repetitions.

Stage 1 runs through early evening as multiple relays complete their 30-shot strings. The format change for 2028 expands finals to eight shooters instead of six, meaning Stage 1 scores gain importance in securing advancement through the qualifying threshold.
SHO13.potentialContendersIntro: China's Li Yuehong won Paris gold, while Germany's Christian Reitz—a Rio 2016 champion—took silver at age 37. Stage 1 is preliminary; finalists are determined after Stage 2 the following day.

SHO14.blurb:
Stage 2 completes qualification before the field cuts to eight finalists for the closing medal match—the last shooting gold of the Games' rifle and pistol program. Stage 2 mirrors Stage 1's format: rapid engagement of five targets across decreasing time windows, adding 30 shots to each competitor's total.

The expanded eight-shooter final represents a format change from Paris, potentially creating more competitive medal races. Finalists face multiple four-second series in sudden-death elimination rounds where the lowest score after each series exits until three remain.

Evening finals at Long Beach carry special energy as the final rifle and pistol session. The rapid-fire format produces the program's fastest-paced action: watch eight shooters draw, aim, and fire in near-simultaneous waves, their combined reports reverberating through the 13,500-seat arena before electronic targets instantly display hit locations.
SHO14.potentialContendersIntro: Li Yuehong's Paris victory continued China's recent 25m Rapid Fire Pistol strength. The eight-finalist format is new for 2028, potentially opening medal opportunities compared to the six-shooter Paris final.

potentialContenders.name: Christian Reitz
potentialContenders.note: Paris 2024 silver medalist; would be 41 in 2028

SKB01.blurb:
The street prelims kick off the 2028 Games skateboarding competition in the San Fernando Valley, where 22 of the world's top female skaters compete for a spot in the finals. Watch as competitors navigate rails, ledges, and stairs in two timed runs plus five best-trick attempts.

Japan's Coco Yoshizawa and Liz Akama return as Paris 2024 gold and silver medalists, likely to face Brazil's Rayssa Leal—who claimed bronze in Paris and has been a podium fixture since Tokyo. The format rewards creativity and technical precision, with younger skaters continuing to push boundaries that seemed impossible a generation ago.

Valley Complex 1's temporary open-air setup puts you close to the action as boards crack against concrete and the crowd erupts for every landed trick.
SKB01.potentialContendersIntro: Japan's Paris 2024 medalists Coco Yoshizawa (gold) and Liz Akama (silver) are expected to return, along with Brazil's Rayssa Leal, who took bronze in Paris.

SKB02.blurb:
Men's street skateboarding opens with morning prelims under the San Fernando Valley sun, as the world's elite technical skaters battle for finals spots. Two 45-second runs and five solo tricks determine who advances—expect high-amplitude stunts and rail wizardry.

Paris 2024 saw Japan's Yuto Horigome defend his Tokyo title while Americans Jagger Eaton and Nyjah Huston secured silver and bronze. If all three return, the final would feature a rematch of that podium alongside rising international challengers pushing the sport's progression.

The Valley Complex 1 course design emphasizes street-style obstacles—handrails, ledges, gaps—where milliseconds and millimeters separate clean landings from bails.
SKB02.potentialContendersIntro: Yuto Horigome of Japan won gold at Paris 2024, with Americans Jagger Eaton (silver) and Nyjah Huston (bronze) rounding out the podium—all three could compete if they qualify.

potentialContenders.name: Yuto Horigome
potentialContenders.note: Gold medalist at Paris 2024, also won Tokyo 2020

SKB03.blurb:
Gold medals are on the line as the women's street final crowns the world's best technical skater under the Valley lights. Finalists get two 45-second runs and five best-trick attempts—the highest combined score from runs plus two best tricks wins.

Paris 2024's podium was a showcase of youth and skill: Japan's Coco Yoshizawa took gold at just 14, compatriot Liz Akama earned silver, and Brazil's Rayssa Leal—herself a teenage prodigy—claimed bronze. A potential rematch would highlight skateboarding's generational shift, where age is no barrier to greatness.

The temporary venue amplifies every slam and stomp, with spectators mere feet from the course as skaters attempt tricks that redefine what's possible on four wheels.
SKB03.potentialContendersIntro: The Paris 2024 podium featured Japan's Coco Yoshizawa (gold) and Liz Akama (silver), plus Brazil's Rayssa Leal (bronze)—all projected contenders if they return.

potentialContenders.name: Coco Yoshizawa
potentialContenders.note: Paris 2024 gold medalist, was 14 years old

potentialContenders.name: Rayssa Leal
potentialContenders.note: Paris 2024 bronze medalist, Tokyo 2020 silver medalist

SKB04.blurb:
Twenty-two of the world's best street skaters converge on the Valley Complex for the sport's homecoming — a final staged in the heart of Southern California's skate culture. The format rewards creativity and risk: two 45-second runs and five trick attempts, with the four highest scores combined for a medal.

Yuto Horigome claimed back-to-back golds in Tokyo and Paris, cementing his status as the era's defining street skater. Jagger Eaton and Nyjah Huston will push for podium upgrades on home soil if they qualify.

The open-air venue in Sepulveda Basin brings spectators close to the action — rails, ledges, and stairs arranged in urban geometry. Evening light and Valley atmosphere frame one of skateboarding's marquee nights.
SKB04.potentialContendersIntro: Yuto Horigome arrives as the reigning two-time champion, while Jagger Eaton and Nyjah Huston could represent the U.S. if they advance through qualification.

potentialContenders.name: Yuto Horigome
potentialContenders.note: Gold medalist at Tokyo 2020 and Paris 2024

potentialContenders.name: Nyjah Huston
potentialContenders.note: Bronze medalist at Paris 2024, multi-time X Games champion

SKB05.blurb:
The qualification gantlet begins under San Fernando Valley skies as the world's top park skaters chase eight final spots. Three-hour sessions test consistency—skaters get three 45-second runs, best score counts, but only complete runs matter.

Reigning champion Keegan Palmer of Australia headlines a field hunting the magic number to advance, alongside Paris silver medalist Tom Schaar and bronze winner Augusto Akio of Brazil. Expect high-risk aerial tricks off bowl transitions and technical lip work as riders push limits knowing one clean run beats three crashes.

Valley Complex 2 puts you trackside in a compact temporary bowl where you'll hear boards snap against coping and feel the crowd react to every landed trick. Morning session means cooler temps and skaters finding their rhythm early.
SKB05.potentialContendersIntro: Paris 2024's podium finishers are projected to return, with defending champion Palmer leading a competitive field that could include rising talents from the World Skateboarding Tour rankings.

potentialContenders.name: Keegan Palmer
potentialContenders.note: Defended his title at Paris 2024 with a score of 93.11

SKB06.blurb:
Under evening lights, women's park skating kicks off with prelims that could feature the youngest podium chasers in the Games. The same format as the men—three runs, best score advances eight to the final—rewards both technical precision and fearless amplitude.

Paris gold medalist Arisa Trew of Australia could return alongside Japan's Kokona Hiraki and Great Britain's Sky Brown, both Paris medalists who've inspired a generation of skaters. The teenage trend continues with competitors pushing inverted aerials and complex lip combinations that redefine what's possible in women's skateboarding.

Nighttime sessions at Valley Complex 2 bring energy as portable lights illuminate the bowl and the San Fernando Valley cools down. You're close enough to see riders pick their lines and hear the roar when someone sticks a massive McTwist.
SKB06.potentialContendersIntro: Paris 2024's medalists—all teenagers when they competed—are likely to shape the field if they qualify, continuing skateboarding's youth movement into Los Angeles.

potentialContenders.name: Arisa Trew
potentialContenders.note: Paris 2024 gold medalist as a teenager

potentialContenders.name: Sky Brown
potentialContenders.note: Paris 2024 bronze medalist with stated inspiration goals

SKB07.blurb:
Medal night arrives as eight finalists drop into the bowl for three 45-second runs of pure amplitude and technical wizardry. Gold hangs on whoever throws down the cleanest combination of inverted airs, grinds, and transfer tricks when it counts.

Keegan Palmer could become the sport's first three-time park champion if he qualifies, having won Tokyo and defended in Paris with a dominant 93.11. Tom Schaar and Augusto Akio—Paris silver and bronze—represent the depth of talent pushing Palmer, while emerging riders from the World Skateboarding Tour could crash the podium party.

Prime evening slot means packed stands and maximum energy at Valley Complex 2. The temporary bowl becomes a cauldron of noise as riders launch ten feet above the coping and the crowd loses it with every landed 540.
SKB07.potentialContendersIntro: Paris 2024's podium could reassemble, led by back-to-back champion Palmer, though the 18-month World Skateboarding Rankings will determine the final eight who drop in.

potentialContenders.name: Tom Schaar
potentialContenders.note: Paris 2024 silver medalist competing on home soil

SKB08.blurb:
The final medal session crowns a champion in women's park as eight finalists get three runs to land their most progressive tricks. Expect inverted aerials, backside 540s, and technical lip work that's advanced dramatically since the sport's Games debut.

Arisa Trew headlines the projected field after her Paris gold performance, with Kokona Hiraki and Sky Brown potentially returning to defend their Paris silver and bronze. The youth movement continues—these athletes are redefining progression while barely out of their teens, pushing boundaries on what's achievable in the bowl.

Thursday night under lights at Valley Complex 2 delivers the perfect skateboarding atmosphere—intimate bowl setup where every landed trick resonates through the stands. You'll witness history as a new generation writes the next chapter in women's park skating.
SKB08.potentialContendersIntro: Paris 2024's teenage podium—gold medalist Trew, silver medalist Hiraki, and bronze winner Brown—could return if they navigate qualification, though the field remains wide open based on World Skateboarding Tour results.

SQU01.blurb:
Squash arrives at the Games for the first time as the inaugural Round of 16 opens at Universal Studios' iconic Courthouse Square. History unfolds on a purpose-built glass court where 16 elite players per gender begin their quest for the sport's first-ever medals.

Four matches alternate between men's and women's singles in this evening session, showcasing the sport's explosive pace and tactical depth. The single-elimination format means every point carries elimination stakes from the opening serve.

Watch world-class athletes battle in the enclosed all-glass court under Hollywood lights, the famous backlot creating a cinematic atmosphere unlike any other Games venue. Evening start times make this an accessible entry point to experience squash's debut.
SQU01.potentialContendersIntro: The field will include five continental champions per gender and eight qualifiers from May 2028 PSA World Rankings, with no confirmed roster yet available.

SQU02.blurb:
The debut continues with four more Round of 16 clashes under the Universal Studios lights. Single-elimination tension drives every rally as players fight for quarterfinal spots in squash's maiden Games appearance.

Expect a mix of continental champions and top PSA-ranked athletes, with each nation limited to two competitors per gender. The glass-court setup puts every explosive movement and strategic shot on full display for spectators.

The Courthouse Square setting offers a surreal backdrop—world-class sport framed by one of cinema's most recognizable locations. Evening sessions capture the energy of opening rounds without the premium pricing of medal matches.
SQU02.potentialContendersIntro: Rosters remain unconfirmed until June 2028, but continental qualification events through 2027 and May 2028 world rankings will determine the 32-player field.

SQU03.blurb:
Round of 16 action continues on the Hollywood backlot as squash's historic debut unfolds across four more elimination matches. With only eight spots per gender advancing to quarterfinals, the stakes intensify.

The enclosed court amplifies every sound—the thwack of ball on strings, the squeak of shoes, the roar when a point ends. Players must master the glass walls and tight angles while managing the physical grind of best-of-five scoring.

Universal's purpose-built venue transforms a film set into a sporting arena, offering sightlines that capture the athleticism and chess-match strategy that define elite squash. This is your chance to witness the sport's Games baptism up close.
SQU03.potentialContendersIntro: The 16-player draws per gender will feature winners from African Games, Asian Games, Pan-American Games, and European Games alongside PSA-ranked qualifiers.

SQU04.blurb:
The final Round of 16 session completes squash's opening bracket, determining the last quarterfinal spots. Four matches close out the preliminary phase as the field narrows from 16 to 8 per gender.

Qualification pathways spanning three continents and two years converge here, with continental champions facing off against world-ranking qualifiers. The mix creates unpredictable matchups and potential upsets as diverse playing styles collide.

Catch the debut sport in its most accessible session before quarterfinal pricing kicks in. The Courthouse Square backdrop adds Hollywood glamour to athletic drama, making this a uniquely Los Angeles experience.
SQU04.potentialContendersIntro: Final rosters will be confirmed by late June 2028 after qualification tournaments conclude, with entries prioritizing continental winners over world rankings.

SQU05.blurb:
Quarterfinal night elevates the intensity as the top eight per gender battle for semifinal berths. Medal contention sharpens into focus with just two rounds remaining in squash's historic debut.

The all-glass court design delivers maximum drama—no blind spots, every angle visible, the physicality of five-game matches laid bare. Expect tactical masterclasses as continental champions and PSA elites adapt strategies mid-match to survive elimination.

Universal Studios' temporary arena transforms into a pressure cooker for four high-stakes matches. Evening sessions under lights heighten the cinematic quality of squash's biggest stage yet.
SQU05.potentialContendersIntro: Quarterfinal matchups will be determined by Round of 16 results, likely featuring the highest PSA-ranked qualifiers and strong continental champions.

SQU06.blurb:
The second quarterfinal session completes the bracket, sending four more athletes per gender into semifinal contention. With medals just one win away after tonight, every rally carries medal-round implications.

Squash's best-of-five format tests endurance and mental toughness across 11-point games decided by two-point margins. The glass court's intimate confines mean spectators feel every momentum swing as players push toward exhaustion.

This is peak elimination drama before the medal sessions—quarterfinal stakes at a more accessible price point than finals. The Hollywood backlot setting ensures even preliminary rounds feel like main events.
SQU06.potentialContendersIntro: The final four quarterfinal spots will be decided by single-elimination outcomes from the Round of 16, with rosters unconfirmed until June 2028.

SQU07.blurb:
The first women's medals in Games squash history will be decided tomorrow—tonight, the semifinal determines who competes for gold and bronze. Two matches separate four elite athletes from the podium in this groundbreaking session.

Expect the world's top-ranked women, projected to include PSA leaders from the May 2028 cutoff, battling in best-of-five clashes. The glass court showcases the sport's speed and precision, with winners advancing to Sunday's medal matches.

Universal's purpose-built venue offers front-row views of history as squash crowns its first-ever medalists. The Courthouse Square backdrop adds cinematic flair to athletic milestone moments.
SQU07.potentialContendersIntro: Semifinalists will emerge from quarterfinals, likely including top PSA-ranked women and continental champions who dominated qualification pathways through 2027-28.

SQU08.blurb:
Men's semifinal night sets the stage for Monday's medal matches as four survivors compete for gold and bronze positions. This is squash's penultimate session in its debut Games, with history one match away.

The highest-ranked men from May 2028 PSA standings are projected to reach this stage, bringing explosive power and tactical depth. Winners play for gold; semifinal losers meet for bronze in a format that guarantees competitive medal matches.

Catch the last chance to see squash before finals pricing as the sport's elite deliver peak performance under Hollywood lights. The glass-court setting amplifies the athletic spectacle against Universal's iconic backlot.
SQU08.potentialContendersIntro: Men's semifinalists will be determined by quarterfinal outcomes, expected to feature PSA top-ranked players and dominant continental qualifiers.

SQU09.blurb:
Squash makes history as the first women's medals are awarded in the sport's Games debut. Bronze and gold matches bookend the evening, crowning the inaugural champions and podium finishers.

The bronze match opens with semifinal losers battling for third place, followed by the gold-medal final between the top two survivors. Best-of-five scoring means every game could shift medal outcomes in this single-elimination climax.

Witness a genuine first—no prior champions exist in Games squash. The glass court at Universal Studios frames athletic excellence against Hollywood's most famous film set, a fitting stage for pioneering moments.
SQU09.potentialContendersIntro: Medal contenders will be the top four women from semifinals, projected to include the highest PSA-ranked athletes and strongest continental qualifiers from 2028 rankings.

SQU10.blurb:
The men's bronze and gold medal matches close squash's historic debut, awarding the sport's first-ever Games medals. Two matches determine the podium in the final session at Universal Studios.

Bronze goes first as semifinal losers compete for third, followed by the championship final between the last two standing. The enclosed glass court intensifies the drama as players leave everything on display in best-of-five battles.

This is squash's coronation moment—the sport's inaugural champions crowned under lights on the Courthouse Square backlot. It's the last chance to see the debut sport that fought decades for this stage.
SQU10.potentialContendersIntro: The final four men will emerge from semifinals, expected to feature top PSA-ranked competitors and continental champions who dominated qualification from 2026-28.

SRF01.blurb:
Surfing opens at one of California's most storied breaks, where 48 competitors launch their medal campaigns across men's and women's Round 1. Lower Trestles delivers consistent, rippable waves that reward both power surfing and technical precision—expect athletic opening statements as surfers navigate the elimination format.

The field mixes reigning champions from Paris 2024 with emerging talents qualified through the World Surfing Games and Championship Tour rankings. Round 1 sets the bracket for the week ahead, with each heat contested in 20-30 minute windows dependent on Pacific swell conditions.

Spectators watch from natural beach vantage points with unobstructed views of the lineup, feeling the salt air and hearing waves break across multiple sections of the point. It's an accessible introduction to elite shortboard surfing in a quintessentially Southern California setting.
SRF01.potentialContendersIntro: Paris 2024 medalists are likely to return if they secure qualification spots through the restructured 2028 system, which caps entries at one surfer per country from the Championship Tour's top five.

potentialContenders.name: Caroline Marks
potentialContenders.note: Paris 2024 gold medalist and host nation representative

potentialContenders.name: Kauli Vaast
potentialContenders.note: Paris 2024 gold medalist, qualified through World Surfing Games

potentialContenders.name: Jack Robinson
potentialContenders.note: Paris 2024 silver medalist, projected CT qualifier

potentialContenders.name: Gabriel Medina
potentialContenders.note: Paris 2024 bronze medalist, three-time world champion

potentialContenders.name: Tatiana Weston-Webb
potentialContenders.note: Paris 2024 silver medalist, if Brazil secures spot

SRF02.blurb:
Round 2 continues the elimination gauntlet as surviving competitors face refined bracket positioning and evolving ocean conditions. The format compresses fields toward quarterfinal spots, intensifying tactical choices about wave selection and risk management on Trestles' shifting peaks.

Surfers who advanced from Round 1 now face opponents with tape to study and adjustments to make—expect sharper performances as athletes dial in equipment and approach. Morning sessions typically offer glassier conditions before afternoon onshore breezes texture the surface.

The beach setting remains intimate and natural, with spectators close enough to track individual maneuvers and hear competitors' boards carving through wave faces. Early rounds offer value for fans wanting extended exposure to the world's best across a full day of heats.
SRF02.potentialContendersIntro: The same field from Round 1 continues, now narrowed and strategically sharpened after opening-round performances.

potentialContenders.name: Johanne Defay
potentialContenders.note: Paris 2024 bronze medalist, experienced in Games pressure

potentialContenders.name: Kauli Vaast
potentialContenders.note: Defending men's champion, if France fields him

potentialContenders.name: Jack Robinson
potentialContenders.note: Paris silver medalist known for powerful rail surfing

SRF03.blurb:
The final preliminary round whittles fields to eight men and eight women, setting quarterfinal matchups for medal day. Round 3 carries the highest stakes of the prelims—lose here and the medal chase ends before the pressure truly peaks.

Trestles' mechanical consistency becomes both ally and opponent: waves arrive predictably, but margins between advancement and elimination shrink to tenths of points. Surfers must maximize every rideable set, balancing aggression with completion rates as judges score on a 10-point scale weighted toward difficulty and execution.

The natural amphitheater setting compresses energy as the field narrows, with fans tracking leaderboards between heats and debating wave priority calls. It's the last chance to watch eliminated contenders before attention shifts entirely to medal rounds.
SRF03.potentialContendersIntro: Quarterfinal spots are decided here, likely featuring a mix of Paris medalists and Championship Tour standouts who've navigated the qualification maze.

potentialContenders.name: Gabriel Medina
potentialContenders.note: Paris bronze medalist, historically clutch in elimination heats

potentialContenders.name: Tatiana Weston-Webb
potentialContenders.note: Paris silver medalist, technical specialist on point breaks

potentialContenders.name: Caroline Marks
potentialContenders.note: Defending champion with local knowledge of Trestles' sections

potentialContenders.name: Jack Robinson
potentialContenders.note: Paris silver medalist, progressive aerial game

SRF04.blurb:
Two gold medals are decided in a marathon finals day that crowns shortboard champions through quarterfinals, semifinals, and medal matches. Lower Trestles becomes center stage as the world's eight best men and eight best women compete in single-elimination heats with no second chances—every wave counts toward podium placement.

Defending champions Caroline Marks and Kauli Vaast could return to defend their Paris 2024 titles if qualification breaks their way, facing challengers like Jack Robinson and Gabriel Medina who narrowly missed gold in Tahiti. The format awards bronze through a third-place match, then stages gold-medal finals under peak afternoon conditions.

Spectators witness the full arc from quarterfinal tension to medal celebrations on the sand, with prime positioning to see every critical maneuver. The natural bowl-shaped beach funnels sound and energy toward the water, creating an amphitheater effect as champions are crowned on one of surfing's most revered waves.
SRF04.potentialContendersIntro: The finals day field will feature Paris 2024 medalists and top-ranked Championship Tour surfers, assuming they've qualified under the one-per-country CT cap and survived preliminary rounds.

potentialContenders.name: Caroline Marks
potentialContenders.note: Paris 2024 gold medalist, defending champion with host advantage

potentialContenders.name: Kauli Vaast
potentialContenders.note: Paris 2024 gold medalist, if France fields him in 2028

potentialContenders.name: Jack Robinson
potentialContenders.note: Paris 2024 silver medalist, projected top CT qualifier

potentialContenders.name: Gabriel Medina
potentialContenders.note: Paris 2024 bronze medalist, three-time world champion

potentialContenders.name: Tatiana Weston-Webb
potentialContenders.note: Paris 2024 silver medalist, technical point-break specialist

SWA01.blurb:
The technical routine launches the duet competition, showcasing precision choreography performed in near-perfect unison. Up to 18 pairs compete in this two-hour prelim, executing prescribed elements—spins, lifts, and formations—within strict time limits.

The U.S. duet enters with automatic host qualification, facing powerhouse programs from China, Japan, and European contenders. Top-ranked pairs from the 2027 World Championships in Budapest will set the technical standard.

Long Beach's outdoor aquatics venue offers intimate sightlines around a 50-meter pool, with morning sun illuminating underwater choreography through crystal-clear water.
SWA01.potentialContendersIntro: China dominated Paris 2024 duet events with Wang Liuyi and Wang Qianyi taking gold in both technical and free routines. Projected contenders for 2028 include reigning champions and emerging pairs from the 2027 World Championships.

potentialContenders.name: Wang Liuyi / Wang Qianyi
potentialContenders.note: Paris 2024 gold medalists in both duet events, could extend their dominance if they continue competing

potentialContenders.name: Kate Shortman / Isabelle Thorpe
potentialContenders.note: Paris 2024 bronze medalists, likely to return as medal contenders

potentialContenders.name: U.S. Duet
potentialContenders.note: Automatically qualified as host nation, expected to field a competitive pair

SWA02.blurb:
Medals are decided in the free routine final, where duets perform four-minute programs blending artistry and athleticism. Pairs showcase original choreography without prescribed elements, judged on synchronization, difficulty, and creative interpretation.

The format combines prelim technical scores with this final performance, rewarding both precision and innovation. China's dominant Paris 2024 pairing could return, though European programs from Great Britain and the Netherlands remain medal threats.

Midday competition brings crowds to the open-air venue, where poolside seating captures every lift and split-second timing adjustment as pairs chase podium positions.
SWA02.potentialContendersIntro: Wang Liuyi and Wang Qianyi swept both duet golds in Paris 2024. The free routine final will determine whether China maintains dominance or new challengers emerge from 2027 World Championships qualification.

potentialContenders.name: Wang Liuyi / Wang Qianyi
potentialContenders.note: Paris 2024 champions in duet free, potential favorites if competing

potentialContenders.name: Kate Shortman / Isabelle Thorpe
potentialContenders.note: Bronze medalists in Paris, strong contenders for podium return

potentialContenders.name: Bregje de Brouwer / Noortje de Brouwer
potentialContenders.note: Consistent European competitors, could challenge for medals

potentialContenders.name: U.S. Duet
potentialContenders.note: Host nation pair aiming to break into medal contention

SWA03.blurb:
Ten eight-person teams launch the open competition with technical routines featuring required elements and precise formations. Teams may include up to two male athletes, continuing the format introduced at Paris 2024 that expanded the sport's traditional boundaries.

China claimed gold across all three team routines in Paris, establishing the benchmark for synchronization and difficulty. Spain, the U.S., and other qualified teams from 2027 Budapest Worlds will challenge Chinese dominance in this 90-minute prelim.

The compact venue amplifies crowd energy as teams execute rapid transitions between surface and underwater sequences, with judges scoring from poolside stations mere feet from the action.
SWA03.potentialContendersIntro: China swept all three open team events at Paris 2024. The technical routine prelim will feature returning champions alongside U.S. host qualifiers and top teams from 2027 World Championships.

potentialContenders.name: China
potentialContenders.note: Paris 2024 gold medalists in all three team events, heavy favorites

potentialContenders.name: United States
potentialContenders.note: Automatically qualified as host, building program momentum

potentialContenders.name: Spain
potentialContenders.note: Consistent European power, projected to compete for medals

potentialContenders.name: Japan
potentialContenders.note: Traditional contender likely to qualify through World Championships

SWA04.blurb:
Teams perform four-minute free routines with creative choreography unconstrained by technical requirements. This second routine combines with technical scores to set the stage for Saturday's acrobatic finale, where medals will ultimately be decided.

The open format allows up to two males per eight-person squad, enabling lifts and formations impossible in traditional all-female teams. China's Paris sweep looms over competitors from Spain, the U.S., and other nations vying to close the scoring gap.

Long Beach's outdoor setting frames morning performances against Southern California coastline views, with shaded seating keeping spectators comfortable through 90 minutes of high-difficulty routines.
SWA04.potentialContendersIntro: China dominated open team free at Paris 2024 as part of their three-routine sweep. Teams from the 2027 qualification cycle will aim to challenge Chinese superiority in this prelim round.

potentialContenders.name: China
potentialContenders.note: Reigning champions in free routine, expected to lead scoring

potentialContenders.name: United States
potentialContenders.note: Host nation looking to improve on past results with home advantage

SWA05.blurb:
The acrobatic routine crowns team champions on Super Saturday, artistic swimming's final session across five days of competition. Teams perform high-risk elements emphasizing lifts, throws, and dynamic formations, with scores combined across all three routines to determine medals.

China's Paris 2024 dominance positions them as favorites, but the acrobatic finale's high difficulty creates upset potential. Ten teams including host U.S. squad and European challengers will chase podium positions in this 105-minute championship session.

Crowds pack the intimate Long Beach venue for artistic swimming's signature moment—the final session coincides with 26 medal events across the Games, creating an electric atmosphere as teams execute their most daring choreography.
SWA05.potentialContendersIntro: China won all three open team golds at Paris 2024, including the acrobatic finale. Medal positions will be determined by combined scores from technical, free, and acrobatic routines performed across three days.

potentialContenders.name: China
potentialContenders.note: Three-time Paris champions, overwhelming favorites for gold

potentialContenders.name: United States
potentialContenders.note: Host nation with automatic qualification, aiming for breakthrough medal

potentialContenders.name: Spain
potentialContenders.note: Silver medal contender based on European championship results
