potentialContenders.note: Traditional power likely competing for bronze position

SWM01.blurb:
Opening morning in the pool brings seven events across heats, introducing two new sprint distances making their 2028 debut. The Men's 50m Butterfly and Women's 50m Backstroke join the program for the first time, showcasing pure speed alongside established races like the 400m Free and relay heats.

These prelims set the stage for the week, with swimmers posting qualifying times across distances from 50 to 400 meters. Both 4x100m Free Relay teams take their first swings, and individual contenders begin their medal campaigns in a wide-open session.

Inside 2028 Stadium, the temporary pool configuration seats nearly 38,000 under a translucent roof that floods the venue with natural light. Morning heats offer an accessible entry point to watch future medalists lay down their opening marks.
SWM01.potentialContendersIntro: Paris 2024 medalists Torri Huske and Gretchen Walsh (USA) in Women's 100m Butterfly, Lukas Märtens (GER) and Ariarne Titmus (AUS) in the 400m Free races, and the reigning relay champions could appear if they qualify for 2028.

SWM02.blurb:
The first finals session crowns four champions and sets semifinal fields in a packed evening card. Gold medals go up in the Men's and Women's 400m Freestyle, the new Men's 50m Butterfly, the new Women's 50m Backstroke, and both 4x100m Free Relays—five finals in two hours.

Lukas Märtens of Germany won the men's 400m Free at Paris 2024; Ariarne Titmus of Australia took the women's. The relay finals could see USA's men and Australia's women defending their Paris titles, if they qualify.

The compact bowl design puts every seat close to the action, and evening sessions draw the loudest crowds. Day one wraps with relay anchors racing for the wall under arena-wide roar.
SWM02.potentialContendersIntro: Paris 2024 champions Lukas Märtens (GER) and Ariarne Titmus (AUS) head the 400m Free fields if they compete. Relay rosters remain open, but USA's men and Australia's women won gold in Paris.

SWM03.blurb:
Day two heats feature four individual events from breaststroke to backstroke to freestyle to the grueling 400m IM. Thomas Ceccon of Italy won the Men's 100m Back at Paris 2024; Sarah Sjöström of Sweden took the Women's 100m Free at age 30.

Léon Marchand of France dominated the 400m IM in Paris and could return as the reigning champion if he competes. These morning swims determine who advances to semifinals and finals over the next 24 hours.

The stadium's sightlines keep the entire pool visible from anywhere in the bowl. Mornings are quieter than evenings, offering a close view of swimmers fine-tuning their race plans.
SWM03.potentialContendersIntro: Paris 2024 winners Tatjana Schoenmaker-Smith (RSA), Thomas Ceccon (ITA), Sarah Sjöström (SWE), and Léon Marchand (FRA) could headline their respective events if they qualify.

SWM04.blurb:
Sunday evening delivers six medal races including the first golds in the new 50m sprint events. Torri Huske won the Women's 100m Butterfly at Paris 2024 in a USA 1-2 finish with Gretchen Walsh; Nicolò Martinenghi of Italy took the Men's 100m Breast.

The Men's 50m Fly and Women's 50m Back finals make their Games debut here—pure speed over a single pool length. Léon Marchand's 400m IM gold also comes up for grabs, and semifinals in three other events keep the session moving.

Evening crowds fill the stadium bowl, and the compact layout amplifies the noise as sprinters explode off the blocks. Six finals in one session means medal ceremonies back-to-back under the translucent roof.
SWM04.potentialContendersIntro: Paris 2024 champions Torri Huske (USA), Nicolò Martinenghi (ITA), and Léon Marchand (FRA) could defend titles if they compete. The new 50m events are open races with no prior Games history.

SWM05.blurb:
Monday morning heats span six events from 50 to 1500 meters, mixing sprints with the marathon Men's 1500m Free. Summer McIntosh of Canada won the Women's 400m IM at Paris 2024; Kaylee McKeown of Australia took the Women's 100m Back; David Popovici of Romania claimed the Men's 200m Free.

The new Men's 50m Breaststroke makes its debut in this session, adding another sprint to the program. Léon Marchand also won the Men's 200m Butterfly in Paris and could appear if he qualifies.

Inglewood mornings bring natural light through the stadium's translucent panels. These prelims sort the field for semifinals and finals, with swimmers racing the clock as much as each other.
SWM05.potentialContendersIntro: Paris 2024 gold medalists Summer McIntosh (CAN), David Popovici (ROU), Kaylee McKeown (AUS), and Léon Marchand (FRA) are potential contenders if they compete in their respective events.

SWM06.blurb:
Four gold medals hang in the balance Monday evening: Women's 100m Free, Men's 100m Back, Women's 100m Breast, and the monumental Women's 400m IM. Sarah Sjöström won the sprint free at Paris 2024; Thomas Ceccon took the backstroke; Tatjana Schoenmaker-Smith the breaststroke; Summer McIntosh the medley.

Semifinals in four other events keep the session dense with racing, including the new Men's 50m Breaststroke. The compact schedule means finals and semis alternate with minimal downtime.

The stadium's bowl design seats 38,000 in close proximity to the deck. Evening sessions draw peak energy, and four medal ceremonies punctuate two hours of elite swimming.
SWM06.potentialContendersIntro: Paris 2024 champions Sarah Sjöström (SWE), Thomas Ceccon (ITA), Tatjana Schoenmaker-Smith (RSA), and Summer McIntosh (CAN) could defend titles if they qualify for 2028.

SWM07.blurb:
Tuesday heats bring sprints and distance together: the Men's 50m Free, Women's 200m Free and 200m Breast, and the endurance test of the Women's 1500m Free. Cameron McEvoy of Australia won the 50m Free in Paris; Kate Douglass of USA took the 200m Breast.

Mollie O'Callaghan and Ariarne Titmus went 1-2 for Australia in the Women's 200m Free at Paris 2024. If both compete in 2028, they could renew that rivalry.

Morning sessions inside the stadium offer a quieter atmosphere than prime-time finals. The translucent roof provides natural light, and heats let you watch technique and strategy unfold across multiple distances.
SWM07.potentialContendersIntro: Paris 2024 winners Cameron McEvoy (AUS), Mollie O'Callaghan (AUS), and Kate Douglass (USA) are projected contenders if they qualify for their respective events.

SWM08.blurb:
Six gold medals up for grabs in a stacked Tuesday evening: Men's 200m Free, Men's 50m Breast, Men's 1500m Free, Men's 200m Fly, Women's 100m Back, and Women's 200m Free. David Popovici won the 200m Free in Paris; Kaylee McKeown the backstroke; Mollie O'Callaghan the women's 200m.

Léon Marchand took the 200m Fly at Paris 2024 and could defend if he competes. The new Men's 50m Breaststroke crowns its first champion, and the 1500m Free closes with endurance drama.

Evening crowds pack the stadium bowl, and six finals in one session means rapid-fire medal ceremonies. The compact venue amplifies roars as swimmers hit the wall, and sightlines keep you locked on every turn.
SWM08.potentialContendersIntro: Paris 2024 champions David Popovici (ROU), Léon Marchand (FRA), Kaylee McKeown (AUS), and Mollie O'Callaghan (AUS) could headline if they qualify. The Men's 50m Breaststroke is a new event with no prior champion.

SWM09.blurb:
Wednesday morning heats feature the Men's 200m IM, Men's 200m Back, and the Men's 4x200m Free Relay, plus the new Women's 50m Butterfly. Léon Marchand won the 200m IM in Paris; Hubert Kós of Hungary took the backstroke; Great Britain won the relay gold.

The Women's 50m Fly makes its first appearance in the program, adding another sprint event to the mix. These heats set the fields for evening semifinals and finals.

Morning sessions offer a more relaxed entry point to watch world-class swimming. The stadium's translucent roof lets in natural daylight, and the compact bowl keeps every lane in view.
SWM09.potentialContendersIntro: Paris 2024 winners Léon Marchand (FRA) and Hubert Kós (HUN) could compete in individual events. Great Britain's relay quartet won gold in Paris if the team qualifies for 2028.

SWM10.blurb:
Five finals crown champions Wednesday night: Women's 200m Free, Men's 50m Free, Women's 200m Breast, Women's 1500m Free, and the Men's 4x200m Free Relay. Mollie O'Callaghan won the 200m Free in Paris; Cameron McEvoy the 50m; Kate Douglass the 200m Breast.

Katie Ledecky of USA could defend her 1500m Free title if she competes, having won at Paris 2024. Great Britain's relay team won gold in Paris and could return.

Evening sessions bring peak energy to the 38,000-seat bowl. Five medal races and semifinals in two other events pack the schedule, with relay anchors sprinting home under stadium-wide roar.
SWM10.potentialContendersIntro: Paris 2024 champions Mollie O'Callaghan (AUS), Cameron McEvoy (AUS), Kate Douglass (USA), and Katie Ledecky (USA) are projected to compete if they qualify. Great Britain's relay team won gold in Paris.

SWM11.blurb:
Thursday heats bring five events across medley, sprint, distance, and relay. Summer McIntosh won the Women's 200m IM at Paris 2024; Pan Zhanle of China took the Men's 100m Free in a world-record swim; Kaylee McKeown won the Women's 200m Back.

Léon Marchand claimed the Men's 200m Breaststroke in Paris and could appear if he qualifies. Australia won the Women's 4x200m Free Relay at Paris 2024.

Morning prelims at the stadium offer a chance to see future medalists posting qualifying times. The translucent roof provides natural light, and the compact bowl keeps the entire pool in view from every seat.
SWM11.potentialContendersIntro: Paris 2024 champions Summer McIntosh (CAN), Pan Zhanle (CHN), Léon Marchand (FRA), and Kaylee McKeown (AUS) could headline if they compete. Australia's relay team won gold in Paris.

SWM12.blurb:
Six gold medals awarded Thursday night: Men's 200m Back, Men's 200m Breast, Women's 200m IM, Men's 200m IM, Men's 100m Free, and the Women's 4x200m Free Relay. Hubert Kós won the backstroke in Paris; Léon Marchand swept both medleys and the breaststroke; Pan Zhanle claimed the sprint free.

The new Women's 50m Butterfly final also crowns its first champion. Summer McIntosh could defend her 200m IM title if she competes, and Australia's relay team won the 4x200m Free in Paris.

Evening sessions in the stadium bowl draw the loudest crowds. Six finals and semifinals keep the pace relentless, with medal ceremonies unfolding under the translucent roof as relay teams sprint for gold.
SWM12.potentialContendersIntro: Paris 2024 champions Hubert Kós (HUN), Léon Marchand (FRA), Summer McIntosh (CAN), and Pan Zhanle (CHN) are projected to compete if they qualify. Australia's relay team won gold in Paris.

potentialContenders.name: Léon Marchand
potentialContenders.note: Paris 2024 gold, Men's 200m Breaststroke and 200m IM

SWM13.blurb:
Friday morning brings heats in the Men's 100m Fly, Men's 800m Free, and the Mixed 4x100m Medley Relay, plus two new 50m sprint events. Kristóf Milák of Hungary won the butterfly at Paris 2024; Daniel Wiffen of Ireland took the 800m Free in a breakthrough performance.

The Men's 50m Backstroke and Women's 50m Breaststroke make their first appearances, expanding the sprint program. USA won the Mixed Medley Relay in Paris and could field a strong quartet if they qualify.

These heats set semifinal and final lineups for the weekend. The stadium's compact design keeps you close to the action, and natural light filters through the translucent roof during morning sessions.
SWM13.potentialContendersIntro: Paris 2024 winners Kristóf Milák (HUN) and Daniel Wiffen (IRL) could compete in individual events if they qualify. USA's mixed relay team won gold in Paris.

SWM14.blurb:
Five golds awarded Friday evening: Women's 200m Back, Men's 200m Breast, Women's 200m IM, Men's 100m Free, and Men's 100m Fly. Kaylee McKeown swept both backstroke golds in Paris; Léon Marchand the breaststroke and both medleys; Pan Zhanle the sprint free; Kristóf Milák the butterfly.

Semifinals in the new Men's 50m Backstroke and Women's 50m Breaststroke also feature. This session showcases the depth of the week's program, from power sprints to technical medleys.

The stadium bowl seats 38,000 in a compact layout that amplifies crowd energy. Five medal ceremonies and semifinals keep the evening moving, with swimmers racing under the translucent roof as the sun sets over Inglewood.
SWM14.potentialContendersIntro: Paris 2024 champions Kaylee McKeown (AUS), Léon Marchand (FRA), Summer McIntosh (CAN), Pan Zhanle (CHN), and Kristóf Milák (HUN) could defend titles if they qualify for their events.

SWM15.blurb:
The final morning of heats features five events including the Women's 50m Free, Women's 200m Fly, Women's 800m Free, and both medley relay teams. Sarah Sjöström won the 50m Free in Paris at age 30; Summer McIntosh took the 200m Fly; Katie Ledecky the 800m.

China won the Men's 4x100m Medley Relay at Paris 2024; USA won the Women's. These heats determine who advances to Saturday night's closing finals session.

Morning sessions inside the stadium offer a relaxed atmosphere before the weekend crowds arrive. The translucent roof provides natural light, and you can watch future medalists fine-tune their final swims of the week.
SWM15.potentialContendersIntro: Paris 2024 champions Sarah Sjöström (SWE), Summer McIntosh (CAN), and Katie Ledecky (USA) could compete in individual events if they qualify. China's men and USA's women won the medley relays in Paris.

SWM16.blurb:
The penultimate night of swimming packs seven medal events into a two-hour session, including finals in three of the newly added 50m sprint disciplines. Gold medals will be decided in the men's 100m butterfly, men's 50m backstroke, women's 50m breaststroke, and the grueling men's 800m freestyle, while semifinals set the stage for the women's 50m free and 200m butterfly.

The session closes with the mixed 4x100m medley relay final, where four-swimmer teams alternate stroke specialists in a format that debuted at Tokyo 2020. Expect tight finishes in the sprint races, where hundredths of a second separate podium places.

2028 Stadium's compact bowl brings 38,000 spectators close to the action, with the translucent roof bathing the temporary pool in natural light as the California sun sets. The roar echoes differently than traditional aquatics centers, creating an arena atmosphere for the sport's fastest races.
SWM16.potentialContendersIntro: Paris 2024 champions headline the projected field, with Kristóf Milák (HUN) defending his 100m butterfly title, Daniel Wiffen (IRL) likely returning in the 800m free, and Summer McIntosh (CAN) expected to compete in the 200m fly semifinals.

SWM17.blurb:
Swimming's final session closes the nine-day competition with five championship races, including the last individual medal event of the entire 2028 Games. The women's 50m freestyle final—a pure test of explosive speed—crowns its champion less than 24 hours before the Closing Ceremony.

Three relay finals bring national teams into head-to-head battles: men's and women's 4x100m medley relays plus the women's 800m freestyle, where Katie Ledecky of the USA could extend her distance-racing legacy if she competes. The men's and women's medley relays will see powerhouse squads from the USA, Australia, and China projected to contend based on Paris 2024 results.

The afternoon start time fills the stadium with sunlight streaming through the translucent roof. Fans witness the meet's crescendo as relay teams anchor their way to the final medals of the aquatics program, sending swimmers into retirement or toward another four-year cycle.
SWM17.potentialContendersIntro: Paris 2024 medalists Sarah Sjöström (SWE), Katie Ledecky (USA), Ariarne Titmus (AUS), and Summer McIntosh (CAN) could appear in individual finals, while relay powerhouses USA, Australia, and China are projected to battle for team medals.

TEN01.blurb:
The 2028 Games tennis tournament opens with mixed doubles under the California sun. Three first-round matches unfold on Carson's main stadium court, where the hard surface and 360-degree seating offer fans close views of doubles strategy.

The compact format compresses the event into two days, turning opening matches into high-stakes affairs. Teams blend singles stars with doubles specialists, creating unpredictable partnerships.

Carson Center Court sits within Dignity Health Sports Park, a multi-sport complex where tennis courts share the grounds with soccer facilities. The outdoor setting and all-angles sightlines make every service return visible.
TEN01.potentialContendersIntro: Paris 2024 champions Zheng Qinwen and Zhang Zhizhen of China could return to defend their title, while American pairs Jessica Pegula/Rajeev Ram (bronze) and Desiree Krawczyk/Neal Skupski (silver) are likely contenders if they compete.

TEN02.blurb:
Mixed doubles first-round action spreads across Carson's secondary courts, where three matches run simultaneously. Court 1 sits at the heart of the USTA High-Performance Training Center, a facility that's groomed American junior talent since 2003.

Temporary seating brings roughly 2,500 fans right to the baseline, creating an intimate atmosphere for doubles volleys and quick exchanges. The compressed event schedule means early exits end medal hopes immediately.

Outdoor hard courts favor aggressive net play, and the Southern California weather typically delivers clear skies and consistent conditions for afternoon tennis.
TEN02.potentialContendersIntro: The Paris 2024 podium featured China's Zheng/Zhang (gold), American pairs Krawczyk/Skupski (silver) and Pegula/Ram (bronze). Similar tandems could appear if players commit to the 2028 event.

TEN03.blurb:
Two first-round mixed doubles matches launch the tournament on Court 2, an outdoor stadium with panoramic seating. The venue once hosted WTA Premier competition and sits adjacent to the Dignity Health soccer stadium.

With only 16 teams entering the draw, every opening match carries quarterfinal implications. Partnerships often form weeks before the Games, blending chemistry with individual skill.

The 8,000-capacity setup wraps around the hard court, giving spectators unobstructed angles on serves and return positioning. Afternoon sunlight and coastal breezes are part of the Carson tennis experience.
TEN03.potentialContendersIntro: China's Zheng/Zhang claimed Paris 2024 gold, while Americans took both other medals. The 2028 field will likely feature similar pairings of top singles and doubles talent.

TEN04.blurb:
Mixed doubles quarterfinals arrive the same day as first-round play, a scheduling quirk that creates urgency from the tournament's opening serve. Two matches on center court separate eight teams from medal contention.

The evening session under lights shifts the atmosphere—cooler air, tighter ball flight, and crowds settling in for pivotal encounters. One set can swing a quarterfinal; doubles specialists thrive on break-point conversions.

Carson Center Court's all-angles seating ensures every baseline rally and net exchange is visible. The venue's history hosting WTA events brings a proven pedigree to the 2028 hard-court action.
TEN04.potentialContendersIntro: Paris 2024's top finishers—China's Zheng/Zhang (gold) and Americans Krawczyk/Skupski (silver)—are projected contenders if they return. The quarterfinals could feature several Paris medalists.

TEN05.blurb:
Two more mixed doubles quarterfinals unfold under Carson Court 1's evening lights, narrowing the field to four semifinal teams. The USTA training facility turns into a Games venue, blending development infrastructure with temporary championship seating.

Quarterfinal stakes amplify every service game—lose here and medal hopes vanish. Doubles partnerships rely on synchronized net coverage and return aggression to advance.

The 2,500-capacity setup puts fans close to the action, where you can hear racket-string contact and on-court communication. Night sessions offer cooler temperatures and a more relaxed crowd vibe than afternoon heat.
TEN05.potentialContendersIntro: The same Paris 2024 medal-winning pairs—Zheng/Zhang, Krawczyk/Skupski, Pegula/Ram—could appear in these quarterfinals if they qualify and compete in 2028.

TEN06.blurb:
Mixed doubles semifinals determine the two pairs playing for gold and the two battling for bronze. Day two brings medal-match certainty—win and you're guaranteed hardware; lose and it's a consolation bout.

The afternoon session on center court features both semifinals back-to-back, a scheduling gauntlet that tests endurance and nerve. Doubles tactics shift when elimination looms one set away.

Carson's 8,000-seat wraparound bowl creates a cauldron atmosphere for high-leverage points. The outdoor hard courts and California sun complete the setting for what could be career-defining matches.
TEN06.potentialContendersIntro: Paris 2024's top finishers—Zheng/Zhang (gold), Krawczyk/Skupski (silver)—are likely semifinal contenders if they compete, alongside other top-ranked doubles pairings.

TEN07.blurb:
Singles competition begins with first-round matches across men's and women's draws, each featuring 64 players. Court 1 hosts a full day of opening-round action where ranking position meets Games-stage pressure.

The outdoor hard courts at Carson reward power serves and baseline consistency. Round 1 mixes seeds against qualifiers, veterans against rising talent, with upsets always possible in best-of-three format.

The USTA training facility's competitive pedigree shows—sightlines are sharp, surfaces are true, and the 2,500-seat setup keeps fans close enough to track every forehand grip adjustment. Nine hours of tennis delivers relentless variety.
TEN07.potentialContendersIntro: Paris 2024 men's singles saw Novak Djokovic (gold), Carlos Alcaraz (silver), and Lorenzo Musetti (bronze) on the podium. The 2028 men's field could include Alcaraz and other top ATP players, while the women's draw remains open based on June 2028 rankings.

potentialContenders.name: Carlos Alcaraz
potentialContenders.note: Paris 2024 men's singles silver medalist, multi-Slam champion

TEN08.blurb:
First-round singles matches fill Court 2's schedule, offering an accessible entry point to Games tennis. The 64-player draws ensure a mix of marquee names and unknown qualifiers across men's and women's events.

Hard courts and outdoor conditions favor aggressive baseliners, but best-of-three scoring compresses margins—one bad service game can shift a match. Round 1 is where reputations meet reality.

Court 2's 8,000-capacity bowl seats fans around the entire playing surface. The venue's WTA tournament history and adjacent soccer stadium location make it a proven big-event setting within the Dignity Health Sports Park complex.
TEN08.potentialContendersIntro: Novak Djokovic won Paris 2024 men's singles gold at age 37; his 2028 participation is uncertain. Carlos Alcaraz (silver) and other top-ranked players are projected to compete based on June 2028 ATP/WTA rankings.

TEN09.blurb:
Courts 3 through 11 host simultaneous first-round singles matches, creating a tennis sampler platter across nine courts. Budget-priced general admission lets fans roam between courts, catching rising stars and upset bids.

The outdoor hard-court cluster sits within the USTA High-Performance Training Center grounds, where temporary builds expand capacity for Games competition. You'll see more raw matches-in-progress here than polished stadium showdowns.

This is tennis at its most democratic—no assigned court, no curated storyline, just dozens of players fighting through Round 1. Bring sunscreen and a willingness to explore.

TEN10.blurb:
Mixed doubles concludes with bronze and gold medal matches on center court, awarding the event's first medals of the 2028 Games. Two matches separate four teams from the podium.

The evening session delivers high-stakes doubles tennis under lights—quick points, aggressive net play, and partnerships defined by a handful of break chances. The bronze match precedes the gold final, ensuring both carry full medal weight.

Carson Center Court's 360-degree seating wraps 8,000 fans around the action. The venue's history hosting professional tennis returns as it crowns the 2028 mixed doubles champions in prime-time Southern California conditions.
TEN10.potentialContendersIntro: Paris 2024 champions Zheng Qinwen/Zhang Zhizhen (China) and silver medalists Krawczyk/Skupski (USA) are projected finalists if they compete, alongside other top pairings from the semifinals.

potentialContenders.name: Zheng Qinwen / Zhang Zhizhen
potentialContenders.note: Paris 2024 gold medalists, potential title defenders

TEN11.blurb:
Singles Round 1 continues on center court, where afternoon matches showcase both men's and women's draws. With 64 players per gender, opening rounds mix seeds against qualifiers in best-of-three survival tests.

The hard-court surface and outdoor setting favor power baseliners, but upsets define every major tournament's first week. Qualification is based on June 2028 rankings, ensuring the field reflects current form.

Carson Center Court's wraparound seating and 8,000 capacity create a stadium feel for what are technically preliminary matches. The venue's professional pedigree—it hosted WTA events for six years—shows in sightlines and court quality.
TEN11.potentialContendersIntro: Singles qualification uses ATP/WTA rankings from June 12, 2028. Top-ranked players earn direct acceptance into the 64-player draws, with potential contenders determined weeks before the Games.

potentialContenders.name: Carlos Alcaraz
potentialContenders.note: Paris 2024 men's singles silver medalist, likely top seed if he competes

TEN12.blurb:
Singles and doubles first rounds overlap on Court 1, delivering nine hours of continuous tennis across four events. Men's and women's singles feature 64-player draws; doubles fields 32 teams per gender.

The format creates scheduling density—a seed can play singles in the afternoon, doubles in the evening. Qualification uses separate rankings for each discipline, published June 12, 2028, with NOCs limited to two teams per doubles event.

Italy won women's doubles gold in Paris 2024, while Individual Neutral Athletes took silver and Spain earned bronze. Court 1's training-facility roots and temporary seating keep fans close to baseline exchanges and net volleys.
TEN12.potentialContendersIntro: Women's doubles qualification is based on WTA doubles rankings, not singles standings. Paris 2024 champions Italy and medalists Spain could return if rankings hold.

TEN13.blurb:
Court 2 hosts overlapping singles and doubles opening rounds, with men's and women's events sharing the schedule. The 32-team doubles draws begin alongside the 64-player singles battles.

Doubles qualification uses dedicated doubles rankings, meaning pure specialists can earn spots over higher-profile singles stars. Italy claimed Paris 2024 women's doubles gold; whether those champions return depends on 2028 form.

The outdoor hard courts and 8,000-seat stadium bowl create a pro-tournament atmosphere within the Dignity Health Sports Park complex. Expect nine hours of first-round action across multiple disciplines.
TEN13.potentialContendersIntro: Italy won Paris 2024 women's doubles gold, while Individual Neutral Athletes earned silver. The 2028 doubles field is determined by June 2028 doubles rankings, with a maximum two teams per NOC.

TEN14.blurb:
The outer courts host a full slate of singles and doubles Round 1 matches, with general admission granting access to all nine courts. It's tennis in its rawest form—players fighting for survival, fans roaming between matches.

With 172 total athletes competing across five events, and NOCs capped at six per gender, the field blends superstars with lesser-known qualifiers. Spain took women's doubles bronze in Paris; watch for returning medalists.

The USTA training facility's temporary builds expand capacity for preliminary rounds. Budget pricing and open seating make this the most accessible ticket in the tennis draw—bring stamina and sunscreen.

TEN15.blurb:
Center court's evening session delivers prime-time singles Round 1 matches under lights. The outdoor hard courts and cooler night air shift playing conditions from afternoon heat.

Singles draws feature 64 players per gender, with top players seeded based on June 2028 rankings. Age minimums require men to be 14 and women 15 by the event's start, ensuring a mix of veterans and emerging talent.

Carson Center Court's all-angles seating and professional pedigree—it hosted WTA events from 2003 to 2009—create a big-match feel even for opening rounds. Spain's Paris 2024 women's doubles bronze medalists could appear in doubles action during the session.
TEN15.potentialContendersIntro: Singles contenders are determined by ATP/WTA rankings published June 12, 2028. Top-56 ranked eligible players earn direct acceptance into the draws.

potentialContenders.name: Carlos Alcaraz
potentialContenders.note: Paris 2024 men's singles silver medalist, projected top contender

TEN16.blurb:
Round 2 of men's and women's singles on Carson's main stadium kicks off a pivotal day of elimination tennis. The 64-player draw narrows as the top performers from opening matches battle for quarterfinal berths.

Rankings through early June 2028 determine the field, with a maximum of four players per country in singles competition. Expect a mix of seeded stars and dangerous qualifiers testing each other in best-of-three set duels.

Carson Center Court wraps 8,000 seats in a 360-degree bowl around the hard court, putting fans close to every baseline rally and net exchange under the Southern California sun.
TEN16.potentialContendersIntro: The singles draws will likely feature the world's top-ranked players as of June 2028, with reigning major champions and recent Paris 2024 medalists expected to headline the field.

potentialContenders.name: Iga Świątek
potentialContenders.note: Paris 2024 bronze medalist, multiple Grand Slam champion

potentialContenders.name: Novak Djokovic
potentialContenders.note: Paris 2024 gold medalist, all-time Grand Slam leader

TEN17.blurb:
A marathon day-to-night session blending singles knockout rounds with the opening of doubles competition. Court 1 becomes a stage for both individual brilliance and team chemistry as partnerships debut.

Women's doubles brings back Italy's Paris 2024 champions, who claimed gold in a memorable final. The 32-team draw per gender features nations limited to two pairs each, sharpening the competitive edge.

This USTA training facility turned Games venue seats 2,500 in temporary stands, offering intimate sightlines for an all-day tennis immersion.
TEN17.potentialContendersIntro: Italy's Sara Errani and Jasmine Paolini return as Paris 2024 gold medalists and will be favorites if they compete together again in women's doubles.

TEN18.blurb:
Singles survivors advance while doubles teams make their first appearance across Court 2's dual-action schedule. The 32-pair brackets in men's and women's doubles launch with first-round matches scattered throughout the day.

Paris 2024 saw Italy's Errani and Paolini take gold, with the silver going to Individual Neutral Athletes in a tightly contested final. Spain's bronze medalists could return seeking an upgrade.

Court 2's outdoor bowl sits beside the Dignity Health Sports Park soccer stadium, a reminder that this Carson complex pulses with multi-sport energy even on tennis-only days.
TEN18.potentialContendersIntro: Watch for the Paris 2024 women's doubles medalists if they reunite, alongside top-ranked men's pairs chasing their first Games gold.

TEN19.blurb:
The outer courts come alive with wall-to-wall tennis as singles Round 2 and doubles Round 1 unfold simultaneously across nine playing surfaces. This is ground zero for the grind—qualifiers hunting upsets, partnerships finding rhythm, and the tournament's breadth on full display.

TKW01.blurb:
The 2028 Games taekwondo competition opens with two flyweight divisions battling through 48 early-round bouts across four hours. Athletes in the women's -49kg and men's -58kg classes face single-elimination pressure from the first kick, with survivors advancing to afternoon quarterfinals.

South Korea's Jang Jun claimed Paris 2024 gold in the women's division, while Iran's Mohammadrashad Khodavandi took the men's title. Qualification runs through mid-2026 rankings, so the field mixes proven medalists with rising contenders.

LA Convention Center Hall 1 converts its exhibit floors into a tight arena with 36-foot ceilings overhead. Early sessions offer accessible entry prices and close views of spinning hook kicks echoing off the walls.
TKW01.potentialContendersIntro: Paris 2024 medalists anchored their divisions, but the 2028 field depends on two-year ranking cycles concluding in 2026.

Afternoon light filters through Hall 1's high windows as crowds build toward evening finals. Best-of-three-round scoring keeps every exchange urgent.

Prime-time energy fills Hall 1 as medalists bow on the mat and flags rise overhead. Downtown LA's convention hall becomes a cauldron for the first taekwondo golds of the Games.

TRI01.blurb:
The women's triathlon final opens the medal chase with a 1.5 km ocean swim off Venice Beach, a 40 km bike through neighborhood streets, and a 10 km beach run to the finish. Athletes dive into the Pacific at dawn, emerge for a high-speed transition on the sand, then push inland before returning to the shore for the run.

Paris 2024 medalists Cassandre Beaugrand, Julie Derron, and Beth Potter could return, alongside world championship contenders still fighting for qualification slots. The race unfolds over two and a half hours of constant lead changes and tactical positioning.

Spectators spread along the beach and Venice Boulevard catch multiple views as athletes loop past. The boardwalk's street culture and sunrise light over the ocean create an energetic, open-air theater for endurance sport.
TRI01.potentialContendersIntro: Paris 2024 medalists lead the projected field, though rosters remain unconfirmed until qualification concludes.

TRI02.blurb:
The men's triathlon final follows the same punishing course: Pacific swim, 40 km bike, and a sandy 10 km run along Venice Beach. Early morning conditions test endurance, bike-handling on city streets, and the ability to run on sand after two hours of racing.

Alex Yee and Hayden Wilde, the Paris 2024 gold and silver medalists, are projected contenders if they qualify, alongside bronze medalist Léo Bergère. The race typically sees breakaways form on the bike and dissolve on the run, with medals decided in the final kilometers.

Crowds line the boardwalk and boulevard to watch transitions and the beach sprint to the finish. Venice's casual vibe and ocean backdrop frame one of the sport's most grueling single-day tests.
TRI02.potentialContendersIntro: Paris 2024's podium finishers are likely to return, pending qualification and continued form through 2028.

TRI03.blurb:
The mixed relay triathlon closes the program with four-athlete teams racing in rapid succession—two women, two men, each completing a super-sprint distance before tagging a teammate. Germany won Paris 2024, edging the United States and Great Britain in a format built for drama and tight finishes.

Each leg is shorter than the individual race but faster and more tactical. Teams rotate through swim, bike, and run in under two hours, with transitions happening in full view of the beach crowd. National depth matters as much as individual brilliance.

Venice Beach hosts its final triathlon session with the boardwalk packed and the ocean churning. The relay format makes every exchange visible, turning the sand transition zone into the event's emotional center.
TRI03.potentialContendersIntro: Paris 2024 medalist nations Germany, United States, and Great Britain could field strong squads again, though rosters will shift by 2028.

TTE01.blurb:
Table tennis opens its 2028 campaign with morning action inside LA Convention Center Hall 3, a downtown exhibit space transformed into a tight, column-free arena. The session mixes wide-open singles play—64 men and 64 women battling through opening rounds—with the round of 16 in mixed doubles, where pairs begin their sprint toward medals.

Singles matches at this stage feature a global cast, from ranked stars to qualifiers hunting upsets in best-of-seven games. Mixed doubles narrows to 16 teams, with China—gold medalists in Paris 2024—likely among the favorites if they field a pair.

Theater-style seating wraps the tables, putting you close enough to hear paddle strikes and see spin in real time. It's an accessible entry point, priced for curious fans and devotees alike.
TTE01.potentialContendersIntro: China swept Paris 2024 mixed doubles gold and could enter a strong pair here, while DPR Korea (silver) and Korea (bronze) may return competitive duos.

TTE02.blurb:
Afternoon doubles action brings 16 men's pairs and 16 women's pairs to the convention hall, each chasing quarterfinal berths. Expect rapid-fire rallies and tactical positioning as teams lean on chemistry built over years of international competition.

China historically dominates doubles events, while Korea and DPR Korea have proven they can challenge at the highest level. Early rounds can spring surprises—underdogs with nothing to lose facing seasoned pairs under pressure.

The polished concrete floor and pre-function lobby give the venue a utilitarian feel, but the intimacy inside makes every point feel consequential.
TTE02.potentialContendersIntro: Paris 2024 saw strong Asian performances across doubles; expect China, Korea, and Japan to feature prominently if they qualify pairs.

TTE03.blurb:
Prime-time singles bring the day's third round-of-64 session, with men and women grinding through opening battles under Hall 3's bright lights. Matches at this stage separate contenders from pretenders, though upsets are always lurking in a sport measured in milliseconds.

The format stays unchanged from Paris 2024, where Fan Zhendong (China) claimed men's gold and Chen Meng (China) took women's honors. Whether those champions return or new faces emerge, expect precision and power from the world's best.

Evening crowds tend toward die-hards who appreciate the sport's tactical chess and lightning reflexes. Downtown LA's dining and nightlife are steps away for post-match exploration.
TTE03.potentialContendersIntro: Fan Zhendong and Chen Meng won Paris 2024 singles gold for China; if they compete in 2028, they'll be among the favorites.

TTE04.blurb:
Mixed doubles hits the quarterfinal stage, where eight pairs compete for semifinal spots and medal hopes crystallize. China's Paris 2024 gold medalists could be heavy favorites if they return, but DPR Korea and Korea—silver and bronze in Paris—have proven they can disrupt.

Singles prelims continue in parallel, offering a buffet of styles and storylines across multiple tables. The session blends high-stakes doubles drama with the wide-open feel of early-round singles.

Sunday morning at the convention center means manageable crowds and a chance to see future champions before they're household names.
TTE04.potentialContendersIntro: Paris 2024's mixed doubles podium—China (gold), DPR Korea (silver), Korea (bronze)—may field similar pairs if top players return.

TTE05.blurb:
Another round-of-64 singles session fills the afternoon, whittling the men's and women's fields as players chase round-of-32 spots. At this stage, ranked athletes begin asserting themselves, though the knockout format keeps every game urgent.

Fans see a cross-section of global talent—Europe's technical masters, Asia's power-spin hybrids, emerging stars from unexpected federations. It's table tennis at its most democratic, before the field tightens to household names.

Afternoon light filters through the convention center's industrial bones, and ticket prices remain accessible for families or curious newcomers testing the sport's appeal.
TTE05.potentialContendersIntro: This prelim session could feature Paris 2024 medalists like Fan Zhendong or Sun Yingsha if they compete, alongside rising challengers.

TTE06.blurb:
Mixed doubles semifinals cap a long Sunday night, with four pairs fighting for Monday's medal matches. China's Paris 2024 champions—if they advance—would be expected here, but DPR Korea's silver medalists and Korea's bronze team proved in Paris they can challenge.

Singles prelims run simultaneously, offering late-night variety across the hall's multiple tables. The session stretches past 11 PM, rewarding night owls with a chance to witness history in the making.

Downtown LA hums outside, but inside Hall 3 the focus narrows to paddle, ball, and the relentless geometry of world-class doubles.
TTE06.potentialContendersIntro: Paris 2024's top mixed doubles pairs—China (gold), DPR Korea (silver), Korea (bronze)—are projected semifinalists if they qualify.

TTE07.blurb:
Doubles quarterfinals arrive for both men and women, with eight pairs in each event scrapping for semifinal slots. The morning session blends medal-track intensity with ongoing singles prelims, creating a multi-table spectacle of spins, slams, and strategy.

China swept mixed doubles gold in Paris 2024 and fields deep rosters across all doubles events. Korea and Japan could challenge, while European and other Asian teams hunt breakthrough performances.

LA Convention Center's central location makes this an easy weekday stop—grab breakfast in Little Tokyo or Grand Central Market, then settle in for three hours of high-speed competition.
TTE07.potentialContendersIntro: China's dominance in Paris 2024 mixed doubles suggests strong showings here, with Korea and Japan likely in the quarterfinal mix.

TTE08.blurb:
Singles prelims continue Monday afternoon, narrowing the round of 64 as the field tilts toward ranked stars. Expect tighter matches and fewer easy wins as the draw sorts itself into contenders and cinderellas.

Paris 2024 saw China claim both singles golds—Fan Zhendong in men's, Chen Meng in women's—while Sweden's Truls Möregårdh and France's Félix Lebrun broke through for medals. Whether those names reappear or new challengers emerge, this session offers a snapshot of 2028's singles landscape.

Afternoon sessions are priced for accessibility, with the convention center's pre-function areas offering space to stretch between matches.
TTE08.potentialContendersIntro: Paris 2024 medalists like Fan Zhendong, Chen Meng, and Truls Möregårdh could appear if they qualify, along with top-ranked challengers.

TTE09.blurb:
Mixed doubles crowns its champions in a prime-time double-header—bronze match followed by gold. China won Paris 2024 and could be heavy favorites if they field a top pair, while DPR Korea (silver) and Korea (bronze) have proven they can reach the podium.

The format is unforgiving: best-of-seven games, every point magnified under medal pressure. Expect tactical brilliance, defensive scrambles, and the occasional audacious winner that shifts momentum in an instant.

Monday night at Hall 3 delivers the Games' first table tennis medals. The atmosphere crackles with tension, and tickets at this price point guarantee a front-row seat to history.
TTE09.potentialContendersIntro: China's Paris 2024 gold medalists are projected favorites, with DPR Korea and Korea likely competing for medals if they advance.

TTE10.blurb:
Men's doubles reaches the semifinal stage, with four pairs battling for Tuesday night's medal matches. China's depth could put them in both semifinals, but Korea, Japan, and European challengers have upset potential.

Singles round-of-32 action runs in parallel, featuring top seeds making their first appearances and lower-ranked players defending survival. The session offers a dual narrative—doubles pairs one win from medals, singles athletes one loss from elimination.

Tuesday morning at the convention center means lighter crowds and easier access, ideal for fans who want to watch world-class table tennis without fighting for sightlines.
TTE10.potentialContendersIntro: China's Paris 2024 mixed doubles dominance suggests strong men's doubles pairs, with Korea and Japan projected as semifinal contenders.

TTE11.blurb:
Singles round of 32 brings the sport's elite into sharper focus, with 32 men and 32 women fighting for round-of-16 spots. Fan Zhendong and Chen Meng won Paris 2024 gold for China; if they compete here, expect clinical performances against overmatched opponents.

Sweden's Truls Möregårdh (Paris silver) and France's Félix Lebrun (Paris bronze) proved non-Chinese players can medal. Japan's Hina Hayata took women's bronze in Paris, signaling depth beyond China's traditional dominance.

Afternoon sessions offer value pricing and a chance to see multiple matches unfold simultaneously across Hall 3's tables. The venue's central location makes it an easy mid-trip addition.
TTE11.potentialContendersIntro: Paris 2024 singles medalists—Fan Zhendong, Chen Meng, Truls Möregårdh, and others—could all feature if they qualify for 2028.

TTE12.blurb:
Men's doubles decides its medals in a Tuesday night showcase—bronze match at 9 PM, gold to follow. China's track record suggests they could dominate both matches, but Korea and Japan have proven capable of disrupting expectations.

The stakes are pure: win bronze or go home empty-handed, then watch the gold-medal pair claim the podium's top step. Best-of-seven games leave no margin for error, rewarding the pair that holds nerve under pressure.

Prime-time medals justify the premium ticket price. Hall 3's compact layout puts every seat close to the action, and the downtown setting offers post-match celebration options across DTLA's restaurant scene.
TTE12.potentialContendersIntro: China's Paris 2024 mixed doubles gold suggests strong men's pairs; Korea and Japan are projected medal contenders if they advance.

TTE13.blurb:
Women's doubles semifinals narrow four pairs to the two that will contest Wednesday night's gold medal. China's depth in women's events—Chen Meng won Paris singles gold, Sun Yingsha took silver—could translate to dominant doubles pairs if they field them.

Singles round-of-32 matches continue across the hall, offering a multi-event morning for fans who want variety. Korea, Japan, and Chinese Taipei have all fielded competitive women's doubles teams in recent cycles.

Wednesday morning provides a midweek table tennis fix before the competition shifts to singles focus later in the schedule. Downtown LA's walkability makes Hall 3 an easy destination.
TTE13.potentialContendersIntro: China's Paris 2024 singles dominance suggests strong women's doubles pairs; expect Korea and Japan as projected semifinalists.

TTE14.blurb:
Singles round of 32 continues Wednesday afternoon, tightening the field as the draw marches toward quarterfinals. This session could feature Paris 2024 champions Fan Zhendong and Chen Meng if they've advanced, along with challengers hunting statement wins.

Möregårdh's Paris silver showed Europe can challenge China's dominance, while Lebrun's bronze energized French fans. Japan's Hayata proved Asian depth extends beyond China. Whether those names repeat or new stars emerge, the round of 32 separates serious medal contenders from early-round survivors.

Afternoon pricing remains accessible, and the convention center's multiple tables let you sample different playing styles and national strategies across simultaneous matches.
TTE14.potentialContendersIntro: Paris 2024 medalists like Fan Zhendong, Chen Meng, Sun Yingsha, and Truls Möregårdh are projected round-of-32 competitors if they qualify.

TTE15.blurb:
Women's doubles delivers the final pair of table tennis medals in this early stretch—bronze at 9 PM, gold to follow. China's historical dominance and Paris 2024 success across events make them projected favorites, but Korea and Japan have fielded medal-worthy doubles teams in recent years.

The format is unforgiving: best-of-seven games, every rally carrying podium implications. Pairs lean on years of chemistry and split-second communication, reading each other's movements as instinctively as their opponents' spins.

Wednesday night caps the doubles program before the Games shift focus to singles and team events. Premium tickets guarantee premium seats for LA's first women's doubles champions.
TTE15.potentialContendersIntro: China's Paris 2024 mixed doubles gold and singles dominance suggest strong women's pairs; Korea and Japan are projected medal contenders.

TTE16.blurb:
The Round of 16 marks the first serious elimination stage in singles play, where the field narrows from 64 to 16 survivors. Both men's and women's matches run simultaneously across multiple tables in this morning session.

Participants qualified through preliminary rounds will face off in best-of-seven-game matches, with each game played to 11 points. The pace is relentless—serves crack like gunfire, rallies blur into milliseconds of reaction time.

LA Convention Center Hall 3's theater-style seating brings spectators close to the action, where you can hear the ball's distinctive ping and see the spin that makes elite table tennis almost impossible to follow with the naked eye.
TTE16.potentialContendersIntro: Paris 2024 medalists Fan Zhendong and Chen Meng could appear if they qualify for the 2028 Games, along with other top-ranked players from the preliminary rounds.

TTE17.blurb:
Afternoon elimination continues as the Round of 16 unfolds across both singles draws. This session features the remaining matches that will set the quarterfinal brackets.

Expect intense defensive scrambles and sudden offensive explosions—at this level, players can shift from absorbing power to attacking in a single stroke. The winner-takes-all format means every point carries knockout weight.

The downtown venue's compact configuration puts every seat within range of the action, where the squeak of shoes and players' grunts punctuate each exchange.
TTE17.potentialContendersIntro: Paris 2024 bronze medalists Félix Lebrun and Hina Hayata could feature if they advance, alongside other qualifiers from earlier rounds.

TTE18.blurb:
Prime time brings the final Round of 16 matches under the lights. Eight survivors in each draw will emerge tonight, their quarterfinal berths secured.

The evening session typically features some of the day's most anticipated matchups, as seeding brings ranked players into direct collision. Watch for the signature serves—some players toss the ball six feet high, generating spin that defies physics.

Hall 3's evening atmosphere intensifies as each match concludes, the elimination stakes sharpening with every game won or lost.
TTE18.potentialContendersIntro: Top seeds from Paris 2024, including Truls Möregårdh and Sun Yingsha, are projected to compete in this session if they reach this round.

TTE19.blurb:
The quarterfinals deliver the Games' first medal-round stakes—four matches in each draw, eight total medal spots on the line. Win and you're guaranteed at least bronze; lose and you're out.

Expect tactical chess matches interrupted by explosive rallies, as players probe for weaknesses while protecting their own vulnerabilities. The best-of-seven format allows no margin for slow starts.

Morning sessions at the Convention Center offer a different energy than evening finals—more intimate, with serious fans who know the game's subtleties filling the seats.
TTE19.potentialContendersIntro: Paris 2024 medalists Fan Zhendong, Truls Möregårdh, Félix Lebrun, Chen Meng, Sun Yingsha, and Hina Hayata could all appear if they reach the quarterfinals.

TTE20.blurb:
Quarterfinal play continues through the afternoon, with the remaining medal-deciding matches across both singles draws. Each winner books a semifinal spot; each loser heads home.

The middle sessions often produce the day's most dramatic moments—underdogs with nothing to lose attacking favorites, momentum shifts that turn matches inside out. At this level, mental strength matters as much as forehand loops.

Downtown LA's summer heat stays outside, but the pressure inside Hall 3 rises with every deuce game and edge ball that clips the table.
TTE20.potentialContendersIntro: All six Paris 2024 medalists could appear in this session if they advance, making it potentially the most star-studded quarterfinal bracket.

potentialContenders.name: Fan Zhendong

TTE21.blurb:
Friday night brings the final quarterfinal matches, completing both semifinal lineups. The last four players standing in each draw will battle for gold and silver two days later.

Late sessions capture table tennis at its most theatrical—dramatic lighting, focused crowds, the stakes clear to everyone in the hall. Players who've survived five days of competition now need two more wins for gold.

The Convention Center's adaptable space transforms into a surprisingly intimate arena for night sessions, where the smallest gestures—a fist pump, a ball slammed in frustration—register across the venue.
TTE21.potentialContendersIntro: Based on Paris 2024 results and current world rankings, Fan Zhendong and Chen Meng remain top contenders if they qualify and reach this stage.

TTE22.blurb:
Two women's singles semifinals determine who plays for gold and who settles the bronze. The winners advance to Saturday night's final; the losers face each other in the bronze medal match.

Semifinals often produce the Games' highest quality play—top-four finishers with nothing held back, attacking freely now that medals are secured. The tactical adjustments between games can swing entire matches.

Afternoon light filters through Hall 3 as the women's draw reaches its climax, with partisan crowds supporting their nations' last remaining hopes.
TTE22.potentialContendersIntro: Paris 2024 medalists Chen Meng, Sun Yingsha, and Hina Hayata are projected to contend if they qualify and advance to this stage.

potentialContenders.name: Sun Yingsha

TTE23.blurb:
Saturday night crowns the women's singles champion across two medal matches. The bronze final opens, followed by the gold medal showdown that determines the greatest women's player of the 2028 Games.

Gold finals deliver pressure like nothing else in table tennis—every serve scrutinized, every edge ball debated, the weight of national expectations visible in players' faces. The best-of-seven format means leads can evaporate and underdogs can shock.

Prime time at the Convention Center brings maximum crowd energy, with the venue's theater seating creating a wall of sound that crashes over each crucial point.
TTE23.potentialContendersIntro: Based on Paris 2024 results and 2024-2026 rankings, Chen Meng and Sun Yingsha are expected to contend if they qualify for the Games.

TTE24.blurb:
The men's semifinals match the women's format—two battles for final spots, with winners advancing to Sunday night's gold medal match. Bronze is guaranteed, but gold remains two wins away.

Men's singles at the highest level showcases raw power combined with impossible precision—forehands that sound like gunshots, backhands that curve midair. The athletes who reach this stage have mastered both physical and psychological warfare.

Sunday afternoon at Hall 3 builds toward evening's finale, the semifinals serving as the final filter before the ultimate showdown.
TTE24.potentialContendersIntro: Paris 2024 medalists Fan Zhendong, Truls Möregårdh, and Félix Lebrun could all appear if they qualify and reach the semifinals, with Lebrun rising in rankings since his bronze.

TTE25.blurb:
The men's singles competition concludes with bronze and gold medal matches that will define careers. Two matches, three medals, one champion.

Gold finals often become instant classics—the tension of years of preparation compressed into best-of-seven games, with each player hunting for the tactical edge that breaks their opponent. Expect long rallies interrupted by sudden kills, defensive scrambles that end in impossible winners.

Sunday night in downtown LA delivers the Games' table tennis climax, with Hall 3 packed and the sport's global audience watching two players battle for the right to stand atop the podium.
TTE25.potentialContendersIntro: Fan Zhendong holds the top world ranking as of 2026 and is expected to contend if he qualifies, along with Paris 2024 medalists Möregårdh and Lebrun.

TTE26.blurb:
Mixed team competition begins with groups 1 and 2 playing their opening round matches. Teams feature men and women competing in singles and doubles formats, with nation versus nation across multiple disciplines.

China won mixed team gold at Paris 2024, while Japan took silver—both nations are likely to field strong squads if they qualify. The group stage determines knockout bracket seeding, making early wins valuable.

The Convention Center's morning sessions offer an accessible entry point to table tennis, with multiple matches running simultaneously and the format's variety keeping the action fresh.
TTE26.potentialContendersIntro: Based on Paris 2024 results, teams from China and Japan are projected to contend in the mixed team event.

TTE27.blurb:
Groups 3 and 4 launch their mixed team campaigns in this evening session. The format combines individual brilliance with team tactics, as nations build rosters from their best singles and doubles players.

Asian powerhouses have dominated recent mixed team events, though European and other nations continue developing competitive programs. Each team match consists of multiple individual contests, with the aggregate score determining winners.

Evening sessions at Hall 3 draw larger crowds as downtown workers and local fans fill seats, creating a more festive atmosphere than morning preliminaries.

TTE28.blurb:
Round 2 brings groups 1 and 2 back for their second matches, with early group standings beginning to take shape. Teams that won their openers can nearly secure knockout berths; those that lost face must-win pressure.

The mixed team format rewards depth—nations need multiple world-class players across genders and playing styles. Watch how coaches deploy their rosters tactically, saving top players for crucial matchups or throwing them in early to build momentum.

Midweek morning sessions capture the tournament's working rhythm, with teams grinding through group play while managing player fatigue across a long competition schedule.
TTE28.potentialContendersIntro: Paris 2024 gold medalists from China are likely to participate if they qualify, maintaining their mixed team dominance.

Groups 3 and 4 continue with round 2 matches, building toward Wednesday's group-deciding finales. Playoff positioning and potential knockout matchups come into focus.

Strong Asian teams typically dominate group stages based on recent international results, though upsets remain possible when underdogs catch favorites in tactical traps. The multi-match format within each team encounter allows for dramatic comebacks.

Two more group-stage rounds in the mixed team event, where nations field their best pairs in head-to-head battles for quarterfinal spots. Each match is a best-of-five format combining singles and doubles rubbers, making every point a potential tipping point.

China dominated this event in Paris with Sun Yingsha and Wang Chuqin claiming gold, while Japan's Hayata-Harimoto pair took silver. France's Lebrun brothers earned bronze on home soil. Groups 3 and 4 could see any of these contenders, plus Sweden and host-nation USA teams fighting for advancement.

TTE37.blurb:
The gold medal match closes out the table tennis program on championship Saturday night, crowning the world's best mixed team in a winner-takes-all final. Two nations face off in best-of-five rubbers where every point carries medal weight and national glory.

China crushed Japan 4-1 in the Paris 2024 final behind Sun Yingsha and Wang Chuqin's brilliance and enters as the favorite if they advance. Japan's Harimoto and Hayata are top-ranked challengers expected to push for revenge. The format blends singles and doubles, demanding both individual skill and tactical pairing.

Prime time in the Convention Center, the polished floor gleaming under lights as the crowd packs in close enough to feel the spin and speed, witnessing the sport's fastest reflexes and finest margins decide gold.
TTE37.potentialContendersIntro: China is the projected gold medal favorite based on their Paris 2024 victory and current world rankings, with Japan the likely challenger if both reach the final.

VBV01.blurb:
Beach volleyball opens at Alamitos Beach Stadium on a sunny Saturday morning with three preliminary matches across men's and women's pools. The session offers an accessible entry point to watch 2028 contenders begin their campaigns, with teams battling in best-of-three sets on the sand.

Preliminary pools determine which squads advance to the knockout rounds—every match counts. Sweden's Paris 2024 men's gold medalists could appear alongside Brazil's reigning women's champions if they qualify.

The beachfront venue sits steps from Long Beach's waterfront, with ocean breezes and open sightlines creating a relaxed coastal atmosphere. Arrive early for warmups and settle into the rhythm of serves, spikes, and sand dives.
VBV01.potentialContendersIntro: Sweden's Paris 2024 men's gold medalists and Brazil's women's champions are projected contenders if they qualify for the 2028 Games.

VBV02.blurb:
Four preliminary matches fill the afternoon session as pool play intensifies on opening day. Men's and women's teams rotate through the sand court, with each set win shaping bracket positioning for the knockout rounds.

Nations can enter up to two teams per gender—watch for powerhouse countries fielding multiple medal threats. The format mirrors Paris 2024, where Norway took men's bronze and Switzerland claimed women's bronze.

The temporary stadium seats 12,000 along Long Beach's coastline, offering unobstructed views of volleys and blocks. The afternoon sun casts dramatic shadows across the court as the ocean stretches beyond the venue's western edge.
VBV02.potentialContendersIntro: Norway's Paris 2024 bronze team and Switzerland's women's medalists could return if they secure qualification through continental championships or world rankings.

VBV03.blurb:
The opening day closes with three evening matches under lights at Alamitos Beach. Pool standings begin to take shape as teams hunt wins before the field narrows to 16 per gender.

Host USA holds one guaranteed quota place per gender, with potential for a second team through standard qualification pathways. Evening sessions bring cooler ocean air and a more intimate crowd vibe as the sun sets over the Pacific.

Floodlights illuminate the sand court against the darkening sky. Long Beach's downtown skyline glows in the near distance while waves crash beyond the venue's temporary bleachers.
VBV03.potentialContendersIntro: Teams from the 2026 Continental Championships—awarding five quota places per gender—could feature in this session alongside Paris 2024 medalists.

Qualification through the 2027 World Championships awards one spot per gender—watch for those winners making early statements. The best-of-three format favors momentum, with sets played to 21 points and a two-point margin required.

Morning sessions offer the coolest temperatures before the Southern California sun climbs high. Soft sand underfoot and open-air seating create a casual beach atmosphere for watching world-class competition.
VBV04.potentialContendersIntro: Paris 2024 medalists likely to appear include Sweden's men's champions and Canada's women's silver team, if they navigate 2026-2028 qualification.

Alamitos Beach Stadium stretches along Long Beach's western shoreline with wide-open sightlines. The temporary venue was purpose-built for the 2028 Games, offering modern amenities against a classic California beach backdrop.

WLF01.blurb:
The 2028 Games weightlifting competition opens with the Men's 65kg final, where lifters will attack the snatch and clean & jerk in pursuit of the first medals of the program. This lightest men's category showcases technical precision and explosive power in equal measure.

Indonesia's Rizki Juniansyah won gold at Paris 2024 with a 309kg total, establishing himself as the division's rising force. The session unfolds as a group final where all qualified lifters compete together, each taking three attempts per lift with rising weights.

Peacock Theater's intimate 7,100-seat setup puts you close to the platform, where chalk clouds hang in the spotlight and each successful lift draws roars that echo off the theater walls.
WLF01.potentialContendersIntro: Rizki Juniansyah claimed gold at Paris 2024 with a dominant 309kg total. If he competes, expect other strong lifters from Asia and South America to challenge.

WLF02.blurb:
The Women's 53kg final brings Day 1 to a primetime close as the lightest women on the platform chase gold. This category demands flawless technique, with fractions of a kilogram separating medals when totals are tallied.

China's Hou Zhihui dominated Paris 2024 with a 206kg total to defend her Tokyo gold, though her 2028 participation remains uncertain. Thailand and Indonesia have produced recent medal contenders in this division, setting up potential regional rivalries.

The evening session creates an electric atmosphere in downtown LA's theater district, with every successful clean & jerk met by thunderous applause in the mid-sized venue where no seat sits more than 220 feet from the action.
WLF02.potentialContendersIntro: Hou Zhihui won Paris 2024 gold with 206kg. If she doesn't compete, Thailand's Surodchana Khambao (Paris bronze) could be among the favorites.

WLF03.blurb:
Day 2 delivers a marathon afternoon featuring back-to-back women's finals at 61kg and 69kg, nearly five hours of elite lifting across two weight classes. Both divisions showcase different styles—61kg often favors speed and precision, while 69kg brings raw power into sharper focus.

Canada's Maude Charron won Paris 2024 gold at 64kg under the previous class structure, though the current 61kg and 69kg categories may see her and other former 64kg lifters redistributed. China historically fields strong contenders across multiple women's categories, making them projected medal threats.

The double-final format means sustained energy in Peacock Theater's four-level lobby areas between competitions, with fans moving between concessions and their seats as one medal ceremony flows into the next warmup session.
WLF03.potentialContendersIntro: The 61kg and 69kg classes feature lifters who competed under different weight structures at Paris 2024. Maude Charron (Canada) won the former 64kg category; she could compete in either division here.

WLF04.blurb:
Men's 75kg closes Wednesday night with one of weightlifting's most competitive divisions, where multiple nations routinely produce world-class lifters. The category sits in the sweet spot between technical finesse and absolute strength.

Bulgaria's Karlos Nasar won Paris 2024 gold with a stunning 404kg total, setting a new standard for the weight class at just 20 years old. If Nasar competes in 2028, he'll be a formidable favorite entering his athletic prime.

Primetime under Peacock Theater's stage lights amplifies the drama as lifters chalk up for final clean & jerk attempts, the crowd hushed during the setup before erupting when barbells lock out overhead.
WLF04.potentialContendersIntro: Karlos Nasar demolished the field at Paris 2024 with a 404kg total. The young Bulgarian could dominate if he returns, though challenges may come from Asia and the Middle East.

WLF05.blurb:
Thursday afternoon packs two men's finals into one ticket—85kg and 95kg—showcasing the middleweight divisions where power and speed converge. These classes often produce dramatic competitions, with lifters pushing career bests when medals hang in the balance.

Albania's Antonino Pizzolato won Paris 2024 gold at 89kg, while Armenia's Varazdat Lalayan took gold at 96kg under the previous structure. Both could appear in the current 85kg and 95kg categories, depending on their competitive weight adjustments.

The back-to-back format keeps Peacock Theater buzzing for hours, with warmup rooms backstage cycling through athletes while the competition platform sees barbells reloaded every few minutes as lifters rotate through attempts.
WLF05.potentialContendersIntro: The 85kg and 95kg fields will likely draw from athletes who competed at 89kg and 96kg in Paris 2024, including gold medalists from those former classes.

WLF06.blurb:
Women's 77kg takes the evening spotlight on Day 3, a category where athletes blend strength with athleticism in one of the sport's most dynamic divisions. This weight class has produced memorable Games performances across multiple editions.

Ecuador's Angie Palacios won Paris 2024 gold at 81kg with a 268kg total, becoming her nation's first-ever weightlifting gold medalist. The current 77kg class may see her and other former 81kg lifters adjust their competition weight, reshaping the competitive landscape.

Under the theater's focused lighting, every technical cue becomes visible—the lifter's grip adjustment, the deep breath before pulling, the knee drive in the jerk—creating an immersive experience for fans in the tightly configured seating bowl.
WLF06.potentialContendersIntro: Angie Palacios made history for Ecuador with Paris 2024 gold at 81kg. She could compete at 77kg in 2028, potentially facing other strong lifters from Asia and the Americas.

WLF07.blurb:
Friday evening brings Women's 86kg, a newer category in the streamlined 2028 program that tests pure strength alongside technical mastery. These athletes move serious weight, with successful clean & jerks often exceeding what lighter men's divisions post.

South Korea's Park Hye-jeong won Paris 2024 gold at 81kg with 299kg, while China's Li Wenwen dominated the super-heavyweight class. How athletes redistribute across the new 86kg and +86kg structure will determine this session's medal picture.

The late-week slot finds Peacock Theater's audience seasoned to weightlifting's rhythms—they know when to hold silence during the pull and when to explode into cheers as the referee's lights flash white for a good lift.
WLF07.potentialContendersIntro: The 86kg category draws from former 81kg and super-heavyweight lifters. Park Hye-jeong (Paris 2024 gold at 81kg) and redistributed heavyweights could clash.

WLF08.blurb:
Men's 110kg headlines Friday night as the penultimate session, where athletes approaching the super-heavyweight threshold display remarkable combinations of size, speed, and flexibility. This division consistently produces totals above 400kg, with successful lifts creating seismic platform impacts.

Georgia's Lasha Bezhania won Paris 2024 gold at 102kg with 390kg, though the current 110kg class may see different competitive dynamics as lifters from multiple former categories converge. Middle Eastern and Eastern European nations typically field strong 110kg contenders.

The venue's sound system captures every detail—the barbell's metallic ring during the initial pull, the lifter's shout at lockout, the thunderous drop when judges' decisions are rendered—amplified in the enclosed theater space.
WLF08.potentialContendersIntro: Lasha Bezhania (Georgia) won Paris 2024 gold at 102kg. The 110kg class could draw lifters from both the former 102kg and 109kg categories, creating a deep field.

WLF09.blurb:
Men's +110kg closes Saturday afternoon with weightlifting's traditional showcase—the super-heavyweights, where the sport's strongest athletes compete. These lifters regularly eclipse 200kg in single lifts, bending barbells loaded with every available plate.

Georgia's Lasha Talakhadze has dominated the super-heavyweight class for years, winning gold at Rio 2016, Tokyo 2020, and Paris 2024 with a 470kg total. If he competes in 2028 at age 35, Talakhadze would seek an unprecedented fourth consecutive gold.

Peacock Theater's stage becomes an arena as the heaviest athletes chalk up, the entire platform flexing under loads that test both human limits and equipment tolerances, with the downtown LA crowd on its feet for every successful lift.
WLF09.potentialContendersIntro: Lasha Talakhadze (Georgia) has won three consecutive golds and could make history with a fourth if he competes. His 470kg Paris 2024 total set the standard.

potentialContenders.name: Lasha Talakhadze
potentialContenders.note: Paris 2024 gold medalist, 470kg total; three-time champion

WLF10.blurb:
Women's +86kg concludes the entire weightlifting program Saturday night, bringing five days of competition to a climactic finish. The super-heavyweight women move extraordinary weight with technique honed over years, their final lifts determining the last medals awarded in the sport.

China's Li Wenwen won Paris 2024 gold in the super-heavyweight division with a commanding 309kg total. Her status for 2028 remains to be confirmed, but China consistently develops world-class talent in the women's heavyweight categories.

The closing session carries ceremonial weight beyond the medal stakes, with Peacock Theater's lights and crowd energy building toward the final clean & jerk attempts that will write the last chapter of 2028 weightlifting history.
WLF10.potentialContendersIntro: Li Wenwen (China) dominated Paris 2024 with 309kg. If she competes, she'll be the favorite, though challengers from Korea, the UK, and Samoa could emerge.

potentialContenders.name: Li Wenwen
potentialContenders.note: Paris 2024 gold medalist in super-heavyweight, 309kg total

Men's water polo continues its preliminary finale Monday afternoon with two more matches. Final group results determine the entire knockout bracket—who plays whom, and which side of the draw offers the clearest medal path.

Tournament heavyweights face must-win scenarios or seeding battles, while underdogs hunt upset victories that could reshape the bracket. The United States will look to leverage home support if they compete, chasing another podium finish.

The temporary venue's intimate layout puts you poolside in an open-air setting near the beach. Afternoon sessions offer a relaxed atmosphere as the preliminary phase draws to a close and the medal rounds loom.
WPO14.potentialContendersIntro: Serbia, Croatia, and the United States are confirmed Paris 2024 medalists and projected to return, alongside other top European programs likely to qualify.

WPO15.blurb:
The men's preliminary rounds close Monday night with two final matches under the lights. Group play ends, quarterfinal pairings lock, and the path to medals becomes clear.

This session could feature decisive moments—last-gasp qualification bids, seeding battles between rivals, or dominant performances from title favorites. Serbia and Croatia's Paris 2024 final set a high bar; both are projected to return in pursuit of another medal.

Evening poolside seating at Long Beach Aquatics Center delivers an intimate coastal setting. Open sky and ocean breezes accompany the action as teams complete preliminary play and shift focus to knockout rounds.
WPO15.potentialContendersIntro: Paris 2024 podium finishers Serbia, Croatia, and the United States are expected to compete, wrapping up group play before the single-elimination phase begins.

WPO16.blurb:
The women's water polo tournament opens with two preliminary matches at Long Beach's temporary waterfront stadium. With 12 teams competing for the first time—matching the men's field—the expanded format promises more nations and more drama throughout the tournament.

Preliminary rounds set the stage for knockout play, determining quarterfinal seeding and potential medal paths. Every match matters in the round-robin format, where goal differential can swing bracket positioning.

The outdoor venue sits just blocks from the Pacific, with cooling ocean breezes and open-air sightlines. Compact seating puts spectators close to the action, where you'll hear every splash and whistle.
WPO16.potentialContendersIntro: Spain arrives as Paris 2024 gold medalists, while Australia and Netherlands—silver and bronze in Paris—are projected to return as top contenders.

potentialContenders.name: Spain
potentialContenders.note: Gold medalists at Paris 2024, defending champions

potentialContenders.name: United States
potentialContenders.note: Perennial medal contenders, playing on home soil

WPO17.blurb:
Two more women's preliminary matches fill the afternoon session as the 12-team tournament continues its opening day. Teams begin establishing tournament rhythm and testing lineups before the knockout rounds.

Group standings start to take shape after the morning session, with coaches adjusting tactics and rotations. Home-pool advantage could matter for any U.S. squad in the draw, playing in familiar Southern California waters.

The mid-afternoon session offers a more relaxed viewing experience before the evening crowd arrives. Shaded sections provide relief from the summer sun, while the pool's all-deep configuration allows for the sport's signature vertical play.
WPO17.potentialContendersIntro: Spain, Australia, and Netherlands enter as the Paris 2024 podium finishers, with Hungary and Italy also likely to field strong squads.

potentialContenders.name: Hungary
potentialContenders.note: Traditional European power, multiple world championship medals

WPO18.blurb:
The opening day concludes with prime-time women's water polo under the lights. Evening sessions at Long Beach draw bigger crowds, with sunset casting long shadows across the temporary stadium as teams battle for early tournament positioning.

The expanded 12-team format brings more geographic diversity and more competitive depth than past Games. First-round results begin revealing which nations might challenge the established powers.

The outdoor pool takes on a different character at night, with artificial lighting creating dramatic contrasts on the water. The compact venue amplifies crowd noise, turning close matches into pressure-cooker environments.
WPO18.potentialContendersIntro: The Paris 2024 medalists—Spain, Australia, and Netherlands—are expected to appear across the preliminary round, along with emerging programs from the expanded 12-team field.

WPO19.blurb:
The men's knockout stage begins with the first two quarterfinal matches. Eight teams remain from the 12-team preliminary round—this is where medal dreams either advance or end.

Serbia enters as defending Paris 2024 champions, while Croatia and the United States earned silver and bronze. The quarterfinal draw determines which traditional powers might clash early and which get clearer paths to the medal rounds.

Long Beach's intimate setup intensifies the knockout atmosphere. With only four teams advancing to the semifinals, every possession matters as squads deploy their deepest tactical playbooks.
WPO19.potentialContendersIntro: Serbia won gold at Paris 2024, with Croatia taking silver and the U.S. earning bronze. All three are projected to reach the quarterfinals if they qualify.

potentialContenders.name: United States

potentialContenders.name: Spain
